text.NRSV: There was a certain man of Ramathaim, a Zuphite from the hill country of Ephraim, whose name was Elkanah son of Jeroham son of Elihu son of Tohu son of Zuph, an Ephraimite.
text.Rotherham: And there was a certain man, of Ramathaim-zuphi, of the hill country of Ephraim,—whose name, was Elkanah, son of Jeroham, son of Elihu, son of Tohu, son of Zuph, an Ephraimite;
text.KJV: Now there was a certain man of Ramathaim–zophim, of mount Ephraim, and his name was Elkanah, the son of Jeroham, the son of Elihu, the son of Tohu, the son of Zuph, an Ephrathite:
text.NABRE: There was a certain man from Ramathaim, a Zuphite from the hill country of Ephraim. His name was Elkanah, the son of Jeroham, son of Elihu, son of Tohu, son of Zuph, an Ephraimite.
text.DRB: There was a man of Ramathaimsophim, of mount Ephraim, & his name Elcana, the son of Jeroham, the son of Eliu, the son of Thohu, the son of Suph, an Ephraite:

text.NRSV: He had two wives; the name of the one was Hannah, and the name of the other Peninnah. Peninnah had children, but Hannah had no children.
text.Rotherham: and, he, had two wives, the name of the one, Hannah, and, the name of the other, Peninnah,—and Peninnah had children, but, Hannah, had no children.
text.KJV: And he had two wives; the name of the one was Hannah, and the name of the other Peninnah: and Peninnah had children, but Hannah had no children.
text.NABRE: He had two wives, one named Hannah, the other Peninnah; Peninnah had children, but Hannah had no children.
text.DRB: and he had two wives, the name of one was Anna, and the name of the second Phenenna. Phenenna had children: but Anna had not children.

text.NRSV: Now this man used to go up year by year from his town to worship and to sacrifice to the Lord of hosts at Shiloh, where the two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, were priests of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So then that man went up, out of his city, from time to time, to worship and to sacrifice unto Yahweh of hosts, in Shiloh,—and, there, were the two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, priests unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And this man went up out of his city yearly to worship and to sacrifice unto the Lord of hosts in Shiloh. And the two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, the priests of the Lord, were there.
text.NABRE: Each year this man went up from his city to worship and offer sacrifice to the Lord of hosts at Shiloh, where the two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, were ministering as priests of the Lord.
text.DRB: And that man went up from his city upon ordinary days, to adore and sacrifice unto the Lord of hosts in Silo. And there were the two sons of Heli, Ophni and Phinees, Priests of our Lord.

text.NRSV: On the day when Elkanah sacrificed, he would give portions to his wife Peninnah and to all her sons and daughters;
text.Rotherham: And, when the day came for Elkanah to sacrifice, he used to give, to Peninnah his wife, and to all her sons and her daughters, portions;
text.KJV: And when the time was that Elkanah offered, he gave to Peninnah his wife, and to all her sons and her daughters, portions:
text.NABRE: When the day came for Elkanah to offer sacrifice, he used to give portions to his wife Peninnah and to all her sons and daughters,
text.DRB: The day came therefore, & Elcana immolated, and gave to Phenenna his wife, & to all her sons and daughters parts:

text.NRSV: but to Hannah he gave a double portion, because he loved her, though the Lord had closed her womb.
text.Rotherham: and, unto Hannah, used he to give one portion,—howbeit, Hannah, he loved, although, Yahweh, had restrained her from having children.
text.KJV: But unto Hannah he gave a worthy portion; for he loved Hannah: but the Lord had shut up her womb.
text.NABRE: but he would give a double portion to Hannah because he loved her, though the Lord had closed her womb.
text.DRB: but to Anna he gave one part with heavy cheer, because he loved Anna. And our Lord had shut her matrice.

text.NRSV: Her rival used to provoke her severely, to irritate her, because the Lord had closed her womb.
text.Rotherham: And her rival used even to cause her great vexation, for the sake of provoking her,—because Yahweh had restrained her from having children.
text.KJV: And her adversary also provoked her sore, for to make her fret, because the Lord had shut up her womb.
text.NABRE: Her rival, to upset her, would torment her constantly, since the Lord had closed her womb.
text.DRB: Her adversary also afflicted her, and vexed her sore, in so much that she upbraided her, that our Lord had shut her matrice:

text.NRSV: So it went on year by year; as often as she went up to the house of the Lord, she used to provoke her. Therefore Hannah wept and would not eat.
text.Rotherham: And, thus, used she to do, year by year, whenever she went up to the house of Yahweh, thus, used she to vex her,—and she wept, and would not eat.
text.KJV: And as he did so year by year, when she went up to the house of the Lord, so she provoked her; therefore she wept, and did not eat.
text.NABRE: Year after year, when she went up to the house of the Lord, Peninnah would provoke her, and Hannah would weep and refuse to eat.
text.DRB: and so did she every year, when the time returned, that they went up to the temple of our Lord: and so she provoked her: moreover she wept, and took not meat.

text.NRSV: Her husband Elkanah said to her, “Hannah, why do you weep? Why do you not eat? Why is your heart sad? Am I not more to you than ten sons?”
text.Rotherham: So Elkanah her husband said to her—Hannah! wherefore shouldst thou weep? and wherefore wilt thou not eat? and wherefore should thy heart be sad? Am, I, not better to thee, than ten sons?
text.KJV: Then said Elkanah her husband to her, Hannah, why weepest thou? and why eatest thou not? and why is thy heart grieved? am not I better to thee than ten sons?
text.NABRE: Elkanah, her husband, would say to her: “Hannah, why are you weeping? Why are you not eating? Why are you so miserable? Am I not better for you than ten sons?”
text.DRB: Elcana therefore her husband said to her: Anna, why weepest thou? And why dost thou not eat? And wherefore dost thou afflict thy heart? Am not I better to thee, than ten children?

text.NRSV: After they had eaten and drunk at Shiloh, Hannah rose and presented herself before the Lord. Now Eli the priest was sitting on the seat beside the doorpost of the temple of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And Hannah rose up, after she had eaten in Shiloh, and after she had drunk,—and, Eli the priest, was sitting upon his chair, by the door-post of the temple of Yahweh;
text.KJV: So Hannah rose up after they had eaten in Shiloh, and after they had drunk. Now Eli the priest sat upon a seat by a post of the temple of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Hannah’s Prayer. Hannah rose after one such meal at Shiloh, and presented herself before the Lord; at the time Eli the priest was sitting on a chair near the doorpost of the Lord ’s temple.
text.DRB: And Anna arose after she had eaten and drunk in Silo. And Heli the Priest sitting upon a Stool before the posts of the house of our Lord,

text.NRSV: She was deeply distressed and prayed to the Lord, and wept bitterly.
text.Rotherham: and, she being in bitterness of soul, prayed unto Yahweh, and, wept sore.
text.KJV: And she was in bitterness of soul, and prayed unto the Lord, and wept sore.
text.NABRE: In her bitterness she prayed to the Lord, weeping freely,
text.DRB: whereas Anna had a heavy heart, she prayed to our Lord, weeping aboundantly,

text.NRSV: She made this vow: “O Lord of hosts, if only you will look on the misery of your servant, and remember me, and not forget your servant, but will give to your servant a male child, then I will set him before you as a nazirite until the day of his death. He shall drink neither wine nor intoxicants, and no razor shall touch his head.”
text.Rotherham: And she vowed a vow, and said—Yahweh of hosts! If thou wilt, look, upon the humiliation of thy handmaid, and remember me, and not forget thy handmaid, but wilt give unto thy handmaid a man-child, then will I give him unto Yahweh, all the days of his life, and no, razor, shall come upon his head.
text.KJV: And she vowed a vow, and said, O Lord of hosts, if thou wilt indeed look on the affliction of thine handmaid, and remember me, and not forget thine handmaid, but wilt give unto thine handmaid a man child, then I will give him unto the Lord all the days of his life, and there shall no razor come upon his head.
text.NABRE: and made this vow: “O Lord of hosts, if you look with pity on the hardship of your servant, if you remember me and do not forget me, if you give your handmaid a male child, I will give him to the Lord all the days of his life. No razor shall ever touch his head.”
text.DRB: and she vowed a vow, saying: O Lord of hosts, if regarding thou wilt behold the affliction of thy servant, and wilt be mindful of me, and not forget thy handmaid, and wilt give unto thy servant a man child: I will give him to our Lord all the days of his life, & the razor shall not come upon his head.

text.NRSV: As she continued praying before the Lord, Eli observed her mouth.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, as she continued praying before Yahweh, that Eli was watching her mouth.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, as she continued praying before the Lord, that Eli marked her mouth.
text.NABRE: As she continued praying before the Lord, Eli watched her mouth,
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when she multiplied prayers before our Lord, that Heli observed her mouth.

text.NRSV: Hannah was praying silently; only her lips moved, but her voice was not heard; therefore Eli thought she was drunk.
text.Rotherham: But as for, Hannah, she, was speaking in her heart, only her lips, were moving, but, her voice, could not be heard,—so Eli thought she had been drunken.
text.KJV: Now Hannah, she spake in her heart; only her lips moved, but her voice was not heard: therefore Eli thought she had been drunken.
text.NABRE: for Hannah was praying silently; though her lips were moving, her voice could not be heard. Eli, thinking she was drunk,
text.DRB: Moreover Anna spake in her heart, and only her lips moved, and voice there was not heard at all. Heli therefore thought her to be drunk,

text.NRSV: So Eli said to her, “How long will you make a drunken spectacle of yourself? Put away your wine.”
text.Rotherham: And Eli said unto her, How long, wilt thou be, drunken? Put away thy wine from thee.
text.KJV: And Eli said unto her, How long wilt thou be drunken? put away thy wine from thee.
text.NABRE: said to her, “How long will you make a drunken spectacle of yourself? Sober up from your wine!”
text.DRB: and said to her: How long wilt thou be drunk? Digest a little the wine, wherewith thou art wet.

text.NRSV: But Hannah answered, “No, my lord, I am a woman deeply troubled; I have drunk neither wine nor strong drink, but I have been pouring out my soul before the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And Hannah responded and said—Nay! my lord; A woman depressed in spirit, am I: neither wine nor strong drink, have I drunk,—but I poured out my soul, before Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Hannah answered and said, No, my lord, I am a woman of a sorrowful spirit: I have drunk neither wine nor strong drink, but have poured out my soul before the Lord.
text.NABRE: “No, my lord!” Hannah answered. “I am an unhappy woman. I have had neither wine nor liquor; I was only pouring out my heart to the Lord.
text.DRB: Anna answering, Not so, quoth she, my Lord: for I am an exceeding unhappy woman, and wine and whatsoever may inebriate, I have not drunk, but I have poured out my soul in the sight of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Do not regard your servant as a worthless woman, for I have been speaking out of my great anxiety and vexation all this time.”
text.Rotherham: Do not count thy handmaid for an abandoned woman,—for, out of the abundance of my grief and my vexation, have I spoken, hitherto.
text.KJV: Count not thine handmaid for a daughter of Belial: for out of the abundance of my complaint and grief have I spoken hitherto.
text.NABRE: Do not think your servant a worthless woman; my prayer has been prompted by my deep sorrow and misery.”
text.DRB: Account not thy handmaid as one of the daughters of Belial: for of the multitude of my sorrow and heaviness have I spoken until this present.

text.NRSV: Then Eli answered, “Go in peace; the God of Israel grant the petition you have made to him.”
text.Rotherham: Then responded Eli, and said—Go and prosper! And the, God of Israel, grant thy petition which thou hast asked of him!
text.KJV: Then Eli answered and said, Go in peace: and the God of Israel grant thee thy petition that thou hast asked of him.
text.NABRE: Eli said, “Go in peace, and may the God of Israel grant you what you have requested.”
text.DRB: Then Heli said to her: Go in peace: and the God of Israel give thee thy petition, which thou hast asked him.

text.NRSV: And she said, “Let your servant find favor in your sight.” Then the woman went to her quarters, ate and drank with her husband, and her countenance was sad no longer.
text.Rotherham: And she said: Let thy serving-woman find favour, in thine eyes. So the woman went her way, and did eat, and, her countenance, was sad no longer.
text.KJV: And she said, Let thine handmaid find grace in thy sight. So the woman went her way, and did eat, and her countenance was no more sad.
text.NABRE: She replied, “Let your servant find favor in your eyes,” and left. She went to her quarters, ate and drank with her husband, and no longer appeared downhearted.
text.DRB: But she said: Would God thy handmaid may find grace in thine eyes. And the woman went on her way, and did eat, and her countenance was no more changed otherwise.

text.NRSV: They rose early in the morning and worshiped before the Lord; then they went back to their house at Ramah. Elkanah knew his wife Hannah, and the Lord remembered her.
text.Rotherham: And they rose up early in the morning, and worshipped before Yahweh, and returned, and entered their own house, in Ramah,—and Elkanah knew Hannah his wife, and Yahweh remembered her.
text.KJV: And they rose up in the morning early, and worshipped before the Lord, and returned, and came to their house to Ramah: and Elkanah knew Hannah his wife; and the Lord remembered her.
text.NABRE: Early the next morning they worshiped before the Lord, and then returned to their home in Ramah. When they returned Elkanah had intercourse with his wife Hannah, and the Lord remembered her.
text.DRB: And they rose in the morning, and adored before our Lord: and they returned, & came into their house to Ramatha. And Elcana knew Anna his wife: and our Lord remembered her.

text.NRSV: In due time Hannah conceived and bore a son. She named him Samuel, for she said, “I have asked him of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And so it was, when the days had come round during which Hannah was with child, that she bare a son,—and called his name Samuel [="Heard of God"], Because, of Yahweh, I asked him.
text.KJV: Wherefore it came to pass, when the time was come about after Hannah had conceived, that she bare a son, and called his name Samuel, saying, Because I have asked him of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Hannah Bears a Son. She conceived and, at the end of her pregnancy, bore a son whom she named Samuel. “Because I asked the Lord for him.”
text.DRB: And it came to pass after a certain compass of days, Anna conceived & bare a son, and called his name Samuel: because she asked him of our Lord.

text.NRSV: The man Elkanah and all his household went up to offer to the Lord the yearly sacrifice, and to pay his vow.
text.Rotherham: And the man Elkanah, and all his house, went up, to offer unto Yahweh the yearly sacrifice, and [each] his own vow.
text.KJV: And the man Elkanah, and all his house, went up to offer unto the Lord the yearly sacrifice, and his vow.
text.NABRE: The next time her husband Elkanah was going up with the rest of his household to offer the customary sacrifice to the Lord and to fulfill his vows,
text.DRB: And Elcana her husband went up, and all her house, to immolate unto our Lord the solemn host, and his vow,

text.NRSV: But Hannah did not go up, for she said to her husband, “As soon as the child is weaned, I will bring him, that he may appear in the presence of the Lord, and remain there forever; I will offer him as a nazirite for all time.”
text.Rotherham: But, Hannah, went not up,—for she said to her husband—[Not] till the boy is weaned, then will I take him, and he shall appear before Yahweh, and abide there evermore.
text.KJV: But Hannah went not up; for she said unto her husband, I will not go up until the child be weaned, and then I will bring him, that he may appear before the Lord, and there abide for ever.
text.NABRE: Hannah did not go, explaining to her husband, “Once the child is weaned, I will take him to appear before the Lord and leave him there forever.”
text.DRB: and Anna went not up: for she said to her husband: I will not go till the infant be weaned, and till I may bring him, that he may appear before the sight of our Lord, and may remain there continually.

text.NRSV: Her husband Elkanah said to her, “Do what seems best to you, wait until you have weaned him; only—may the Lord establish his word.” So the woman remained and nursed her son, until she weaned him.
text.Rotherham: And Elkanah her husband said to her—Do what is good in thine own eyes, tarry until thou have weaned him, only may Yahweh establish his word! So the woman tarried, and nursed her son, until she weaned him.
text.KJV: And Elkanah her husband said unto her, Do what seemeth thee good; tarry until thou have weaned him; only the Lord establish his word. So the woman abode, and gave her son suck until she weaned him.
text.NABRE: Her husband Elkanah answered her: “Do what you think best; wait until you have weaned him. Only may the Lord fulfill his word!” And so she remained at home and nursed her son until she had weaned him.
text.DRB: And Elcana her husband said to her. Do that which seemeth good to thee, and tarry till thou wean him: and I pray that our Lord fulfill his word. The woman therefore tarried, and gave her son suck, till she removed him from the milk.

text.NRSV: When she had weaned him, she took him up with her, along with a three-year-old bull, an ephah of flour, and a skin of wine. She brought him to the house of the Lord at Shiloh; and the child was young.
text.Rotherham: Then took she him up with her, when she had weaned him, with a bullock of three years old, and one ephah of meal, and a skin of wine, and took him to the house of Yahweh, at Shiloh,—the boy yet being young.
text.KJV: And when she had weaned him, she took him up with her, with three bullocks, and one ephah of flour, and a bottle of wine, and brought him unto the house of the Lord in Shiloh: and the child was young.
text.NABRE: Hannah Presents Samuel to the Lord. Once he was weaned, she brought him up with her, along with a three-year-old bull, an ephah of flour, and a skin of wine, and presented him at the house of the Lord in Shiloh.
text.DRB: And she brought him with her, after she had weaned him, with three calves, & three bushels of meal, and a flagon of wine, and she brought him to the house of our Lord in Silo. But the child was yet a little infant:

text.NRSV: Then they slaughtered the bull, and they brought the child to Eli.
text.Rotherham: So they slew the bullock,—and took the boy in, unto Eli.
text.KJV: And they slew a bullock, and brought the child to Eli.
text.NABRE: After they had slaughtered the bull, they brought the child to Eli.
text.DRB: and they immolated a calf, and offered the child to Heli.

text.NRSV: And she said, “Oh, my lord! As you live, my lord, I am the woman who was standing here in your presence, praying to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And she said—Pardon, my lord! By the life of thy soul, my lord,—I, am the woman who was standing near thee here, praying unto Yahweh:
text.KJV: And she said, Oh my lord, as thy soul liveth, my lord, I am the woman that stood by thee here, praying unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: Then Hannah spoke up: “Excuse me, my lord! As you live, my lord, I am the woman who stood here near you, praying to the Lord.
text.DRB: And Anna said: I beseech thee my Lord, thy soul liveth my Lord: I am that woman, which stood before thee here praying our Lord.

text.NRSV: For this child I prayed; and the Lord has granted me the petition that I made to him.
text.Rotherham: For this boy, I prayed,—And Yahweh hath given me my petition which I asked of him.
text.KJV: For this child I prayed; and the Lord hath given me my petition which I asked of him:
text.NABRE: I prayed for this child, and the Lord granted my request.
text.DRB: For this child did I pray, and our Lord hath given me my petition, which I asked him.

text.NRSV: Therefore I have lent him to the Lord; as long as he lives, he is given to the Lord.” She left him there for the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Hence, I also, have loaned him unto Yahweh, all the days that he liveth. He, is lent unto Yahweh. And he bowed down there, unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Therefore also I have lent him to the Lord; as long as he liveth he shall be lent to the Lord. And he worshipped the Lord there.
text.NABRE: Now I, in turn, give him to the Lord; as long as he lives, he shall be dedicated to the Lord.” Then they worshiped there before the Lord.
text.DRB: Therefore I also have given him to our Lord all the days, which he shall live, that he may be applied to our Lord. And they adored our Lord there. And Anna prayed, and said:

text.NRSV: Hannah prayed and said, “My heart exults in the Lord; my strength is exalted in my God. My mouth derides my enemies, because I rejoice in my victory.
text.Rotherham: Then prayed Hannah, and said, My heart hath leaped for joy in Yahweh, My horn is exalted in Yahweh, My mouth is opened wide, o’er my foes, Because I rejoice in thy salvation.
text.KJV: And Hannah prayed, and said, My heart rejoiceth in the Lord, mine horn is exalted in the Lord: my mouth is enlarged over mine enemies; because I rejoice in thy salvation.
text.NABRE: Chapter 2 And Hannah prayed: “My heart exults in the Lord, my horn is exalted by my God. I have swallowed up my enemies; I rejoice in your victory.
text.DRB: My heart hath rejoiced in our Lord, and my horn is exalted in my God: my mouth is dilated upon mine enemies: because I have joyed in thy salvation.

text.NRSV: “There is no Holy One like the Lord, no one besides you; there is no Rock like our God.
text.Rotherham: There is none holy like Yahweh, Nay! there, is none, except Thee, Nor, is, there a rock, like our God.
text.KJV: There is none holy as the Lord: for there is none beside thee: neither is there any rock like our God.
text.NABRE: There is no Holy One like the Lord; there is no Rock like our God.
text.DRB: There is none holy as our Lord is: for neither is there another beside thee, and there is none so strong as our God.

text.NRSV: Talk no more so very proudly, let not arrogance come from your mouth; for the Lord is a God of knowledge, and by him actions are weighed.
text.Rotherham: Do not multiply words, so loftily—loftily, Nor let arrogance proceed from your mouth,—For, a GOD of knowledge, is Yahweh, And, for himself, are great doings made firm.
text.KJV: Talk no more so exceeding proudly; let not arrogancy come out of your mouth: for the Lord is a God of knowledge, and by him actions are weighed.
text.NABRE: Speak boastfully no longer, Do not let arrogance issue from your mouths. For an all-knowing God is the Lord, a God who weighs actions.
text.DRB: Do not multiply to speak high things boasting, let old matters depart from your mouth: because our Lord is a God of all knowledge, and to him cogitations are prepared.

text.NRSV: The bows of the mighty are broken, but the feeble gird on strength.
text.Rotherham: The bow of the mighty, is dismayed,—While, the fainting, are girded with strength;
text.KJV: The bows of the mighty men are broken, and they that stumbled are girded with strength.
text.NABRE: “The bows of the mighty are broken, while the tottering gird on strength.
text.DRB: The bow of the strong men is overcome, and the weak are girded with strength.

text.NRSV: Those who were full have hired themselves out for bread, but those who were hungry are fat with spoil. The barren has borne seven, but she who has many children is forlorn.
text.Rotherham: The sated, have, for bread, taken hire, But, the famished, have left off their toil,—So that, the barren, hath given birth unto seven, While, she that hath many sons, languisheth:
text.KJV: They that were full have hired out themselves for bread; and they that were hungry ceased: so that the barren hath born seven; and she that hath many children is waxed feeble.
text.NABRE: The well-fed hire themselves out for bread, while the hungry no longer have to toil. The barren wife bears seven sons, while the mother of many languishes.
text.DRB: They that before were filled have hired out themselves for bread: and the hungry are filled, until the barren woman bare very many: and she that had many children was weakened.

text.NRSV: The Lord kills and brings to life; he brings down to Sheol and raises up.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, doth kill, and make alive,—Taketh down to hades, and bringeth up:
text.KJV: The Lord killeth, and maketh alive: he bringeth down to the grave, and bringeth up.
text.NABRE: “The Lord puts to death and gives life, casts down to Sheol and brings up again.
text.DRB: Our Lord mortifieth and quickeneth, bringeth down to hell and fetcheth back again.

text.NRSV: The Lord makes poor and makes rich; he brings low, he also exalts.
text.Rotherham: Yahweh, maketh poor, and enricheth,—Layeth low, yea exalteth;
text.KJV: The Lord maketh poor, and maketh rich: he bringeth low, and lifteth up.
text.NABRE: The Lord makes poor and makes rich, humbles, and also exalts.
text.DRB: Our Lord maketh poor and enricheth, humbleth and lifteth up.

text.NRSV: He raises up the poor from the dust; he lifts the needy from the ash heap, to make them sit with princes and inherit a seat of honor. For the pillars of the earth are the Lord’s, and on them he has set the world.
text.Rotherham: Raiseth, from the dust, the poor, From the dunghill, uplifteth the needy, To give them a dwelling with nobles, And, a throne of glory, to make them inherit. For, to Yahweh, belong the pillars of the earth, And he setteth thereon the habitable world.
text.KJV: He raiseth up the poor out of the dust, and lifteth up the beggar from the dunghill, to set them among princes, and to make them inherit the throne of glory: for the pillars of the earth are the Lord’s, and he hath set the world upon them.
text.NABRE: He raises the needy from the dust; from the ash heap lifts up the poor, To seat them with nobles and make a glorious throne their heritage. “For the pillars of the earth are the Lord ’s, and he has set the world upon them.
text.DRB: He raiseth the needy man from the dust, and from the dung he lifteth up the poor: that he may sit with Princes, and hold the throne of glory. For the poles of the earth are our Lord's, and upon them he hath set the world.

text.NRSV: He will guard the feet of his faithful ones, but the wicked shall be cut off in darkness; for not by might does one prevail.
text.Rotherham: The feet of his loving ones, he doth guard, But, the lawless, in darkness shall be silent,—For, by strength, shall no man prevail.
text.KJV: He will keep the feet of his saints, and the wicked shall be silent in darkness; for by strength shall no man prevail.
text.NABRE: He guards the footsteps of his faithful ones, but the wicked shall perish in the darkness; for not by strength does one prevail.
text.DRB: The feet of his Saints he will keep, and the impious shall be silent in darkness: because in his own force man shall not be strengthened.

text.NRSV: The Lord! His adversaries shall be shattered; the Most High will thunder in heaven. The Lord will judge the ends of the earth; he will give strength to his king, and exalt the power of his anointed.”
text.Rotherham: As for Yahweh,—they shall be shattered who contend with him, Over him, in the heavens will he thunder, Yahweh, will judge the ends of the earth,—That he may give strength to his King, And exalt the horn of his Anointed One.
text.KJV: The adversaries of the Lord shall be broken to pieces; out of heaven shall he thunder upon them: the Lord shall judge the ends of the earth; and he shall give strength unto his king, and exalt the horn of his anointed.
text.NABRE: The Lord ’s foes shall be shattered; the Most High in heaven thunders; the Lord judges the ends of the earth. May he give strength to his king, and exalt the horn of his anointed!”
text.DRB: Our Lord shall his adversaries fear: & upon them shall he thunder in the heavens: our Lord shall judge the ends of the earth, & shall give empire to his King, and shall exalt the horn of his Christ.

text.NRSV: Then Elkanah went home to Ramah, while the boy remained to minister to the Lord, in the presence of the priest Eli.
text.Rotherham: Then went Elkanah to Ramah, unto his own house,—but, the boy, remained ministering unto Yahweh, before Eli the priest.
text.KJV: And Elkanah went to Ramah to his house. And the child did minister unto the Lord before Eli the priest.
text.NABRE: When Elkanah returned home to Ramah, the child remained in the service of the Lord under the priest Eli.
text.DRB: And Elcana went into Ramatha, unto his house: but the child ministered in the sight of our Lord before the face of Heli the Priest.

text.NRSV: Now the sons of Eli were scoundrels; they had no regard for the Lord
text.Rotherham: Now, the sons of Eli, were abandoned men,—they knew not Yahweh.
text.KJV: Now the sons of Eli were sons of Belial; they knew not the Lord.
text.NABRE: Wickedness of Eli’s Sons. Now the sons of Eli were wicked; they had respect neither for the Lord
text.DRB: Moreover the sons of Heli, were the sons of Belial, not knowing our Lord,

text.NRSV: or for the duties of the priests to the people. When anyone offered sacrifice, the priest’s servant would come, while the meat was boiling, with a three-pronged fork in his hand,
text.Rotherham: And, the custom of the priests with the people, was—when any man offered a sacrifice, then would come the priests young man, as the flesh was boiling, with a three-pronged fork in his hand;
text.KJV: And the priests’ custom with the people was, that, when any man offered sacrifice, the priest’s servant came, while the flesh was in seething, with a fleshhook of three teeth in his hand;
text.NABRE: nor for the priests’ duties toward the people. When someone offered a sacrifice, the priest’s servant would come with a three-pronged fork, while the meat was still boiling,
text.DRB: nor the office of Priests to the people: but whosoever had immolated a victim, the servant of the Priest came, whilst the flesh was in boiling, and had a flesh hook with three teeth in his hand,

text.NRSV: and he would thrust it into the pan, or kettle, or caldron, or pot; all that the fork brought up the priest would take for himself. This is what they did at Shiloh to all the Israelites who came there.
text.Rotherham: and would strike it into the boiler, or into the trough, or into the kettle, or into the pot, all that the fork would bring up, the priest took for himself. Thus and thus, used they to do unto all Israel, who came thither, in Shiloh.
text.KJV: And he struck it into the pan, or kettle, or caldron, or pot; all that the fleshhook brought up the priest took for himself. So they did in Shiloh unto all the Israelites that came thither.
text.NABRE: and would thrust it into the basin, kettle, caldron, or pot. Whatever the fork brought up, the priest would take for himself. They treated all the Israelites who came to the sanctuary at Shiloh in this way.
text.DRB: and thrust it into the kettle, or into the cauldron, or into the pot, or into the pan: and all that the flesh hook brought up, the Priest took to himself. So did they to all Israel that came into Silo.

text.NRSV: Moreover, before the fat was burned, the priest’s servant would come and say to the one who was sacrificing, “Give meat for the priest to roast; for he will not accept boiled meat from you, but only raw.”
text.Rotherham: Also, before any could make perfume with the fat, the priests young man would come in and say to the person who was sacrificing, Come! give flesh for the priest’s roastings,—for he will not take of thee boiled flesh—only raw.
text.KJV: Also before they burnt the fat, the priest’s servant came, and said to the man that sacrificed, Give flesh to roast for the priest; for he will not have sodden flesh of thee, but raw.
text.NABRE: In fact, even before the fat was burned, the priest’s servant would come and say to the one offering the sacrifice, “Give me some meat to roast for the priest. He will not accept boiled meat from you, only raw meat.”
text.DRB: Yea before they burnt the fat, the servant of the Priest came, and said to him that immolated: Give me flesh, that I may boil it for the Priest: for I will not take flesh of thee sod, but raw.

text.NRSV: And if the man said to him, “Let them burn the fat first, and then take whatever you wish,” he would say, “No, you must give it now; if not, I will take it by force.”
text.Rotherham: And, if the man said to him, Let them at least, make incense, at once with the fat, then take thou as much as thy soul craveth, Then said he to him, But, at once, shalt thou give it; or else, I will take it by force.
text.KJV: And if any man said unto him, Let them not fail to burn the fat presently, and then take as much as thy soul desireth; then he would answer him, Nay; but thou shalt give it me now: and if not, I will take it by force.
text.NABRE: And if this one protested, “Let the fat be burned first, then take whatever you wish,” he would reply, “No, give it to me now, or else I will take it by force.”
text.DRB: And he that immolated said to him: Let the fat first be burnt today according to the manner, and take unto thee how much soever thy soul desireth. Who answering said to him: Not so: for thou shalt give it now, or else I will take it away by force.

text.NRSV: Thus the sin of the young men was very great in the sight of the Lord; for they treated the offerings of the Lord with contempt.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, that, the sin of the young men, was exceeding great, before Yahweh,—for men scorned the offerings of Yahweh.
text.KJV: Wherefore the sin of the young men was very great before the Lord: for men abhorred the offering of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Thus the young men sinned grievously in the presence of the Lord, treating the offerings to the Lord with disdain.
text.DRB: Therefore the sin of the young men was exceeding great before our Lord: because men detracted from the sacrifice of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Samuel was ministering before the Lord, a boy wearing a linen ephod.
text.Rotherham: But, as for Samuel, he was ministering before Yahweh,—a boy girded with an ephod of linen.
text.KJV: But Samuel ministered before the Lord, being a child, girded with a linen ephod.
text.NABRE: The Lord Rewards Hannah. Meanwhile the boy Samuel, wearing a linen ephod, was serving in the presence of the Lord.
text.DRB: But Samuel ministered before the face of our Lord: a child, girded with an ephod of linen.

text.NRSV: His mother used to make for him a little robe and take it to him each year, when she went up with her husband to offer the yearly sacrifice.
text.Rotherham: Also, a little robe, used his mother to make for him, and bring it up to him, from year to year,—when she came up with her husband, to offer the sacrifice of the year.
text.KJV: Moreover his mother made him a little coat, and brought it to him from year to year, when she came up with her husband to offer the yearly sacrifice.
text.NABRE: His mother used to make a little garment for him, which she would bring him each time she went up with her husband to offer the customary sacrifice.
text.DRB: And his mother made him a little tunic, which she brought upon the ordinary days, going up with her husband to immolate the solemn host.

text.NRSV: Then Eli would bless Elkanah and his wife, and say, “May the Lord repay you with children by this woman for the gift that she made to the Lord”; and then they would return to their home.
text.Rotherham: And Eli used to bless Elkanah and his wife, and to say—Yahweh give thee seed of this woman, instead of the loan that hath been lent unto Yahweh. So they went their way to his own place.
text.KJV: And Eli blessed Elkanah and his wife, and said, The Lord give thee seed of this woman for the loan which is lent to the Lord. And they went unto their own home.
text.NABRE: And Eli would bless Elkanah and his wife, as they were leaving for home. He would say, “May the Lord repay you with children from this woman for the gift she has made to the Lord!”
text.DRB: And Heli blessed Elcana and his wife: & he said to him: Our Lord render thee seed of this woman, for the usury that thou hast given our Lord. And they went into their place.

text.NRSV: And the Lord took note of Hannah; she conceived and bore three sons and two daughters. And the boy Samuel grew up in the presence of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh visited Hannah, and she conceived, and bare three sons, and two daughters. Thus did the boy Samuel grow up with Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the Lord visited Hannah, so that she conceived, and bare three sons and two daughters. And the child Samuel grew before the Lord.
text.NABRE: The Lord favored Hannah so that she conceived and gave birth to three more sons and two daughters, while young Samuel grew up in the service of the Lord.
text.DRB: Our Lord therefore visited Anna, and she conceived, and bare three sons, and two daughters: and the child Samuel was magnified before our Lord.

text.NRSV: Now Eli was very old. He heard all that his sons were doing to all Israel, and how they lay with the women who served at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
text.Rotherham: Now, Eli, was very old,—but he used to hear all that his sons did unto all Israel, and how they even lay with the women who did service, at the opening of the tent of meeting.
text.KJV: Now Eli was very old, and heard all that his sons did unto all Israel; and how they lay with the women that assembled at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
text.NABRE: Eli’s Futile Rebuke. When Eli was very old, he kept hearing how his sons were treating all Israel, and that they were behaving promiscuously with the women serving at the entry of the meeting tent.
text.DRB: And Heli was very old, and heard all things which his sons did to all Israel: & how they slept with the women that waited at the door of the tabernacle:

text.NRSV: He said to them, “Why do you do such things? For I hear of your evil dealings from all these people.
text.Rotherham: So he said to them, Wherefore should ye do such things as these? for I keep hearing of your wicked doings, from all these people.
text.KJV: And he said unto them, Why do ye such things? for I hear of your evil dealings by all this people.
text.NABRE: So he said to them: “Why are you doing such things? I hear from everyone that your behavior is depraved.
text.DRB: and he said to them: Why do you these kind of things, which I hear, very naughty things, of all the people?

text.NRSV: No, my sons; it is not a good report that I hear the people of the Lord spreading abroad.
text.Rotherham: Nay, my sons! for it is no good report that I do hear: leading into transgression the people of Yahweh.
text.KJV: Nay, my sons; for it is no good report that I hear: ye make the Lord’s people to transgress.
text.NABRE: Stop this, my sons! The report that I hear the Lord ’s people spreading is not good.
text.DRB: Do not so my sons: for it is not a good report, which I do hear, that you make the people of our Lord to transgress.

text.NRSV: If one person sins against another, someone can intercede for the sinner with the Lord; but if someone sins against the Lord, who can make intercession?” But they would not listen to the voice of their father; for it was the will of the Lord to kill them.
text.Rotherham: If one man sin against another, God will interpose, but, if, against Yahweh, a man sin, who will intercede, for him? But they hearkened not unto the voice of their father, for Yahweh was pleased to put them to death.
text.KJV: If one man sin against another, the judge shall judge him: but if a man sin against the Lord, who shall intreat for him? Notwithstanding they hearkened not unto the voice of their father, because the Lord would slay them.
text.NABRE: If someone sins against another, anyone can intercede for the sinner with the Lord; but if anyone sins against the Lord, who can intercede for the sinner?” But they disregarded their father’s warning, since the Lord wanted them dead.
text.DRB: If man shall sin against man, God may be pacified toward him: but if a man shall sin against our Lord who shall pray for him? And they heard not the voice of their father, because our Lord would kill them.

text.NRSV: Now the boy Samuel continued to grow both in stature and in favor with the Lord and with people.
text.Rotherham: But, the boy Samuel, went on growing in stature, and in favour,—both with Yahweh, and also with men.
text.KJV: And the child Samuel grew on, and was in favour both with the Lord, and also with men.
text.NABRE: Meanwhile, young Samuel was growing in stature and in worth in the estimation of the Lord and the people. The Fate of Eli’s House.
text.DRB: But the child Samuel prospered, and grew, and pleased both our Lord and men.

text.NRSV: A man of God came to Eli and said to him, “Thus the Lord has said, ‘I revealed myself to the family of your ancestor in Egypt when they were slaves to the house of Pharaoh.
text.Rotherham: And there came a man of God, unto Eli,—and said unto him—Thus, saith Yahweh, I, did indeed reveal myself, unto the house of thy father, when they were in Egypt, as servants unto the house of Pharaoh;
text.KJV: And there came a man of God unto Eli, and said unto him, Thus saith the Lord, Did I plainly appear unto the house of thy father, when they were in Egypt in Pharaoh’s house?
text.NABRE: A man of God came to Eli and said to him: “Thus says the Lord: I went so far as to reveal myself to your father’s house when they were in Egypt as slaves to the house of Pharaoh.
text.DRB: And there came a man of God to Heli, and said to him: Thus saith our Lord: Was not I openly revealed to thy father's house, when they were in Ægypt in the house of Pharao?

text.NRSV: I chose him out of all the tribes of Israel to be my priest, to go up to my altar, to offer incense, to wear an ephod before me; and I gave to the family of your ancestor all my offerings by fire from the people of Israel.
text.Rotherham: choosing him out of all the tribes of Israel unto myself, to minister as priest, to offer upon mine altar, to perfume with incense to bear an ephod before me,—Therefore gave I unto the house of thy father all the altar-flames of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And did I choose him out of all the tribes of Israel to be my priest, to offer upon mine altar, to burn incense, to wear an ephod before me? and did I give unto the house of thy father all the offerings made by fire of the children of Israel?
text.NABRE: I chose them out of all the tribes of Israel to be my priests, to go up to my altar, to burn incense, and to wear the ephod in my presence; and I assigned all the fire offerings of the Israelites to your father’s house.
text.DRB: and I chose him of all the tribes of Israel for my Priest, that he might ascend to my altar, and burn to me incense, and might carry the ephod before me: and I gave to thy father's house all things of the sacrifices of the children of Israel.

text.NRSV: Why then look with greedy eye at my sacrifices and my offerings that I commanded, and honor your sons more than me by fattening yourselves on the choicest parts of every offering of my people Israel?’
text.Rotherham: Wherefore have ye been kicking at my sacrifices, and my presents, which I commanded, to serve for a home,—and shouldest have honoured thy sons more than me: fattening yourselves, with the first of every present of Israel, before me?
text.KJV: Wherefore kick ye at my sacrifice and at mine offering, which I have commanded in my habitation; and honourest thy sons above me, to make yourselves fat with the chiefest of all the offerings of Israel my people?
text.NABRE: Why do you stare greedily at my sacrifices and at the offerings that I have prescribed? Why do you honor your sons more than you honor me, fattening yourselves with the choicest part of every offering of my people Israel?
text.DRB: Why have you with your heel rejected my victim, and my gifts which I commanded to be offered in the temple: and hast rather honoured thy sons than me, that you would eat the first fruits of every sacrifice of Israel my people?

text.NRSV: Therefore the Lord the God of Israel declares: ‘I promised that your family and the family of your ancestor should go in and out before me forever’; but now the Lord declares: ‘Far be it from me; for those who honor me I will honor, and those who despise me shall be treated with contempt.
text.Rotherham: Hence, the oracle of Yahweh God of Israel, I, said, that, thy house, and the house of thy father, should go to and fro in my presence, unto times age-abiding: But, now, (is the oracle of Yahweh)—Be it far from me! For, them who honour me, will I honour, but, they who despise me, shall be lightly esteemed.
text.KJV: Wherefore the Lord God of Israel saith, I said indeed that thy house, and the house of thy father, should walk before me for ever: but now the Lord saith, Be it far from me; for them that honour me I will honour, and they that despise me shall be lightly esteemed.
text.NABRE: This, therefore, is the oracle of the Lord, the God of Israel: I said in the past that your family and your father’s house should minister in my presence forever. But now—oracle of the Lord: Far be it from me! I will honor those who honor me, but those who despise me shall be cursed.
text.DRB: Therefore said our Lord the God of Israel: Speaking I spake that thy house, and the house of thy father should minister in my sight for ever. But now saith our Lord: Be this far from me: but whosoever shall glorify me I will glorify him: and they that contemn me, shall be base.

text.NRSV: See, a time is coming when I will cut off your strength and the strength of your ancestor’s family, so that no one in your family will live to old age.
text.Rotherham: Lo! days are coming, when I will hew off thine arm, and the arm of the house of thy father,—that there shall be no elder in thy house;
text.KJV: Behold, the days come, that I will cut off thine arm, and the arm of thy father’s house, that there shall not be an old man in thine house.
text.NABRE: Yes, the days are coming when I will break your strength and the strength of your father’s house, so that no one in your family lives to old age.
text.DRB: Behold the days come: and I will cut off thy arm, and the arm of thy father's house, that there may not be an old man in thy house.

text.NRSV: Then in distress you will look with greedy eye on all the prosperity that shall be bestowed upon Israel; and no one in your family shall ever live to old age.
text.Rotherham: But thou shalt descry distress at home, in all that shall gladden Israel,—and there shall not be an elder in thine own house, all the days.
text.KJV: And thou shalt see an enemy in my habitation, in all the wealth which God shall give Israel: and there shall not be an old man in thine house for ever.
text.NABRE: You shall witness, like a disappointed rival, all the benefits enjoyed by Israel, but no member of your household shall ever grow old.
text.DRB: And thou shalt see whom thou enviest in the temple, in all prosperities of Israel, and there shall not be an old man in thy house for ever.

text.NRSV: The only one of you whom I shall not cut off from my altar shall be spared to weep out his eyes and grieve his heart; all the members of your household shall die by the sword.
text.Rotherham: But, any man of thine whom I may not cut off from mine altar, it shall be—to consume his eyes, and grieve his soul; Howbeit, all the multitude of thy house, shall die, by the sword of men.
text.KJV: And the man of thine, whom I shall not cut off from mine altar, shall be to consume thine eyes, and to grieve thine heart: and all the increase of thine house shall die in the flower of their age.
text.NABRE: I will leave you one man at my altar to wear out his eyes and waste his strength, but the rest of your family shall die by the sword.
text.DRB: Notwithstanding I will not altogether take away a man of thee from mine altar: but that thine eyes may fail, and thy soul melt: and a great part of thy house shall die when it is come to man's age.

text.NRSV: The sign that shall come upon your two sons, on Hophni and Phinehas, shall be: they shall both die on the same day.
text.Rotherham: And, this, for thee is the sign, which shall come upon thy two sons, upon Hophni and Phinehas,—In one day, shall they, both of them, die;
text.KJV: And this shall be a sign unto thee, that shall come upon thy two sons, on Hophni and Phinehas; in one day they shall die both of them.
text.NABRE: This is a sign for you—what happens to your two sons, Hophni and Phinehas. Both of them will die on the same day.
text.DRB: And this shall be a sign to thee, which shall come upon thy two sons, Ophni, and Phinees: In one day they shall both die.

text.NRSV: I will raise up for myself a faithful priest, who shall do according to what is in my heart and in my mind. I will build him a sure house, and he shall go in and out before my anointed one forever.
text.Rotherham: And I will raise me up a faithful priest, According to that which is in my heart and in my soul, will he do; Therefore will I build for him an assured house, and he shall go to and fro in presence of mine Anointed, all the days.
text.KJV: And I will raise me up a faithful priest, that shall do according to that which is in mine heart and in my mind: and I will build him a sure house; and he shall walk before mine anointed for ever.
text.NABRE: I will choose a faithful priest who shall do what I have in heart and mind. I will establish a lasting house for him and he shall serve in the presence of my anointed forever.
text.DRB: And I will raise up unto me a faithful Priest, which shall do according to my heart, and my soul: and I will build him a faithful house and the same shall walk before my Christ all days.

text.NRSV: Everyone who is left in your family shall come to implore him for a piece of silver or a loaf of bread, and shall say, Please put me in one of the priest’s places, that I may eat a morsel of bread.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be, that, any that is left in thy house, shall come bowing down to him for a small coin of silver, and for a cake of bread, and shall say: Appoint me, I pray thee, to one of the priestly offices, that I may eat a morsel of bread.
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass, that every one that is left in thine house shall come and crouch to him for a piece of silver and a morsel of bread, and shall say, Put me, I pray thee, into one of the priests’ offices, that I may eat a piece of bread.
text.NABRE: Then whoever is left of your family will grovel before him for a piece of silver or a loaf of bread, saying: Please assign me a priestly function, that I may have a crust of bread to eat.”
text.DRB: And it shall come to pass, that whosoever shall remain in thy house, shall come that he may be prayed for, and shall offer a piece of silver, and a manchet of bread, and shall say: Leave me I beseech thee to one priestly part, that I may eat a morsel of bread.

text.NRSV: Now the boy Samuel was ministering to the Lord under Eli. The word of the Lord was rare in those days; visions were not widespread.
text.Rotherham: Now, the boy Samuel, was ministering unto Yahweh, before Eli,—and, the word of Yahweh, had become rare in those days, there was no well-known vision.
text.KJV: And the child Samuel ministered unto the Lord before Eli. And the word of the Lord was precious in those days; there was no open vision.
text.NABRE: During the time young Samuel was minister to the Lord under Eli, the word of the Lord was scarce and vision infrequent.
text.DRB: And the child Samuel ministered to our Lord before Heli, and the word of our Lord was precious in those days, there was no vision manifest.

text.NRSV: At that time Eli, whose eyesight had begun to grow dim so that he could not see, was lying down in his room;
text.Rotherham: But it came to pass, at that time, when Eli was lying down in his place, his eyes, having begun to be dim, he could not see,
text.KJV: And it came to pass at that time, when Eli was laid down in his place, and his eyes began to wax dim, that he could not see;
text.NABRE: One day Eli was asleep in his usual place. His eyes had lately grown so weak that he could not see.
text.DRB: It came to pass therefore on a certain day Heli lay in his place, & his eyes were become dim, neither could he see:

text.NRSV: the lamp of God had not yet gone out, and Samuel was lying down in the temple of the Lord, where the ark of God was.
text.Rotherham: though, the lamp of God, was not yet to be put out, and, Samuel, was lying down in the temple of Yahweh, where was the ark of God,
text.KJV: And ere the lamp of God went out in the temple of the Lord, where the ark of God was, and Samuel was laid down to sleep;
text.NABRE: The lamp of God was not yet extinguished, and Samuel was sleeping in the temple of the Lord where the ark of God was.
text.DRB: before the lamp of God was extinguished, Samuel slept in the temple of our Lord, where the ark of God was.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord called, “Samuel! Samuel!” and he said, “Here I am!”
text.Rotherham: that Yahweh called unto Samuel, and he said—Behold me!
text.KJV: That the Lord called Samuel: and he answered, Here am I.
text.NABRE: The Lord called to Samuel, who answered, “Here I am.”
text.DRB: And our Lord called Samuel. Who answering, said: Lo here I am.

text.NRSV: and ran to Eli, and said, “Here I am, for you called me.” But he said, “I did not call; lie down again.” So he went and lay down.
text.Rotherham: So he ran unto Eli and said—Behold me! for thou calledst me. And he said—I called not, return—Its down. So he went, and lie down.
text.KJV: And he ran unto Eli, and said, Here am I; for thou calledst me. And he said, I called not; lie down again. And he went and lay down.
text.NABRE: He ran to Eli and said, “Here I am. You called me.” “I did not call you,” Eli answered. “Go back to sleep.” So he went back to sleep.
text.DRB: And he ran to Heli and said: Lo here I am: for thou didst call me. Who said: I did not call thee: return and sleep. And he went & slept.

text.NRSV: The Lord called again, “Samuel!” Samuel got up and went to Eli, and said, “Here I am, for you called me.” But he said, “I did not call, my son; lie down again.”
text.Rotherham: And again Yahweh called once there—Samuel! Then rose Samuel, and went unto Eli, and said—Behold me! for thou calledst me. And he said—I called not, my son, return—lie down.
text.KJV: And the Lord called yet again, Samuel. And Samuel arose and went to Eli, and said, Here am I; for thou didst call me. And he answered, I called not, my son; lie down again.
text.NABRE: Again the Lord called Samuel, who rose and went to Eli. “Here I am,” he said. “You called me.” But he answered, “I did not call you, my son. Go back to sleep.”
text.DRB: And our Lord added again to call Samuel. And Samuel rising up went to Heli, & said: Lo here I am: because thou didst call me. Who answered: I did not call thee my son, return & sleep.

text.NRSV: Now Samuel did not yet know the Lord, and the word of the Lord had not yet been revealed to him.
text.Rotherham: Now, Samuel, as yet knew not Yahweh,—nor, as yet, had been revealed unto him, the word of Yahweh.
text.KJV: Now Samuel did not yet know the Lord, neither was the word of the Lord yet revealed unto him.
text.NABRE: Samuel did not yet recognize the Lord, since the word of the Lord had not yet been revealed to him.
text.DRB: Moreover Samuel did not yet know our Lord, neither had the word of our Lord been revealed to him.

text.NRSV: The Lord called Samuel again, a third time. And he got up and went to Eli, and said, “Here I am, for you called me.” Then Eli perceived that the Lord was calling the boy.
text.Rotherham: And again Yahweh called—Samuel! a third time. Then he arose, and went unto Eli, and said—Behold me! for thou calledst me. Then did Eli perceive, that, Yahweh, was calling the boy.
text.KJV: And the Lord called Samuel again the third time. And he arose and went to Eli, and said, Here am I; for thou didst call me. And Eli perceived that the Lord had called the child.
text.NABRE: The Lord called Samuel again, for the third time. Getting up and going to Eli, he said, “Here I am. You called me.” Then Eli understood that the Lord was calling the youth.
text.DRB: And our Lord added, & called Samuel yet the third time. Who rising up went to Heli,

text.NRSV: Therefore Eli said to Samuel, “Go, lie down; and if he calls you, you shall say, ‘Speak, Lord, for your servant is listening.’” So Samuel went and lay down in his place.
text.Rotherham: So Eli said to Samuel—Go, lie down, and it shall be, if he call unto thee, that thou shalt say—Speak, Yahweh, for thy servant is listening. And Samuel went, and lay down in his place.
text.KJV: Therefore Eli said unto Samuel, Go, lie down: and it shall be, if he call thee, that thou shalt say, Speak, Lord; for thy servant heareth. So Samuel went and lay down in his place.
text.NABRE: So he said to Samuel, “Go to sleep, and if you are called, reply, ‘Speak, Lord, for your servant is listening.’” When Samuel went to sleep in his place,
text.DRB: & said: Lo here I am: because thou didst call me. Heli therefore understood that our Lord called the child & said to Samuel: Go, & sleep: and if he shall call thee hereafter, thou shalt say: Speak Lord, for thy servant heareth. Samuel therefore went and slept in his place.

text.NRSV: Now the Lord came and stood there, calling as before, “Samuel! Samuel!” And Samuel said, “Speak, for your servant is listening.”
text.Rotherham: Then came Yahweh, and stood, and called as time after time—Samuel! Samuel! And Samuel said, Speak, for thy servant is listening.
text.KJV: And the Lord came, and stood, and called as at other times, Samuel, Samuel. Then Samuel answered, Speak; for thy servant heareth.
text.NABRE: the Lord came and stood there, calling out as before: Samuel, Samuel! Samuel answered, “Speak, for your servant is listening.”
text.DRB: And our Lord came, and stood: and he called, as he had called twice, Samuel, Samuel. And Samuel said: Speak Lord, for thy servant heareth.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said to Samuel, “See, I am about to do something in Israel that will make both ears of anyone who hears of it tingle.
text.Rotherham: And Yahweh said unto Samuel, Lo! I am doing a thing, in Israel,—at which both ears of everyone that heareth it shall tingle.
text.KJV: And the Lord said to Samuel, Behold, I will do a thing in Israel, at which both the ears of every one that heareth it shall tingle.
text.NABRE: The Lord said to Samuel: I am about to do something in Israel that will make the ears of everyone who hears it ring.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to Samuel: Behold I do a thing in Israel: which whosoever shall hear, both his ears shall tingle.

text.NRSV: On that day I will fulfill against Eli all that I have spoken concerning his house, from beginning to end.
text.Rotherham: In that day, will I confirm against Eli, all that I have spoken against his house,—beginning and finishing.
text.KJV: In that day I will perform against Eli all things which I have spoken concerning his house: when I begin, I will also make an end.
text.NABRE: On that day I will carry out against Eli everything I have said about his house, beginning to end.
text.DRB: In that day will I raise up against Heli all things which I have spoken touching his house: I will begin, and accomplish it.

text.NRSV: For I have told him that I am about to punish his house forever, for the iniquity that he knew, because his sons were blaspheming God, and he did not restrain them.
text.Rotherham: Therefore do I tell him, that I am about to judge his house unto times age-abiding, for the iniquity which he knoweth, in that his sons are cursing God, and he hath not rebuked them.
text.KJV: For I have told him that I will judge his house for ever for the iniquity which he knoweth; because his sons made themselves vile, and he restrained them not.
text.NABRE: I announce to him that I am condemning his house once and for all, because of this crime: though he knew his sons were blaspheming God, he did not reprove them.
text.DRB: For I have foretold him that I would judge his house for ever, because of iniquity, for that he knew that his sons did wickedly, and hath not corrected them.

text.NRSV: Therefore I swear to the house of Eli that the iniquity of Eli’s house shall not be expiated by sacrifice or offering forever.”
text.Rotherham: And therefore have I sworn, respecting the house of Eli,—that the iniquity of the house of Eli shall receive no propitiatory-covering, by sacrifice or by present, unto times age-abiding.
text.KJV: And therefore I have sworn unto the house of Eli, that the iniquity of Eli’s house shall not be purged with sacrifice nor offering for ever.
text.NABRE: Therefore, I swear to Eli’s house: No sacrifice or offering will ever expiate its crime.
text.DRB: Therefore have I sworn to the house of Heli that the iniquity of his house cannot be expiated with victims and gifts for ever.

text.NRSV: Samuel lay there until morning; then he opened the doors of the house of the Lord. Samuel was afraid to tell the vision to Eli.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel lay until the morning, and rose early in the morning, and opened the doors of the house of Yahweh,—but, Samuel, feared to tell the appearing, unto Eli.
text.KJV: And Samuel lay until the morning, and opened the doors of the house of the Lord. And Samuel feared to shew Eli the vision.
text.NABRE: Samuel then slept until morning, when he got up early and opened the doors of the temple of the Lord. He was afraid to tell Eli the vision,
text.DRB: And Samuel slept until morning, and opened the doors of the house of our Lord. And Samuel feared to tell the vision unto Heli.

text.NRSV: But Eli called Samuel and said, “Samuel, my son.” He said, “Here I am.”
text.Rotherham: So Eli called Samuel, and said—Samuel! my son. And he said—Behold me!
text.KJV: Then Eli called Samuel, and said, Samuel, my son. And he answered, Here am I.
text.NABRE: but Eli called to him, “Samuel, my son!” He replied, “Here I am.”
text.DRB: Heli therefore called Samuel, and said: Samuel my son. Who answering, said: Here I am.

text.NRSV: Eli said, “What was it that he told you? Do not hide it from me. May God do so to you and more also, if you hide anything from me of all that he told you.”
text.Rotherham: And he said—What is the word, which he spake unto thee? I pray thee, do not hide it from me. So, let God do to thee, and, so, let him add, if thou hide from me a word, out of anything which he hath spoken unto thee.
text.KJV: And he said, What is the thing that the Lord hath said unto thee? I pray thee hide it not from me: God do so to thee, and more also, if thou hide any thing from me of all the things that he said unto thee.
text.NABRE: Then Eli asked, “What did he say to you? Hide nothing from me! May God do thus to you, and more, if you hide from me a single thing he told you.”
text.DRB: And he asked him: What is the word, that our Lord hath spoken to thee? I beseech thee conceal it not from me. These things do God to thee, and these do he add, if thou shalt hide from me a word of all the words, which were said to thee.

text.NRSV: So Samuel told him everything and hid nothing from him. Then he said, “It is the Lord; let him do what seems good to him.”
text.Rotherham: So Samuel told him all the words, and hid nothing from him. And he said, Yahweh, he is; what is good in his own eyes, let him do.
text.KJV: And Samuel told him every whit, and hid nothing from him. And he said, It is the Lord: let him do what seemeth him good.
text.NABRE: So Samuel told him everything, and held nothing back. Eli answered, “It is the Lord. What is pleasing in the Lord ’s sight, the Lord will do.”
text.DRB: Samuel therefore told him all the words, and did not hide them from him. And he answered: It is our Lord: let him do that which is good in his eyes.

text.NRSV: As Samuel grew up, the Lord was with him and let none of his words fall to the ground.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel grew,—and, Yahweh, proved to he with him, and let none of all his words fall to the ground.
text.KJV: And Samuel grew, and the Lord was with him, and did let none of his words fall to the ground.
text.NABRE: Samuel Acknowledged as Prophet. Samuel grew up, and the Lord was with him, not permitting any word of his to go unfulfilled.
text.DRB: And Samuel grew, and our Lord was with him, and there fell not of his words upon the ground.

text.NRSV: And all Israel from Dan to Beer-sheba knew that Samuel had been established as a prophet of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So all Israel, from Dan even to Beersheba, came to know,—that Samuel had become confirmed as a prophet unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And all Israel from Dan even to Beer–sheba knew that Samuel was established to be a prophet of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Thus all Israel from Dan to Beer-sheba came to know that Samuel was a trustworthy prophet of the Lord.
text.DRB: And all Israel knew from Dan to Bersabee, that faithful Samuel was the Prophet of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord appeared again at Shiloh, for the Lord revealed himself to Samuel at Shiloh by the word of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Thus did Yahweh again appear in Shiloh,—for Yahweh had revealed himself unto Samuel in Shiloh, in the word of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the Lord appeared again in Shiloh: for the Lord revealed himself to Samuel in Shiloh by the word of the Lord.
text.NABRE: The Lord continued to appear at Shiloh, manifesting himself to Samuel at Shiloh through his word. Samuel’s word spread throughout Israel.
text.DRB: And our Lord added to appear in Silo, because our Lord had been revealed to Samuel in Silo, according to the word of our Lord. And the word of Samuel came to pass to all Israel.

text.NRSV: And the word of Samuel came to all Israel. In those days the Philistines mustered for war against Israel, and Israel went out to battle against them; they encamped at Ebenezer, and the Philistines encamped at Aphek.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, when the word of Samuel had reached all Israel, that Israel went forth to meet the Philistines in battle, and they encamped near Eben-ezer, while, the Philistines, encamped in Aphek.
text.KJV: And the word of Samuel came to all Israel. Now Israel went out against the Philistines to battle, and pitched beside Eben–ezer: and the Philistines pitched in Aphek.
text.NABRE: At that time, the Philistines gathered for an attack on Israel. Israel went out to engage them in battle and camped at Ebenezer, while the Philistines camped at Aphek.
text.DRB: And it came to pass in those days, the Philisthiims assembled together to fight: and Israel went forth to meet the Philisthiims into battle, & camped beside the Stone of help. Moreover the Philisthiims came into Aphec,

text.NRSV: The Philistines drew up in line against Israel, and when the battle was joined, Israel was defeated by the Philistines, who killed about four thousand men on the field of battle.
text.Rotherham: And the Philistines set themselves in array to meet Israel, and, when the battle spread, then was Israel smitten before the Philistines,—and there were slain of the army in the field about four thousand men.
text.KJV: And the Philistines put themselves in array against Israel: and when they joined battle, Israel was smitten before the Philistines: and they slew of the army in the field about four thousand men.
text.NABRE: The Philistines then drew up in battle formation against Israel. After a fierce struggle Israel was defeated by the Philistines, who killed about four thousand men on the battlefield.
text.DRB: & put their army in array against Israel. And after they had joined battle, Israel turned their backs to the Philisthiims: and there were slain in the fight here & there through the fields, as it were four thousand men.

text.NRSV: When the troops came to the camp, the elders of Israel said, “Why has the Lord put us to rout today before the Philistines? Let us bring the ark of the covenant of the Lord here from Shiloh, so that he may come among us and save us from the power of our enemies.”
text.Rotherham: And, when the people came into the camp, the elders of Israel said, Wherefore hath Yahweh smitten us to-day, before the Philistines? Let us fetch unto us, out of Shiloh, the ark of the covenant of Yahweh, that, when it cometh into our midst, it may save us out of the hand of our enemies.
text.KJV: And when the people were come into the camp, the elders of Israel said, Wherefore hath the Lord smitten us to day before the Philistines? Let us fetch the ark of the covenant of the Lord out of Shiloh unto us, that, when it cometh among us, it may save us out of the hand of our enemies.
text.NABRE: When the troops retired to the camp, the elders of Israel said, “Why has the Lord permitted us to be defeated today by the Philistines? Let us fetch the ark of the Lord from Shiloh that it may go into battle among us and save us from the grasp of our enemies.”
text.DRB: And the people returned to the camp: & the ancients of Israel said: Why hath our Lord struck us today before the Philisthiims? Let us fetch unto us the ark of the covenant of our Lord from Silo, and let it come into the midst of us, that it may save us from the hand of our enemies.

text.NRSV: So the people sent to Shiloh, and brought from there the ark of the covenant of the Lord of hosts, who is enthroned on the cherubim. The two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, were there with the ark of the covenant of God.
text.Rotherham: So the people sent to Shiloh, and bare from thence the ark of the covenant of Yahweh of hosts, who inhabiteth the cherubim,—and, there, were the two sons of Eli, with the ark of the covenant of God, namely, Hophni and Phinehas.
text.KJV: So the people sent to Shiloh, that they might bring from thence the ark of the covenant of the Lord of hosts, which dwelleth between the cherubims: and the two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, were there with the ark of the covenant of God.
text.NABRE: Loss of the Ark. So the people sent to Shiloh and brought from there the ark of the Lord of hosts, who is enthroned upon the cherubim. The two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, accompanied the ark of God.
text.DRB: The people therefore sent into Silo, and they took from thence the ark of the covenant of the Lord of hosts sitting upon the Cherubims: and the two sons of Heli were with the ark of the covenant of God, Ophni and Phinees.

text.NRSV: When the ark of the covenant of the Lord came into the camp, all Israel gave a mighty shout, so that the earth resounded.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, when the ark of the covenant of Yahweh came into the camp, that all Israel brake out into a loud shout of joy,—so that the earth rang again.
text.KJV: And when the ark of the covenant of the Lord came into the camp, all Israel shouted with a great shout, so that the earth rang again.
text.NABRE: When the ark of the Lord arrived in the camp, all Israel shouted so loudly that the earth shook.
text.DRB: And when the ark of the covenant of our Lord was come into the camp, all Israel made a shout with a great cry, and the earth sounded.

text.NRSV: When the Philistines heard the noise of the shout, they said, “What does this great shout in the camp of the Hebrews mean?” When they learned that the ark of the Lord had come to the camp,
text.Rotherham: And, when the Philistines heard the noise of the shout, they said—What meaneth the noise of this loud shout of joy in the camp of the Hebrews? And they learned that, the ark of Yahweh, had come into the camp.
text.KJV: And when the Philistines heard the noise of the shout, they said, What meaneth the noise of this great shout in the camp of the Hebrews? And they understood that the ark of the Lord was come into the camp.
text.NABRE: The Philistines, hearing the uproar, asked, “What does this loud shouting in the camp of the Hebrews mean?” On learning that the ark of the Lord had come into the camp,
text.DRB: And the Philisthiims heard the voice of the cry, & said: What is this voice of a great cry in the camp of the Hebrews? And they knew that the ark of our lord was come into the camp.

text.NRSV: the Philistines were afraid; for they said, “Gods have come into the camp.” They also said, “Woe to us! For nothing like this has happened before.
text.Rotherham: And the Philistines were afraid, for they said—God hath come into the camp. And they said—Woe to us! for there hath not happened the like of this, heretofore.
text.KJV: And the Philistines were afraid, for they said, God is come into the camp. And they said, Woe unto us! for there hath not been such a thing heretofore.
text.NABRE: the Philistines were frightened, crying out, “Gods have come to their camp. Woe to us! This has never happened before.
text.DRB: And the Philisthiims were afraid, saying: God is come into the camp. And they mourned, saying:

text.NRSV: Woe to us! Who can deliver us from the power of these mighty gods? These are the gods who struck the Egyptians with every sort of plague in the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: Woe to us! Who shall rescue us out of the hand of these majestic gods? These, are the gods, who smote the Egyptians with all manner of smiting in the desert!
text.KJV: Woe unto us! who shall deliver us out of the hand of these mighty Gods? these are the Gods that smote the Egyptians with all the plagues in the wilderness.
text.NABRE: Woe to us! Who can deliver us from the power of these mighty gods? These are the gods who struck the Egyptians with various plagues in the desert.
text.DRB: Woe to us: for there was no so great rejoicing yesterday and the day before: woe to us. Who shall keep us from the hand of these high gods? These be the gods that struck Ægypt with all plague, in the desert.

text.NRSV: Take courage, and be men, O Philistines, in order not to become slaves to the Hebrews as they have been to you; be men and fight.”
text.Rotherham: Take courage, and quit yourselves like men, ye Philistines, lest ye come into bondage to the Hebrews, as they have been in bondage unto you,—therefore must ye quit yourselves like men, and fight.
text.KJV: Be strong, and quit yourselves like men, O ye Philistines, that ye be not servants unto the Hebrews, as they have been to you: quit yourselves like men, and fight.
text.NABRE: Take courage and act like soldiers, Philistines; otherwise you will become slaves to the Hebrews, as they were your slaves. Fight like soldiers!”
text.DRB: Take courage, & be men, ye Philisthiims: lest you be servants to the Hebrews, as they also have served you: take courage & fight.

text.NRSV: So the Philistines fought; Israel was defeated, and they fled, everyone to his home. There was a very great slaughter, for there fell of Israel thirty thousand foot soldiers.
text.Rotherham: So the Philistines fought, and Israel was smitten, and they fled every man to his home; and the slaughter became exceeding great,—and there fell of Israel—thirty thousand footmen.
text.KJV: And the Philistines fought, and Israel was smitten, and they fled every man into his tent: and there was a very great slaughter; for there fell of Israel thirty thousand footmen.
text.NABRE: The Philistines fought and Israel was defeated; everyone fled to their own tents. It was a disastrous defeat; Israel lost thirty thousand foot soldiers.
text.DRB: The Philisthiims therefore fought, and Israel was slain, and every man fled into his tabernacle: and there was made an exceeding great plague: and there fell of Israel thirty thousand footmen.

text.NRSV: The ark of God was captured; and the two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, died.
text.Rotherham: And, the ark of God, was taken,—and, the two sons of Eli, were slain, Hophni and Phinehas.
text.KJV: And the ark of God was taken; and the two sons of Eli, Hophni and Phinehas, were slain.
text.NABRE: The ark of God was captured, and Eli’s two sons, Hophni and Phinehas, were dead.
text.DRB: And the ark of God was taken: the two sons also of Heli died, Ophni and Phinees.

text.NRSV: A man of Benjamin ran from the battle line, and came to Shiloh the same day, with his clothes torn and with earth upon his head.
text.Rotherham: And there ran a man of Benjamin out of the army, and entered Shiloh on the same day,—with his clothes rent, and with earth upon his head.
text.KJV: And there ran a man of Benjamin out of the army, and came to Shiloh the same day with his clothes rent, and with earth upon his head.
text.NABRE: Death of Eli. A Benjaminite fled from the battlefield and reached Shiloh that same day, with his clothes torn and his head covered with dirt.
text.DRB: And a man of Benjamin running out of the battle array, came into Silo that day, his garment rent, and sprinkled on his head with dust.

text.NRSV: When he arrived, Eli was sitting upon his seat by the road watching, for his heart trembled for the ark of God. When the man came into the city and told the news, all the city cried out.
text.Rotherham: And, when he entered, lo! Eli was sitting upon his chair, by the wayside, watching, for his heart was trembling for the ark of God,—and, the man, entered to tell the news in the city, and all the city made outcry.
text.KJV: And when he came, lo, Eli sat upon a seat by the wayside watching: for his heart trembled for the ark of God. And when the man came into the city, and told it, all the city cried out.
text.NABRE: When he arrived, Eli was sitting in his chair beside the gate, watching the road, for he was troubled at heart about the ark of God. The man, however, went into the city to announce his news; then the whole city cried out.
text.DRB: And when he was come, Heli sat upon a stool over against the way looking. For his heart was fearful for the ark of God. And that man after he was entered in, told it to the city: and all the city howled.

text.NRSV: When Eli heard the sound of the outcry, he said, “What is this uproar?” Then the man came quickly and told Eli.
text.Rotherham: And, when Eli heard the noise of the outcry, he said—What meaneth the noise of this tumult? And, the man, hasted, and came, and told Eli.
text.KJV: And when Eli heard the noise of the crying, he said, What meaneth the noise of this tumult? And the man came in hastily, and told Eli.
text.NABRE: When Eli heard the uproar, he wondered why there was such commotion. Just then the man rushed up to inform him.
text.DRB: And Heli heard the sound of the cry, and said: What is this sound of this same tumult? But he hastened, and came, and told Heli.

text.NRSV: Now Eli was ninety-eight years old and his eyes were set, so that he could not see.
text.Rotherham: Now, Eli, was ninety-eight years old; and his eyes were fixed, that he could not see.
