text.DRB: Who answering said to them: Here he is. Lo before thee, make haste now, for this day he came into the city, because this day there is a sacrifice of the people in the excelse.

text.NRSV: As soon as you enter the town, you will find him, before he goes up to the shrine to eat. For the people will not eat until he comes, since he must bless the sacrifice; afterward those eat who are invited. Now go up, for you will meet him immediately.”
text.Rotherham: As ye enter the city, so, shall ye surely find him, ere yet he shall go up to the high place to eat, for the people will not eat until he hath come, for, he, doth bless the sacrifice, after that, will they eat who have been bidden. Now, therefore, go up, for, about this very time, shall ye surely find him.
text.KJV: As soon as ye be come into the city, ye shall straightway find him, before he go up to the high place to eat: for the people will not eat until he come, because he doth bless the sacrifice; and afterwards they eat that be bidden. Now therefore get you up; for about this time ye shall find him.
text.NABRE: When you enter the city, you may reach him before he goes up to the high place to eat. The people will not eat until he arrives; only after he blesses the sacrifice will the invited guests eat. Go up immediately, for you should find him right now.”
text.DRB: Entering into the city immediately you shall find him, before he go up into the excelse to eat, for the people will not eat till he come: because he will bless the Host, and afterward they shall eat that are invited. Now therefore go up, because this day you shall find him.

text.NRSV: So they went up to the town. As they were entering the town, they saw Samuel coming out toward them on his way up to the shrine.
text.Rotherham: So they went up into the city. As they were entering into the midst of the city, lo! Samuel, coming out over against them, to ascend the high place.
text.KJV: And they went up into the city: and when they were come into the city, behold, Samuel came out against them, for to go up to the high place.
text.NABRE: Saul Meets Samuel. So they went up to the city. As they entered it—there was Samuel coming toward them on his way to the high place.
text.DRB: And they went up into the city. And when they walked in the midst of the city, Samuel appeared coming forth against them, to go up into the excelse.

text.NRSV: Now the day before Saul came, the Lord had revealed to Samuel:
text.Rotherham: Now, Yahweh, had unveiled the ear of Samuel,—one day before Saul came, saying:
text.KJV: Now the Lord had told Samuel in his ear a day before Saul came, saying,
text.NABRE: The day before Saul’s arrival, the Lord had revealed to Samuel:
text.DRB: And our Lord had revealed the ear of Samuel one day before Saul came, saying:

text.NRSV: “Tomorrow about this time I will send to you a man from the land of Benjamin, and you shall anoint him to be ruler over my people Israel. He shall save my people from the hand of the Philistines; for I have seen the suffering of my people, because their outcry has come to me.”
text.Rotherham: About this time to-morrow, will I send unto thee, a man out of the land of Benjamin, whom thou shalt anoint, to be leader over my people Israel, and he shall save my people, out of the hand of the Philistines,—for I have looked upon the oppression, because their outcry hath come in unto me.
text.KJV: To morrow about this time I will send thee a man out of the land of Benjamin, and thou shalt anoint him to be captain over my people Israel, that he may save my people out of the hand of the Philistines: for I have looked upon my people, because their cry is come unto me.
text.NABRE: At this time tomorrow I will send you a man from the land of Benjamin whom you are to anoint as ruler of my people Israel. He shall save my people from the hand of the Philistines. I have looked upon my people; their cry has come to me.
text.DRB: This very hour, that now is, tomorrow will I send to thee a man of the Land of Benjamin, and thou shalt anoint him ruler over my people of Israel: & he shall save my people from the hand of the Philistiims: because I have respected my people, for their cry is come to me.

text.NRSV: When Samuel saw Saul, the Lord told him, “Here is the man of whom I spoke to you. He it is who shall rule over my people.”
text.Rotherham: And, when, Samuel, beheld Saul, Yahweh, answered him, Lo! the man, of whom I said unto thee, Here, is one shall control my people.
text.KJV: And when Samuel saw Saul, the Lord said unto him, Behold the man whom I spake to thee of! this same shall reign over my people.
text.NABRE: When Samuel caught sight of Saul, the Lord assured him: This is the man I told you about; he shall govern my people.
text.DRB: And when Samuel had beheld Saul, our Lord said to him: behold the man, of whom I told thee, this man shall rule over my people.

text.NRSV: Then Saul approached Samuel inside the gate, and said, “Tell me, please, where is the house of the seer?”
text.Rotherham: And Saul drew near unto Samuel, in the midst of the gate,—and said—I pray thee, do tell me, where is the house of the seer?
text.KJV: Then Saul drew near to Samuel in the gate, and said, Tell me, I pray thee, where the seer’s house is.
text.NABRE: Saul met Samuel in the gateway and said, “Please tell me where the seer lives.”
text.DRB: And Saul came to Samuel in the midst of the gate, & said: Shew me, I pray thee, where is the house of the seer?

text.NRSV: Samuel answered Saul, “I am the seer; go up before me to the shrine, for today you shall eat with me, and in the morning I will let you go and will tell you all that is on your mind.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel answered Saul, and said—I, am, the seer! Go up before me, to the high place, so shall ye eat with me, to-day,—and I will let thee go in the morning, and, all that is in thy heart, will I tell thee.
text.KJV: And Samuel answered Saul, and said, I am the seer: go up before me unto the high place; for ye shall eat with me to day, and to morrow I will let thee go, and will tell thee all that is in thine heart.
text.NABRE: Samuel answered Saul: “I am the seer. Go up ahead of me to the high place and eat with me today. In the morning, before letting you go, I will tell you everything on your mind.
text.DRB: And Samuel answered Saul, saying: I am the seer, go up before me into the excelse, that you may eat with me today, & I will dismiss thee in the morning: and all things that are in thy heart, will I tell thee.

text.NRSV: As for your donkeys that were lost three days ago, give no further thought to them, for they have been found. And on whom is all Israel’s desire fixed, if not on you and on all your ancestral house?”
text.Rotherham: And, as for the asses that went astray from thee three days ago, do not regard them, for they are found,—but to whom belongeth all that is desirable in Israel? is it not to thee, and to all the house of thy father?
text.KJV: And as for thine asses that were lost three days ago, set not thy mind on them; for they are found. And on whom is all the desire of Israel? Is it not on thee, and on all thy father’s house?
text.NABRE: As for your donkeys that were lost three days ago, do not worry about them, for they have been found. Whom should Israel want if not you and your father’s family?”
text.DRB: And concerning the asses, which thou didst lose three days agone, be not careful, because they are found. And whose shall be all the best things of Israel? Not to thee and to all thy father's house?

text.NRSV: Saul answered, “I am only a Benjaminite, from the least of the tribes of Israel, and my family is the humblest of all the families of the tribe of Benjamin. Why then have you spoken to me in this way?”
text.Rotherham: Then answered Saul, and said—Am not I, a man of Benjamin, of the smallest of the tribes of Israel, and [is not], my family, the poorest of all the families of the tribe of Benjamin? Wherefore, then, hast thou spoken unto me of such a thing as this?
text.KJV: And Saul answered and said, Am not I a Benjamite, of the smallest of the tribes of Israel? and my family the least of all the families of the tribe of Benjamin? wherefore then speakest thou so to me?
text.NABRE: Saul replied: “Am I not a Benjaminite, from the smallest of the tribes of Israel, and is not my clan the least among the clans of the tribe of Benjamin? Why say such things to me?” The Meal.
text.DRB: And Saul answering, said: Am not I the son of Jemini of the least tribe of Israel, and my kindred the last among all the families of the tribe of Benjamin? Why therefore hast thou spoken this word to me?

text.NRSV: Then Samuel took Saul and his servant-boy and brought them into the hall, and gave them a place at the head of those who had been invited, of whom there were about thirty.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel took Saul and his young man, and brought them into the guest-chamber,—and gave them a place at the head of them who were bidden, they being about thirty persons.
text.KJV: And Samuel took Saul and his servant, and brought them into the parlour, and made them sit in the chiefest place among them that were bidden, which were about thirty persons.
text.NABRE: Samuel then took Saul and his servant and brought them into the room. He seated them at the head of the guests, of whom there were about thirty.
text.DRB: Samuel therefore taking Saul & his servant, brought them into the parlour, & gave them a place in the chief room of them that were invited. For there were about thirty men.

text.NRSV: And Samuel said to the cook, “Bring the portion I gave you, the one I asked you to put aside.”
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said to the cook, Set on the portion that I gave thee,—of which I said unto thee, Put it by thee.
text.KJV: And Samuel said unto the cook, Bring the portion which I gave thee, of which I said unto thee, Set it by thee.
text.NABRE: He said to the cook, “Bring the portion I gave you and told you to put aside.”
text.DRB: And Samuel said to the cook: Give the portion, which I gave thee, and commanded that thou shouldst lay it up apart with thee.

text.NRSV: The cook took up the thigh and what went with it and set them before Saul. Samuel said, “See, what was kept is set before you. Eat; for it is set for you for this appointed time, as I said when I invited the people.” So Saul ate with Samuel that day.
text.Rotherham: And the cook took up the shoulder, and that which was upon it, and placed it before Saul, and said—Lo! the part reserved! Set it before thee [and] eat, for, unto the time appointed, hath it been kept for thee, since the time that I said, The people, have I bidden. So Saul did eat with Samuel, on that day.
text.KJV: And the cook took up the shoulder, and that which was upon it, and set it before Saul. And Samuel said, Behold that which is left! set it before thee, and eat: for unto this time hath it been kept for thee since I said, I have invited the people. So Saul did eat with Samuel that day.
text.NABRE: So the cook took up the leg and what went with it, and placed it before Saul. Samuel said: “This is a reserved portion that is set before you. Eat, for it was kept for you until this time; I explained that I was inviting some guests.” Thus Saul dined with Samuel that day.
text.DRB: And the cook lifted up a shoulder, & set it before Saul. And Samuel said: Behold that which hath remained, set it before thee, & eat: because of purpose it was kept for thee, when I called the people. And Saul did eat with Samuel that day.

text.NRSV: When they came down from the shrine into the town, a bed was spread for Saul on the roof, and he lay down to sleep.
text.Rotherham: And, when they had come down from the high place into the city, he spread a couch for Saul upon the house-top, and he lay down.
text.KJV: And when they were come down from the high place into the city, Samuel communed with Saul upon the top of the house.
text.NABRE: When they came down from the high place into the city, a mattress was spread for Saul on the roof,
text.DRB: And they descended from the excelse into the town, & he spake with Saul in the top of the house: & he prepared a bed for Saul in the highest room, & he slept.

text.NRSV: Then at the break of dawn Samuel called to Saul upon the roof, “Get up, so that I may send you on your way.” Saul got up, and both he and Samuel went out into the street.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, at the uprisings of the dawn, that Samuel called unto Saul on the house-top, saying, Arise! that I may send thee away. So Saul arose, and they two, he and Samuel, went forth abroad.
text.KJV: And they arose early: and it came to pass about the spring of the day, that Samuel called Saul to the top of the house, saying, Up, that I may send thee away. And Saul arose, and they went out both of them, he and Samuel, abroad.
text.NABRE: and he slept there. Saul’s Anointing. At daybreak Samuel called to Saul on the roof, “Get up, and I will send you on your way.” Saul got up, and he and Samuel went outside the city together.
text.DRB: And when they were risen in the morning, and it began now to be light, Samuel called Saul in the high chamber, saying: Arise that I may dismiss thee. And Saul arose: and they went both forth, to wit, he and Samuel.

text.NRSV: As they were going down to the outskirts of the town, Samuel said to Saul, “Tell the boy to go on before us, and when he has passed on, stop here yourself for a while, that I may make known to you the word of God.”
text.Rotherham: As they were going down the end of the city, Samuel, said unto Saul—Bid the young man, that he pass on before us, (and he passed on) but, thou, stand still where thou art, that I may let thee hear the word of God.
text.KJV: And as they were going down to the end of the city, Samuel said to Saul, Bid the servant pass on before us, (and he passed on,) but stand thou still a while, that I may shew thee the word of God.
text.NABRE: As they were approaching the edge of the town, Samuel said to Saul, “Tell the servant to go on ahead of us, but you stay here for a moment, that I may give you a word from God.”
text.DRB: And when they came down in the uttermost part of the city, Samuel said to Saul: Speak to the servant that he go before us, and pass: but stay thou a little while, that I may tell thee the word of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Samuel took a vial of oil and poured it on his head, and kissed him; he said, “The Lord has anointed you ruler over his people Israel. You shall reign over the people of the Lord and you will save them from the hand of their enemies all around. Now this shall be the sign to you that the Lord has anointed you ruler over his heritage:
text.Rotherham: Then took Samuel a flask of oil, and poured out upon his head, and kissed him,—and said—Is it not, that Yahweh hath anointed thee over his inheritance, as leader?
text.KJV: Then Samuel took a vial of oil, and poured it upon his head, and kissed him, and said, Is it not because the Lord hath anointed thee to be captain over his inheritance?
text.NABRE: Then, from a flask he had with him, Samuel poured oil on Saul’s head and kissed him, saying: “The Lord anoints you ruler over his people Israel. You are the one who will govern the Lord ’s people and save them from the power of their enemies all around them. The Signs Foretold. “This will be the sign for you that the Lord has anointed you ruler over his heritage:
text.DRB: And Samuel took a little vessel of oil, and poured upon his head, and kissed him, and said: Behold, our Lord hath anointed thee upon his inheritance to be Prince, and thou shalt deliver his people out of the hands of their enemies, that are round about them. And this shall be a sign unto thee, that God hath anointed thee to be Prince.

text.NRSV: When you depart from me today you will meet two men by Rachel’s tomb in the territory of Benjamin at Zelzah; they will say to you, ‘The donkeys that you went to seek are found, and now your father has stopped worrying about them and is worrying about you, saying: What shall I do about my son?’
text.Rotherham: When thou departest, to-day, from me, then shalt thou find two men by the grave of Rachel, within the boundary of Benjamin, in Zelzah,—and they will say unto thee, The asses are found, which thou wentest to seek, and lo! thy father hath abandoned caring for the asses, and is concerned for you, saying, What shall I do for my son?
text.KJV: When thou art departed from me to day, then thou shalt find two men by Rachel’s sepulchre in the border of Benjamin at Zelzah; and they will say unto thee, The asses which thou wentest to seek are found: and, lo, thy father hath left the care of the asses, and sorroweth for you, saying, What shall I do for my son?
text.NABRE: When you leave me today, you will meet two men near Rachel’s tomb at Zelzah in the territory of Benjamin. They will say to you, ‘The donkeys you went to look for have been found. Now your father is no longer worried about the donkeys, but is anxious about you and says: What shall I do about my son?’
text.DRB: When thou shalt be departed from me this day, thou shalt find two men beside the sepulchre of Rachel in the borders of Benjamin, in the South, and they shall say to thee: The asses are found, which thou didst go to see: and thy father letting go the asses, is careful for you, and saith: What shall I do concerning my son?

text.NRSV: Then you shall go on from there further and come to the oak of Tabor; three men going up to God at Bethel will meet you there, one carrying three kids, another carrying three loaves of bread, and another carrying a skin of wine.
text.Rotherham: Then shalt thou pass on quickly from thence onwards, and come as far as the oak of Tabor, and there shall find thee there, three men going up unto God, at Bethel,—one, carrying three kids, and, another, carrying three cakes of bread, and, another, carrying a skin of wine;
text.KJV: Then shalt thou go on forward from thence, and thou shalt come to the plain of Tabor, and there shall meet thee three men going up to God to Beth–el, one carrying three kids, and another carrying three loaves of bread, and another carrying a bottle of wine:
text.NABRE: Farther on, when you arrive at the oak of Tabor, three men will meet you as they go up to God at Bethel; one will be bringing three young goats, another three loaves of bread, and the third a skin of wine.
text.DRB: And when thou shalt depart thence, and pass farther, and shalt come to the oak Thabor, three men going up to God into Bethel shall find thee there, one carrying three kids, and another three manchets of bread, and another carrying a flagon of wine.

text.NRSV: They will greet you and give you two loaves of bread, which you shall accept from them.
text.Rotherham: then will they ask thee, of thy welfare,—and give thee two cakes of bread, which thou shalt receive at their hand.
text.KJV: And they will salute thee, and give thee two loaves of bread; which thou shalt receive of their hands.
text.NABRE: They will greet you and offer you two elevated offerings of bread, which you should accept from them.
text.DRB: And when they have saluted thee, they will give thee two loaves, and thou shalt take them of their hand.

text.NRSV: After that you shall come to Gibeath-elohim, at the place where the Philistine garrison is; there, as you come to the town, you will meet a band of prophets coming down from the shrine with harp, tambourine, flute, and lyre playing in front of them; they will be in a prophetic frenzy.
text.Rotherham: After that, shalt thou come unto the hill of God, where is the garrison of the Philistines,—and it shall be, as thou comest in thither into the city, thou shalt light upon a band of prophets, coming down from the high place, and, before them, a harp, and a timbrel, and a flute, and a lyre, they having been moved to prophesy.
text.KJV: After that thou shalt come to the hill of God, where is the garrison of the Philistines: and it shall come to pass, when thou art come thither to the city, that thou shalt meet a company of prophets coming down from the high place with a psaltery, and a tabret, and a pipe, and a harp, before them; and they shall prophesy:
text.NABRE: After that you will come to Gibeath-elohim, where the Philistine garrison is located. As you enter that city, you will meet a band of prophets coming down from the high place. They will be preceded by lyres, tambourines, flutes, and harps, and will be in prophetic ecstasy.
text.DRB: After these things thou shalt come into the hill of God, where the garrison of the Philisthiims is: and when thou shalt be entered there into the city, thou shalt meet there a flock of Prophets coming down from the excelse, and before them psaltery and timbrel, and shalm, and harp, and themselves prophesying.

text.NRSV: Then the spirit of the Lord will possess you, and you will be in a prophetic frenzy along with them and be turned into a different person.
text.Rotherham: Then will come suddenly upon thee, the Spirit of Yahweh, and thou shalt be moved to prophesy with them,—and shalt be changed into another man.
text.KJV: And the Spirit of the Lord will come upon thee, and thou shalt prophesy with them, and shalt be turned into another man.
text.NABRE: The spirit of the Lord will rush upon you, and you will join them in their prophetic ecstasy and will become a changed man.
text.DRB: And the Spirit of our Lord shall seize upon thee, and thou shalt prophesy with them, and shalt be changed into another man.

text.NRSV: Now when these signs meet you, do whatever you see fit to do, for God is with you.
text.Rotherham: And it shall be, when these signs shall come unto thee, then act thou for thyself, as thou shalt find occasion, for, God, is with thee.
text.KJV: And let it be, when these signs are come unto thee, that thou do as occasion serve thee; for God is with thee.
text.NABRE: When these signs have come to pass, do whatever lies to hand, because God is with you.
text.DRB: Therefore when all these signs shall chance to thee, do whatsoever thy hand shall find, because our Lord is with thee.

text.NRSV: And you shall go down to Gilgal ahead of me; then I will come down to you to present burnt offerings and offer sacrifices of well-being. Seven days you shall wait, until I come to you and show you what you shall do.”
text.Rotherham: And thou shalt go down before me to Gilgal, for lo! I am coming down unto thee, to offer up ascending-offerings, to sacrifice peace-offerings,—seven days, shalt thou tarry, until I come unto thee, then will I let thee know what thou shalt do.
text.KJV: And thou shalt go down before me to Gilgal; and, behold, I will come down unto thee, to offer burnt offerings, and to sacrifice sacrifices of peace offerings: seven days shalt thou tarry, till I come to thee, and shew thee what thou shalt do.
text.NABRE: Now go down ahead of me to Gilgal, for I shall come down to you, to offer burnt offerings and to sacrifice communion offerings. Wait seven days until I come to you; I shall then tell you what you must do.”
text.DRB: And thou shalt go down before me into Galgala (for I will come down to thee) that thou mayest offer oblation, and immolate pacific victims: seven days shalt thou expect, till I come to thee, & I will shew thee what thou must do.

text.NRSV: As he turned away to leave Samuel, God gave him another heart; and all these signs were fulfilled that day.
text.Rotherham: And so it was, that, when he turned away to depart from Samuel, God gave him another heart,—and all these signs came to pass that day.
text.KJV: And it was so, that when he had turned his back to go from Samuel, God gave him another heart: and all those signs came to pass that day.
text.NABRE: The Signs Come to Pass. As Saul turned to leave Samuel, God changed his heart. That very day all these signs came to pass….
text.DRB: Therefore when he had turned away his shoulder to depart from Samuel, God changed unto him another heart, and all these things came in that day.

text.NRSV: When they were going from there to Gibeah, a band of prophets met him; and the spirit of God possessed him, and he fell into a prophetic frenzy along with them.
text.Rotherham: And, when they came thither to the hill, lo! a band of prophets coming to meet him,—then came suddenly upon him, the Spirit of God, and he was moved to prophesy in their midst.
text.KJV: And when they came thither to the hill, behold, a company of prophets met him; and the Spirit of God came upon him, and he prophesied among them.
text.NABRE: From there they arrived at Gibeah, where a band of prophets met Saul, and the spirit of God rushed upon him, so that he joined them in their prophetic ecstasy.
text.DRB: And they came to the foresaid hill, and behold a troop of Prophets meeting him: & the Spirit of our Lord seized upon him, and he prophesied in the midst of them.

text.NRSV: When all who knew him before saw how he prophesied with the prophets, the people said to one another, “What has come over the son of Kish? Is Saul also among the prophets?”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, that, all who knew him aforetime, looked, and lo! with the prophets, he did prophesy. So the people said, one to another—What, now, hath befallen the son of Kish? Is, even Saul, among the prophets?
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when all that knew him beforetime saw that, behold, he prophesied among the prophets, then the people said one to another, What is this that is come unto the son of Kish? Is Saul also among the prophets?
text.NABRE: When all who had known him previously saw him in a prophetic state among the prophets, they said to one another, “What has happened to the son of Kish? Is Saul also among the prophets?”
text.DRB: And all that had known him yesterday and the day before, seeing that he was with the prophets, & did prophesy, said to each other: What thing hath happened to the son of Cis? What is Saul also among the Prophets?

text.NRSV: A man of the place answered, “And who is their father?” Therefore it became a proverb, “Is Saul also among the prophets?”
text.Rotherham: Then responded one of that place, and said, But who is, their father? For this cause, it became a proverb, Is, even Saul, among the prophets?
text.KJV: And one of the same place answered and said, But who is their father? Therefore it became a proverb, Is Saul also among the prophets?
text.NABRE: And someone from that district responded, “And who is their father?” Thus the saying arose, “Is Saul also among the prophets?”
text.DRB: And one answered another, saying: And who is their father? Therefore it was turned into a proverb: What is Saul also among the Prophets?

text.NRSV: When his prophetic frenzy had ended, he went home.
text.Rotherham: And, when he had made an end of prophesying, he came to the high place.
text.KJV: And when he had made an end of prophesying, he came to the high place.
text.NABRE: When he came out of the prophetic ecstasy, he went home.
text.DRB: And he ceased to prophesy, and came to the excelse.

text.NRSV: Saul’s uncle said to him and to the boy, “Where did you go?” And he replied, “To seek the donkeys; and when we saw they were not to be found, we went to Samuel.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul’s uncle unto him, and unto his young man—Whither have ye been? And he said, To seek the asses, and, when they were nowhere, to be seen, we came unto Samuel.
text.KJV: And Saul’s uncle said unto him and to his servant, Whither went ye? And he said, To seek the asses: and when we saw that they were no where, we came to Samuel.
text.NABRE: Silence About the Kingship. Saul’s uncle asked him and his servant, “Where have you been?” Saul replied, “Looking for the donkeys. When we could not find them, we went to Samuel.”
text.DRB: And Saul's uncle said to him, and to his servant: Whither went you? Who answered: To seek the asses: which when we had not found we came to Samuel.

text.NRSV: Saul’s uncle said, “Tell me what Samuel said to you.”
text.Rotherham: And Saul’s uncle said,—Do tell me, I pray thee, what Samuel said to you.
text.KJV: And Saul’s uncle said, Tell me, I pray thee, what Samuel said unto you.
text.NABRE: Saul’s uncle said, “Tell me, then, what Samuel said to you.”
text.DRB: And his uncle said to him: Tell me what Samuel said to thee.

text.NRSV: Saul said to his uncle, “He told us that the donkeys had been found.” But about the matter of the kingship, of which Samuel had spoken, he did not tell him anything.
text.Rotherham: And Saul said unto his uncle, He, told, us that the asses were found,—but, as to the matter of the kingdom, he told him not what Samuel had said.
text.KJV: And Saul said unto his uncle, He told us plainly that the asses were found. But of the matter of the kingdom, whereof Samuel spake, he told him not.
text.NABRE: Saul said to his uncle, “He assured us that the donkeys had been found.” But Saul told him nothing about what Samuel had said about the kingship.
text.DRB: And Saul said to his uncle: He told us that the asses were found. But concerning the word of the Kingdom which Samuel had spoken to him, he told him not.

text.NRSV: Samuel summoned the people to the Lord at Mizpah
text.Rotherham: Then Samuel called out the people, unto Yahweh, at Mizpah,
text.KJV: And Samuel called the people together unto the Lord to Mizpeh;
text.NABRE: Saul Chosen King. Samuel called the people together to the Lord at Mizpah
text.DRB: And Samuel called together the people of our Lord in Maspha:

text.NRSV: and said to them, “Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel, ‘I brought up Israel out of Egypt, and I rescued you from the hand of the Egyptians and from the hand of all the kingdoms that were oppressing you.’
text.Rotherham: and said unto the sons of Israel—Thus, saith Yahweh, God of Israel: I myself, brought up Israel, out of Egypt,—and rescued you out of the hand of the Egyptians, and out of the hand of all the kingdoms that distressed you.
text.KJV: And said unto the children of Israel, Thus saith the Lord God of Israel, I brought up Israel out of Egypt, and delivered you out of the hand of the Egyptians, and out of the hand of all kingdoms, and of them that oppressed you:
text.NABRE: and addressed the Israelites: “Thus says the Lord, the God of Israel: It was I who brought Israel up from Egypt and delivered you from the power of the Egyptians and from the power of all the kingdoms that oppressed you.
text.DRB: And said to the children of Israel: Thus saith our Lord the God of Israel: I brought Israel out of Ægypt, and delivered you from the hand of the Ægyptians, and from the hand of all the kings which afflicted you.

text.NRSV: But today you have rejected your God, who saves you from all your calamities and your distresses; and you have said, ‘No! but set a king over us.’ Now therefore present yourselves before the Lord by your tribes and by your clans.”
text.Rotherham: Yet, ye, to-day, have rejected your God, who, himself, hath been giving you salvation from all your calamities and your distresses, and ye have said to him: A king, shalt thou set over us,—Now, therefore, present yourselves before Yahweh, by your tribes, and by your thousands.
text.KJV: And ye have this day rejected your God, who himself saved you out of all your adversities and your tribulations; and ye have said unto him, Nay, but set a king over us. Now therefore present yourselves before the Lord by your tribes, and by your thousands.
text.NABRE: But today you have rejected your God, who saves you from all your evils and calamities, by saying, ‘No! You must appoint a king over us.’ Now, therefore, take your stand before the Lord according to your tribes and families.”
text.DRB: But you this day have rejected your God, who only hath saved you out of all your evils and tribulations: and you have said: Not so: but appoint a king over us. Now therefore stand before our Lord by your tribes, and by your families.

text.NRSV: Then Samuel brought all the tribes of Israel near, and the tribe of Benjamin was taken by lot.
text.Rotherham: And, when Samuel had brought near all the tribes of Israel, then was taken the tribe of Benjamin.
text.KJV: And when Samuel had caused all the tribes of Israel to come near, the tribe of Benjamin was taken.
text.NABRE: So Samuel had all the tribes of Israel come forward, and the tribe of Benjamin was chosen.
text.DRB: And Samuel brought all the tribes of Israel, and the lot fell on the tribe of Benjamin.

text.NRSV: He brought the tribe of Benjamin near by its families, and the family of the Matrites was taken by lot. Finally he brought the family of the Matrites near man by man, and Saul the son of Kish was taken by lot. But when they sought him, he could not be found.
text.Rotherham: And, when he had brought near the tribe of Benjamin, by their families, then was taken the family of Matri,—and, when he had brought near the family of Matri, man by man, then was taken Saul the son of Kish; so they sought him, but he was not to be found.
text.KJV: When he had caused the tribe of Benjamin to come near by their families, the family of Matri was taken, and Saul the son of Kish was taken: and when they sought him, he could not be found.
text.NABRE: Next he had the tribe of Benjamin come forward by clans, and the clan of Matri was chosen, and finally Saul, son of Kish, was chosen. But when they went to look for him, he was nowhere to be found.
text.DRB: And he brought the tribe of Benjamin and the kindreds thereof, & it fell upon the kindred of Metri, and it came unto Saul the son of Cis. They therefore sought him, and he was not found.

text.NRSV: So they inquired again of the Lord, “Did the man come here?” and the Lord said, “See, he has hidden himself among the baggage.”
text.Rotherham: Then asked they again of Yahweh, Hath there yet to come in hither a man? And Yahweh said, Lo! he, hath hid himself among the stores.
text.KJV: Therefore they enquired of the Lord further, if the man should yet come thither. And the Lord answered, Behold, he hath hid himself among the stuff.
text.NABRE: Again they consulted the Lord, “Is there still someone else to come forward?” The Lord answered: He is hiding among the baggage.
text.DRB: And after these things they consulted our Lord whether he would come thither. And our Lord answered: Behold he is hid at home.

text.NRSV: Then they ran and brought him from there. When he took his stand among the people, he was head and shoulders taller than any of them.
text.Rotherham: So they ran, and fetched him thence, and, when he presented himself in the midst of the people, then was he [seen to be] taller than any of the people, from his shoulders and upwards.
text.KJV: And they ran and fetched him thence: and when he stood among the people, he was higher than any of the people from his shoulders and upward.
text.NABRE: They ran to bring him from there; when he took his place among the people, he stood head and shoulders above all the people.
text.DRB: They ran therefore and took him from thence: and he stood in the midst of the people, and he was higher than all the people from the shoulder and upward.

text.NRSV: Samuel said to all the people, “Do you see the one whom the Lord has chosen? There is no one like him among all the people.” And all the people shouted, “Long live the king!”
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said unto all the people—Have ye seen him whom Yahweh hath chosen, that there is none like him, among all the people? And all the people shouted, and said—Let the king live!
text.KJV: And Samuel said to all the people, See ye him whom the Lord hath chosen, that there is none like him among all the people? And all the people shouted, and said, God save the king.
text.NABRE: Then Samuel addressed all the people, “Do you see the man whom the Lord has chosen? There is no one like him among all the people!” Then all the people shouted out, “Long live the king!”
text.DRB: And Samuel said to all the people: Certes you see whom our Lord hath chosen, that there is not the like to him in all the people. And all the people cried, and said: God save the king.

text.NRSV: Samuel told the people the rights and duties of the kingship; and he wrote them in a book and laid it up before the Lord. Then Samuel sent all the people back to their homes.
text.Rotherham: Then Samuel declared unto the people the manner of the kingdom, and wrote it in a scroll, and laid it up before Yahweh. And Samuel sent away all the people, every man to his own house.
text.KJV: Then Samuel told the people the manner of the kingdom, and wrote it in a book, and laid it up before the Lord. And Samuel sent all the people away, every man to his house.
text.NABRE: Samuel next explained to the people the rules of the monarchy, wrote them in a book, and placed them before the presence of the Lord. Samuel then sent the people back to their own homes.
text.DRB: And Samuel spake to the people the law of the Kingdom, and wrote it in a book, and laid it up before our Lord: and Samuel dismissed all the people, every one into his own house.

text.NRSV: Saul also went to his home at Gibeah, and with him went warriors whose hearts God had touched.
text.Rotherham: Yea, even Saul, went to his own house, at Gibeah,—and the valiant men whose heart God had moved went with him.
text.KJV: And Saul also went home to Gibeah; and there went with him a band of men, whose hearts God had touched.
text.NABRE: Saul also went home to Gibeah, accompanied by warriors whose hearts the Lord had touched.
text.DRB: But Saul also departed into his house into Gabaa: & there went with him part of the army, they whose hearts God had touched.

text.NRSV: But some worthless fellows said, “How can this man save us?” They despised him and brought him no present. But he held his peace. Now Nahash, king of the Ammonites, had been grievously oppressing the Gadites and the Reubenites. He would gouge out the right eye of each of them and would not grant Israel a deliverer. No one was left of the Israelites across the Jordan whose right eye Nahash, king of the Ammonites, had not gouged out. But there were seven thousand men who had escaped from the Ammonites and had entered Jabesh-gilead.
text.Rotherham: But, abandoned men, said—How can this one save us? So they treated him with contempt, and brought him no present,—but he was as one that was deaf.
text.KJV: But the children of Belial said, How shall this man save us? And they despised him, and brought him no presents. But he held his peace.
text.NABRE: But some worthless people said, “How can this fellow save us?” They despised him and brought him no tribute.
text.DRB: But the children of Belial said: What, shall this fellow be able to save us? And they despised him, & brought him not presents: but he dissembled as though he heard not.

text.NRSV: About a month later, Nahash the Ammonite went up and besieged Jabesh-gilead; and all the men of Jabesh said to Nahash, “Make a treaty with us, and we will serve you.”
text.Rotherham: Then came up Nahash the Ammonite, and encamped against Jabesh-gilead,—and all the man of Jabesh said unto Nahash, Solemnise with us a covenant, that we may serve thee.
text.KJV: Then Nahash the Ammonite came up, and encamped against Jabesh–gilead: and all the men of Jabesh said unto Nahash, Make a covenant with us, and we will serve thee.
text.NABRE: About a month later, Nahash the Ammonite went up and besieged Jabesh-gilead. All the people of Jabesh begged Nahash, “Make a treaty with us, and we will serve you.”
text.DRB: And it came to pass as it were a month after, Naas the Ammonite ascended, and began to fight against Jabes of Galaad. And all the men of Jabes said to Naas: Make a league with us, and we will serve thee.

text.NRSV: But Nahash the Ammonite said to them, “On this condition I will make a treaty with you, namely that I gouge out everyone’s right eye, and thus put disgrace upon all Israel.”
text.Rotherham: And Nahash the Ammonite said unto them, Hereby, will I solemnise [a covenant] with you by digging out for you, every one’s right eye,—and laying it for a reproach, upon all Israel.
text.KJV: And Nahash the Ammonite answered them, On this condition will I make a covenant with you, that I may thrust out all your right eyes, and lay it for a reproach upon all Israel.
text.NABRE: But Nahash the Ammonite replied, “This is my condition for making a treaty with you: I will gouge out the right eye of every man, and thus bring shame on all Israel.”
text.DRB: And Naas the Ammonite answered them: In this will I make a league with you, that I may pluck out the right eyes of you all, and may make you a reproach in all Israel.

text.NRSV: The elders of Jabesh said to him, “Give us seven days’ respite that we may send messengers through all the territory of Israel. Then, if there is no one to save us, we will give ourselves up to you.”
text.Rotherham: And the elders of Jabesh said unto him—Give us a respite of seven days, that we may send messengers throughout all the bounds of Israel,—and then, if there is none to save us, we will come out unto thee.
text.KJV: And the elders of Jabesh said unto him, Give us seven days’ respite, that we may send messengers unto all the coasts of Israel: and then, if there be no man to save us, we will come out to thee.
text.NABRE: The elders of Jabesh said to him: “Give us seven days to send messengers throughout the territory of Israel. If there is no one to save us, we will surrender to you.”
text.DRB: And the ancients of Jabes said to him: Grant unto us seven days, that we may send messengers unto all the coasts of Israel: & if there shall not be that may defend us, we will come forth to thee.

text.NRSV: When the messengers came to Gibeah of Saul, they reported the matter in the hearing of the people; and all the people wept aloud.
text.Rotherham: And, when the messengers came to Gibeah of Saul, and spake the words in the ears of the people, all the people lifted up their voice, and wept.
text.KJV: Then came the messengers to Gibeah of Saul, and told the tidings in the ears of the people: and all the people lifted up their voices, and wept.
text.NABRE: When the messengers arrived at Gibeah of Saul and reported the news in the people’s hearing, they all wept aloud.
text.DRB: The messengers therefore came into Gabaa of Saul: and they spake these words, in the hearing of the people: and all the people lifted up their voice, and wept.

text.NRSV: Now Saul was coming from the field behind the oxen; and Saul said, “What is the matter with the people, that they are weeping?” So they told him the message from the inhabitants of Jabesh.
text.Rotherham: But lo! Saul, came in, following the oxen, out of the field, and Saul said, What aileth the people, that they should weep? Then were recounted to him the words of the men of Jabesh.
text.KJV: And, behold, Saul came after the herd out of the field; and Saul said, What aileth the people that they weep? And they told him the tidings of the men of Jabesh.
text.NABRE: Just then Saul came in from the field, behind his oxen. “Why are the people weeping?” he asked. They repeated the message of the inhabitants of Jabesh for him.
text.DRB: And behold Saul came, following oxen out of the field, and said: What aileth the people that they weep? And they told him the words of the men of Jabes.

text.NRSV: And the spirit of God came upon Saul in power when he heard these words, and his anger was greatly kindled.
text.Rotherham: And the Spirit of God came suddenly upon Saul, when he heard these words,—and his anger raged furiously.
text.KJV: And the Spirit of God came upon Saul when he heard those tidings, and his anger was kindled greatly.
text.NABRE: As he listened to this report, the spirit of God rushed upon him and he became very angry.
text.DRB: And the Spirit of our Lord seized on Saul, when he had heard these words, and his fury was exceeding wrath.

text.NRSV: He took a yoke of oxen, and cut them in pieces and sent them throughout all the territory of Israel by messengers, saying, “Whoever does not come out after Saul and Samuel, so shall it be done to his oxen!” Then the dread of the Lord fell upon the people, and they came out as one.
text.Rotherham: So he took a yoke of oxen, and cut them in pieces, and sent throughout all the bounds of Israel by the hand of messengers—saying, Whosoever cometh not forth after Saul, and after Samuel, so, shall it be done unto his oxen. Then felt the dread of Yahweh, upon the people, and they came forth, as one man.
text.KJV: And he took a yoke of oxen, and hewed them in pieces, and sent them throughout all the coasts of Israel by the hands of messengers, saying, Whosoever cometh not forth after Saul and after Samuel, so shall it be done unto his oxen. And the fear of the Lord fell on the people, and they came out with one consent.
text.NABRE: Taking a yoke of oxen, he cut them into pieces and sent them throughout the territory of Israel by messengers saying, “If anyone does not come out to follow Saul and Samuel, the same thing will be done to his oxen!” The dread of the Lord came upon the people and they went forth as one.
text.DRB: And taking both the oxen, he cut them into pieces, & sent them into all the coasts of Israel by messengers, saying: Whosoever shall not go forth, and follow Saul and Samuel, so shall it be done to his oxen. Therefore the fear of our Lord invaded the people, and they went forth as it were one man.

text.NRSV: When he mustered them at Bezek, those from Israel were three hundred thousand, and those from Judah seventy thousand.
text.Rotherham: And, when he numbered them in Bezek, the sons of Israel were found to be—three hundred thousand, and the men of Judah thirty thousand.
text.KJV: And when he numbered them in Bezek, the children of Israel were three hundred thousand, and the men of Judah thirty thousand.
text.NABRE: When Saul reviewed them in Bezek, there were three hundred thousand Israelites and seventy thousand Judahites.
text.DRB: And he numbered them in Bezec: and there were of the children of Israel three hundred thousand: and of the men of Juda thirty thousand.

text.NRSV: They said to the messengers who had come, “Thus shall you say to the inhabitants of Jabesh-gilead: ‘Tomorrow, by the time the sun is hot, you shall have deliverance.’” When the messengers came and told the inhabitants of Jabesh, they rejoiced.
text.Rotherham: So they said unto the messengers who had come—Thus, shall ye surely say to the men of Jabesh-gilead, To-morrow, shall ye have deliverance, about the time the son is hot. And, when the messengers came and told the men of Jabesh, they rejoiced.
text.KJV: And they said unto the messengers that came, Thus shall ye say unto the men of Jabesh–gilead, To morrow, by that time the sun be hot, ye shall have help. And the messengers came and shewed it to the men of Jabesh; and they were glad.
text.NABRE: To the messengers who had come he said, “Tell the inhabitants of Jabesh-gilead that tomorrow, when the sun grows hot, they will be saved.” The messengers went and reported this to the inhabitants of Jabesh, and they rejoiced.
text.DRB: And they said to the messengers that came: Thus shall you say to the men, that are in Jabes Galaad: Tomorrow, when the sun shall wax hot, you shall have relief. The messengers therefore came, & told the men of Jabes: Who were glad.

text.NRSV: So the inhabitants of Jabesh said, “Tomorrow we will give ourselves up to you, and you may do to us whatever seems good to you.”
text.Rotherham: So the men of Jabesh said, To-morrow, will we come out unto you,—Then shall ye do with us, according to all that is good in your eyes.
text.KJV: Therefore the men of Jabesh said, To morrow we will come out unto you, and ye shall do with us all that seemeth good unto you.
text.NABRE: The men of Jabesh said to Nahash, “Tomorrow we will surrender to you, and you may do with us whatever you want.”
text.DRB: And they said: In the morning we will come forth to you: & you shall do to us whatsoever shall please you.

text.NRSV: The next day Saul put the people in three companies. At the morning watch they came into the camp and cut down the Ammonites until the heat of the day; and those who survived were scattered, so that no two of them were left together.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass on the morrow, that Saul set the people in three companies, and they entered into the midst of the host, during the morning watch, and smote Ammon until the day was hot. And it came to pass that, they who were left, were scattered, so that there were not left among them, two together.
text.KJV: And it was so on the morrow, that Saul put the people in three companies; and they came into the midst of the host in the morning watch, and slew the Ammonites until the heat of the day: and it came to pass, that they which remained were scattered, so that two of them were not left together.
text.NABRE: The next day, Saul arranged his troops in three companies and invaded the camp during the dawn watch. They slaughtered Ammonites until the day had gotten hot; by then the survivors were so scattered that no two of them were left together.
text.DRB: And it came to pass, when the morrow was come, Saul set the people into three parts: & entered into the midst of the camp in the morning watch, and struck Ammon until the day waxed hot, & the rest were dispersed, so that there were not left among them two together.

text.NRSV: The people said to Samuel, “Who is it that said, ‘Shall Saul reign over us?’ Give them to us so that we may put them to death.”
text.Rotherham: Then said the people unto Samuel, Who is he that was saying, Shall, Saul, reign over us? Give up the men, that we may put them to death.
text.KJV: And the people said unto Samuel, Who is he that said, Shall Saul reign over us? bring the men, that we may put them to death.
text.NABRE: Saul Accepted as King. The people then said to Samuel: “Who questioned whether Saul should rule over us? Hand them over and we will put them to death.”
text.DRB: And the people said to Samuel: Who is this that said: what, shall Saul reign over us? Give us the men and we will kill them.

text.NRSV: But Saul said, “No one shall be put to death this day, for today the Lord has brought deliverance to Israel.”
text.Rotherham: But Saul said, There shall not be put to death a man, this day,—for, to-day, hath Yahweh wrought deliverance in Israel.
text.KJV: And Saul said, There shall not a man be put to death this day: for to day the Lord hath wrought salvation in Israel.
text.NABRE: But Saul objected, “No one will be put to death this day, for today the Lord has rescued Israel.”
text.DRB: And Saul said: No man shall be killed this day, because our Lord this day hath relieved Israel:

text.NRSV: Samuel said to the people, “Come, let us go to Gilgal and there renew the kingship.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Samuel unto the people, Come and let us go to Gilgal,—and let us there renew the kingdom.
text.KJV: Then said Samuel to the people, Come, and let us go to Gilgal, and renew the kingdom there.
text.NABRE: Samuel said to the people, “Come, let us go to Gilgal to renew the kingship there.”
text.DRB: And Samuel said to the people: Come and let us go into Galgal, and let us renew there a Kingdom.

text.NRSV: So all the people went to Gilgal, and there they made Saul king before the Lord in Gilgal. There they sacrificed offerings of well-being before the Lord, and there Saul and all the Israelites rejoiced greatly.
text.Rotherham: So all the people went to Gilgal, and made Saul king there, before Yahweh, in Gilgal, and offered there sacrifices of peace-offerings before Yahweh,—and Saul and all the men of Israel rejoiced there, exceedingly.
text.KJV: And all the people went to Gilgal; and there they made Saul king before the Lord in Gilgal; and there they sacrificed sacrifices of peace offerings before the Lord; and there Saul and all the men of Israel rejoiced greatly.
text.NABRE: So all the people went to Gilgal, and there they made Saul king in the Lord ’s presence. They also sacrificed communion offerings there before the Lord, and Saul and all the Israelites rejoiced greatly.
text.DRB: And all the people went into Galgal, and there they made Saul king before our Lord in Galgal, and they immolated there pacific victims before our Lord. And Saul rejoiced there, and all the men of Israel exceedingly.

text.NRSV: Samuel said to all Israel, “I have listened to you in all that you have said to me, and have set a king over you.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said unto all Israel, Lo! I have hearkened unto your voice, in all that ye said to me,—and have set over you a king.
text.KJV: And Samuel said unto all Israel, Behold, I have hearkened unto your voice in all that ye said unto me, and have made a king over you.
text.NABRE: Samuel addressed all Israel: “I have granted your request in every respect,” he said. “I have set a king over you
text.DRB: And Samuel said to all Israel: Behold I have heard your voice according to all things which you have spoken to me, and I have appointed a king over you.

text.NRSV: See, it is the king who leads you now; I am old and gray, but my sons are with you. I have led you from my youth until this day.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, lo! the king going to and fro before you, But, I, am old and grey-headed, and, my sons, lo! they are with you,—But, I, have gone to and fro before you from my youth until this day.
text.KJV: And now, behold, the king walketh before you: and I am old and grayheaded; and, behold, my sons are with you: and I have walked before you from my childhood unto this day.
text.NABRE: and now the king will lead you. As for me, I am old and gray, and my sons are among you. I was your leader from my youth to the present day.
text.DRB: And now the king goeth before you: and I am waxen old and have gray hairs: moreover my sons are with you: therefore having conversed with you from my youth until this day, lo I am ready.

text.NRSV: Here I am; testify against me before the Lord and before his anointed. Whose ox have I taken? Or whose donkey have I taken? Or whom have I defrauded? Whom have I oppressed? Or from whose hand have I taken a bribe to blind my eyes with it? Testify against me and I will restore it to you.”
text.Rotherham: Behold me! testify against me, before Yahweh, and before his Anointed—Whose, ox, have I taken? or whose, ass, have I taken? or whom have I oppressed? Whom have I crushed? or at whose, hands, have I taken a bribe, to cover up mine eyes therewith? and I will restore it unto you.
text.KJV: Behold, here I am: witness against me before the Lord, and before his anointed: whose ox have I taken? or whose ass have I taken? or whom have I defrauded? whom have I oppressed? or of whose hand have I received any bribe to blind mine eyes therewith? and I will restore it you.
text.NABRE: Here I stand! Answer me in the presence of the Lord and the Lord ’s anointed. Whose ox have I taken? Whose donkey have I taken? Whom have I cheated? Whom have I wronged? From whom have I accepted a bribe and shut my eyes because of it? I will make restitution to you.”
text.DRB: Speak of me before our Lord, and before his Christ, whether I have taken any man's oxe, or ass: If I have calumniated any man, if I have oppressed any man, if I have taken gift of any man's hand: and I will contemn that same this day, and will restore it to you.

text.NRSV: They said, “You have not defrauded us or oppressed us or taken anything from the hand of anyone.”
text.Rotherham: And they said, Thou hast not oppressed us, neither hast thou crushed us,—neither hast thou taken, at the hand of any man, any thing.
text.KJV: And they said, Thou hast not defrauded us, nor oppressed us, neither hast thou taken ought of any man’s hand.
text.NABRE: They replied, “You have neither cheated us, nor oppressed us, nor accepted anything from anyone.”
text.DRB: And they said thou hast not calumniated us, nor oppressed us, nor taken ought of any man's hand.

text.NRSV: He said to them, “The Lord is witness against you, and his anointed is witness this day, that you have not found anything in my hand.” And they said, “He is witness.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto them—Witness, is Yahweh against you, and, witness, is his Anointed, this day, that ye have not found in my hand, any thing! And they said: Witness!
text.KJV: And he said unto them, The Lord is witness against you, and his anointed is witness this day, that ye have not found ought in my hand. And they answered, He is witness.
text.NABRE: So he said to them, “The Lord is witness against you this day, and the Lord ’s anointed is witness, that you have found nothing in my possession.” “The Lord is witness,” they said.
text.DRB: And he said to them: Witness is our Lord against you, and witness is his Christ in this day, that you have not found any thing in my hand. And they said: Witness.

text.NRSV: Samuel said to the people, “The Lord is witness, who appointed Moses and Aaron and brought your ancestors up out of the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said unto the people: Witness, is Yahweh, who wrought with Moses and with Aaron, and who brought up your fathers out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And Samuel said unto the people, It is the Lord that advanced Moses and Aaron, and that brought your fathers up out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: Samuel Admonishes the People. Samuel continued: “The Lord is witness, who appointed Moses and Aaron and brought your ancestors up from the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And Samuel said to the people: Our Lord who made Moyses & Aaron, & brought our fathers out of the Land of Ægypt is present.

text.NRSV: Now therefore take your stand, so that I may enter into judgment with you before the Lord, and I will declare to you all the saving deeds of the Lord that he performed for you and for your ancestors.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, take your stand and let me plead with you, before Yahweh,—and tell you all the righteous acts of Yahweh, which he wrought with you, and with your fathers:
text.KJV: Now therefore stand still, that I may reason with you before the Lord of all the righteous acts of the Lord, which he did to you and to your fathers.
text.NABRE: Now take your stand, that I may judge you in the presence of the Lord according to all the gracious acts that the Lord has done for you and your ancestors.
text.DRB: Now therefore stand, that I may contend in judgment against you before our Lord, concerning all the mercies of our Lord, which he hath done with you, and with your fathers:

text.NRSV: When Jacob went into Egypt and the Egyptians oppressed them, then your ancestors cried to the Lord and the Lord sent Moses and Aaron, who brought forth your ancestors out of Egypt, and settled them in this place.
text.Rotherham: How that, when Jacob had come into Egypt,—and your fathers had made outcry unto Yahweh, then Yahweh sent Moses and Aaron, and they brought forth your fathers out of Egypt, and he caused them to dwell in this place;
text.KJV: When Jacob was come into Egypt, and your fathers cried unto the Lord, then the Lord sent Moses and Aaron, which brought forth your fathers out of Egypt, and made them dwell in this place.
text.NABRE: When Jacob and his sons went to Egypt and the Egyptians oppressed them, your ancestors cried out to the Lord. The Lord then sent Moses and Aaron to bring them out of Egypt and settled them in this place.
text.DRB: how Jacob entered into Ægypt, and your fathers cried to our Lord: and our Lord sent Moyses and Aaron, and brought your fathers out of Ægypt: and placed them in this place.

text.NRSV: But they forgot the Lord their God; and he sold them into the hand of Sisera, commander of the army of King Jabin of Hazor, and into the hand of the Philistines, and into the hand of the king of Moab; and they fought against them.
text.Rotherham: And, when they forgat Yahweh their God, he sold them into the hand of Sisera, prince of the host of Jabin, king of Hazor, and into the hand of the Philistines, and into the hand of the king of Moab, and they fought against them;
text.KJV: And when they forgat the Lord their God, he sold them into the hand of Sisera, captain of the host of Hazor, and into the hand of the Philistines, and into the hand of the king of Moab, and they fought against them.
text.NABRE: But they forgot the Lord their God; and so the Lord sold them into the power of Sisera, the captain of the army of Hazor, the power of the Philistines, and the power of the king of Moab, who made war against them.
text.DRB: Who forgat our Lord their God, and he delivered them in the hand of Sisara master of the host of Hasor, and in the hand of the Philisthiims, and in the hand of the king of Moab, and they fought against them.

text.NRSV: Then they cried to the Lord, and said, ‘We have sinned, because we have forsaken the Lord, and have served the Baals and the Astartes; but now rescue us out of the hand of our enemies, and we will serve you.’
text.Rotherham: But, when they made outcry unto Yahweh and said—We have sinned, in that we have forsaken Yahweh, and have served the Baals and the Ashtoreths,—now, therefore, deliver us out of the hand of our enemies, that we may serve thee,
text.KJV: And they cried unto the Lord, and said, We have sinned, because we have forsaken the Lord, and have served Baalim and Ashtaroth: but now deliver us out of the hand of our enemies, and we will serve thee.
text.NABRE: They cried out to the Lord and said, ‘We have sinned because we abandoned the Lord and served the Baals and Astartes. Now deliver us from the power of our enemies, and we will serve you.’
text.DRB: But afterward they cried to our Lord, & said: We have sinned, because we have forsaken our Lord, & have served Baalim & Astaroth: now therefore deliver us from the hand of our enemies, & we will serve thee.

text.NRSV: And the Lord sent Jerubbaal and Barak, and Jephthah, and Samson, and rescued you out of the hand of your enemies on every side; and you lived in safety.
text.Rotherham: then Yahweh sent Jerubbaal and Bedan, and Jephthah, and Samuel,—and delivered you out of the hand of your enemies, on every side, and ye dwelt in safety.
text.KJV: And the Lord sent Jerubbaal, and Bedan, and Jephthah, and Samuel, and delivered you out of the hand of your enemies on every side, and ye dwelled safe.
text.NABRE: The Lord sent Jerubbaal, Barak, Jephthah, and Samuel; he delivered you from the power of your enemies on every side, so that you could live in security.
text.DRB: And our Lord sent Jerobaal, & Badan, & Jephte, & Samuel, and delivered you from the hand of your enemies round about, & you dwelt securely.

text.NRSV: But when you saw that King Nahash of the Ammonites came against you, you said to me, ‘No, but a king shall reign over us,’ though the Lord your God was your king.
text.Rotherham: But, when ye saw that, Nahash king of the sons of Ammon, came upon you, then said ye unto me, Nay! but, a king, shall reign over us, when, Yahweh your God, was your king!
text.KJV: And when ye saw that Nahash the king of the children of Ammon came against you, ye said unto me, Nay; but a king shall reign over us: when the Lord your God was your king.
text.NABRE: Yet, when you saw Nahash, king of the Ammonites, advancing against you, you said to me, ‘No! A king must rule us,’ even though the Lord your God is your king.
text.DRB: But you seeing that Naas, king of the children of Ammon was come against you, you said to me: Not so, but a king shall reign over us: whereas our Lord your God did reign among you.

text.NRSV: See, here is the king whom you have chosen, for whom you have asked; see, the Lord has set a king over you.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, lo! the king whom ye have chosen, for whom ye have asked,—lo! therefore, Yahweh hath set over you a king.
text.KJV: Now therefore behold the king whom ye have chosen, and whom ye have desired! and, behold, the Lord hath set a king over you.
text.NABRE: Warnings for People and King. “Now here is the king you chose. See! The Lord has given you a king.
text.DRB: Now therefore your king is ready, whom you have chosen and desired: behold our Lord hath given you a king.

text.NRSV: If you will fear the Lord and serve him and heed his voice and not rebel against the commandment of the Lord, and if both you and the king who reigns over you will follow the Lord your God, it will be well;
text.Rotherham: If ye will revere Yahweh, and serve him, and hearken unto his voice, and not rebel against the bidding of Yahweh, then shall, both ye and your king that reigneth over you, continue to follow after Yahweh your God.
text.KJV: If ye will fear the Lord, and serve him, and obey his voice, and not rebel against the commandment of the Lord, then shall both ye and also the king that reigneth over you continue following the Lord your God:
text.NABRE: If you fear and serve the Lord, if you listen to the voice of the Lord and do not rebel against the Lord ’s command, if both you and the king, who rules over you, follow the Lord your God—well and good.
text.DRB: If you shall fear our Lord, and serve him, & hear his voice, and not exasperate the mouth of our Lord: both you, & the king which reigneth over you, shall be followers of our Lord your God:

text.NRSV: but if you will not heed the voice of the Lord, but rebel against the commandment of the Lord, then the hand of the Lord will be against you and your king.
text.Rotherham: But, if ye hearken not unto the voice of Yahweh, but rebel against the bidding of Yahweh, then will the hand of Yahweh continue to be against you, and against your fathers.
text.KJV: But if ye will not obey the voice of the Lord, but rebel against the commandment of the Lord, then shall the hand of the Lord be against you, as it was against your fathers.
text.NABRE: But if you do not listen to the voice of the Lord and if you rebel against the Lord ’s command, the hand of the Lord will be against you and your king.
text.DRB: But if you will not hear the voice of our Lord, but shall exasperate his words, the hand of our Lord shall be upon you, & upon your fathers.

text.NRSV: Now therefore take your stand and see this great thing that the Lord will do before your eyes.
text.Rotherham: Even now, stand still and see this great thing,—which Yahweh is about to do before your eyes:
text.KJV: Now therefore stand and see this great thing, which the Lord will do before your eyes.
text.NABRE: Now then, stand ready to witness the great marvel the Lord is about to accomplish before your eyes.
text.DRB: But now also stand, & see this great thing which our Lord will do in your sight.

text.NRSV: Is it not the wheat harvest today? I will call upon the Lord, that he may send thunder and rain; and you shall know and see that the wickedness that you have done in the sight of the Lord is great in demanding a king for yourselves.”
text.Rotherham: Is it not wheat harvest, to-day? I will cry unto Yahweh, that he may give forth thunderings and rain,—know ye then and see, that, your wrong, is great which ye have done in the sight of Yahweh, in asking for yourselves, a king.
text.KJV: Is it not wheat harvest to day? I will call unto the Lord, and he shall send thunder and rain; that ye may perceive and see that your wickedness is great, which ye have done in the sight of the Lord, in asking you a king.
text.NABRE: Are we not in the harvest time for wheat? Yet I will call upon the Lord, and he will send thunder and rain. Thus you will see and understand how great an evil it is in the eyes of the Lord that you have asked for a king.”
text.DRB: Is it not wheat harvest today? I will call upon our Lord, & he will give noises & rain: and you shall know, & see that you have done great evil to yourselves in the sight of our Lord, desiring a king over you.

text.NRSV: So Samuel called upon the Lord, and the Lord sent thunder and rain that day; and all the people greatly feared the Lord and Samuel.
text.Rotherham: So Samuel cried unto Yahweh, and Yahweh gave forth thunderings and rain, on that day,—and all the people greatly feared Yahweh, and Samuel.
text.KJV: So Samuel called unto the Lord; and the Lord sent thunder and rain that day: and all the people greatly feared the Lord and Samuel.
text.NABRE: Samuel called upon the Lord, and the Lord sent thunder and rain that day. Assistance Promised. Then all the people feared the Lord and Samuel.
text.DRB: And Samuel cried to our Lord, & our Lord gave noises & rain in that day.

text.NRSV: All the people said to Samuel, “Pray to the Lord your God for your servants, so that we may not die; for we have added to all our sins the evil of demanding a king for ourselves.”
text.Rotherham: And all the people said unto Samuel—Pray for thy servants, unto Yahweh thy God, and let it not be that we die,—For we have added, to all our sins, a wrong, in asking for ourselves a king.
text.KJV: And all the people said unto Samuel, Pray for thy servants unto the Lord thy God, that we die not: for we have added unto all our sins this evil, to ask us a king.
text.NABRE: They said to Samuel, “Pray to the Lord your God for us, your servants, that we may not die for having added to all our other sins the evil of asking for a king.”
text.DRB: And all the people feared exceedingly our Lord and Samuel. And all the people said to Samuel: Pray for thy servants to our Lord thy God, that we die not. For we have added evil to all our sins, that we desired unto us a king.

text.NRSV: And Samuel said to the people, “Do not be afraid; you have done all this evil, yet do not turn aside from following the Lord, but serve the Lord with all your heart;
text.Rotherham: Then said Samuel unto the people—Do not fear, ye, have done all this wrong,—nevertheless, do not turn aside from following Yahweh, but serve Yahweh, with all your heart;
text.KJV: And Samuel said unto the people, Fear not: ye have done all this wickedness: yet turn not aside from following the Lord, but serve the Lord with all your heart;
text.NABRE: “Do not fear,” Samuel answered them. “You have indeed committed all this evil! Yet do not turn from the Lord, but serve him with your whole heart.
text.DRB: And Samuel said to the people: Fear not, you have done all this evil: but yet depart not from the back of our Lord, but serve our Lord in all your heart.

text.NRSV: and do not turn aside after useless things that cannot profit or save, for they are useless.
text.Rotherham: and turn not aside after vanities, that can neither profit nor deliver, because, vanities, they are.
text.KJV: And turn ye not aside: for then should ye go after vain things, which cannot profit nor deliver; for they are vain.
text.NABRE: Do not turn aside to gods who are nothing, who cannot act and deliver. They are nothing.
text.DRB: And decline not after vain things, which shall not profit you, nor deliver you, because they are vain.

text.NRSV: For the Lord will not cast away his people, for his great name’s sake, because it has pleased the Lord to make you a people for himself.
text.Rotherham: For Yahweh will not give up his people, because of his great name, for Yahweh was minded to make you his people.
text.KJV: For the Lord will not forsake his people for his great name’s sake: because it hath pleased the Lord to make you his people.
text.NABRE: For the sake of his own great name the Lord will not abandon his people, since the Lord has decided to make you his people.
text.DRB: And our Lord will not forsake his people for his great name: because our Lord hath sworn to make you a people to himself.

text.NRSV: Moreover as for me, far be it from me that I should sin against the Lord by ceasing to pray for you; and I will instruct you in the good and the right way.
text.Rotherham: As for me also, far be it from me, that I should sin against Yahweh, by ceasing to pray for you,—but I will direct you, in the good and right way.
text.KJV: Moreover as for me, God forbid that I should sin against the Lord in ceasing to pray for you: but I will teach you the good and the right way:
text.NABRE: As for me, far be it from me to sin against the Lord by ceasing to pray for you and to teach you the good and right way.
text.DRB: And far from me be this sin in our Lord, that I should cease to pray for you, and I will teach you the good and right way.

text.NRSV: Only fear the Lord, and serve him faithfully with all your heart; for consider what great things he has done for you.
text.Rotherham: Only, revere Yahweh, and serve him in truth, with all your heart,—for see, what great things he hath done with you.
text.KJV: Only fear the Lord, and serve him in truth with all your heart: for consider how great things he hath done for you.
text.NABRE: But you must fear the Lord and serve him faithfully with all your heart, for you have seen the great things the Lord has done among you.
text.DRB: Therefore fear our Lord, and serve him in truth and from your whole heart. For you have seen the great works which he hath done among you.

text.NRSV: But if you still do wickedly, you shall be swept away, both you and your king.”
text.Rotherham: But, if ye, will do wrong, both ye and your king shall be swept away.
text.KJV: But if ye shall still do wickedly, ye shall be consumed, both ye and your king.
text.NABRE: If instead you continue to do evil, both you and your king shall be swept away.”
text.DRB: But if you shall persevere in malice, both you and your king shall perish together.

text.NRSV: Saul was thirty years old when he began to reign, and he reigned forty-two years over Israel.
text.Rotherham: Saul was [thirty] years old when he began to reign; and, when he had reigned two years over Israel,
text.KJV: Saul reigned one year; and when he had reigned two years over Israel,
text.NABRE: [Saul was…years old when he became king and he reigned…-two years over Israel.]
text.DRB: Achild of one year was Saul when he began to reign, and two years he reigned over Israel.

text.NRSV: Saul chose three thousand out of Israel; two thousand were with Saul in Michmash and the hill country of Bethel, and a thousand were with Jonathan in Gibeah of Benjamin; the rest of the people he sent home to their tents.
text.Rotherham: Saul chose him three thousand men out of Israel, of whom there were with Saul, two thousand in Michmash and in the hill-country of Bethel, and, a thousand, were with Jonathan, in Gibeah of Benjamin,—but, the rest of the people, he let go, every man to his own home.
text.KJV: Saul chose him three thousand men of Israel; whereof two thousand were with Saul in Michmash and in mount Beth–el, and a thousand were with Jonathan in Gibeah of Benjamin: and the rest of the people he sent every man to his tent.
text.NABRE: Saul Offers Sacrifice. Saul chose three thousand of Israel, of whom two thousand remained with him in Michmash and in the hill country of Bethel, and one thousand were with Jonathan in Gibeah of Benjamin. He sent the rest of the army back to their tents.
text.DRB: And Saul chose to himself three thousand of Israel: and there were with Saul two thousand in Machmas, and in the mount of Bethel: and a thousand with Jonathas in Gabaa of Benjamin. Moreover the rest of the people he sent back every man into their tabernacles:

text.NRSV: Jonathan defeated the garrison of the Philistines that was at Geba; and the Philistines heard of it. And Saul blew the trumpet throughout all the land, saying, “Let the Hebrews hear!”
text.Rotherham: Then did Jonathan smite the garrison of Philistines that was in Geba, and the Philistines heard of it,—and, Saul, blew with a horn throughout all the land saying, Let the Hebrews hear!
text.KJV: And Jonathan smote the garrison of the Philistines that was in Geba, and the Philistines heard of it. And Saul blew the trumpet throughout all the land, saying, Let the Hebrews hear.
text.NABRE: Now Jonathan struck the Philistine garrison in Gibeah, and the Philistines got word of it. Then Saul sounded the horn throughout the land, saying, “Let the Hebrews hear!”
text.DRB: And Jonathas struck the garrison of the Philisthiims, which was in Gabaa. Which when the Philisthiims had heard, Saul sounded with the trumpet in all the land, saying: Let the Hebrews hear.

text.NRSV: When all Israel heard that Saul had defeated the garrison of the Philistines, and also that Israel had become odious to the Philistines, the people were called out to join Saul at Gilgal.
text.Rotherham: And, all Israel, heard say—Saul hath smitten the garrison of the Philistines, Moreover, also, Israel have made themselves odious among the Philistines,—so the people were called together to follow Saul, to Gilgal.
text.KJV: And all Israel heard say that Saul had smitten a garrison of the Philistines, and that Israel also was had in abomination with the Philistines. And the people were called together after Saul to Gilgal.
text.NABRE: Then all Israel heard the report, “Saul has struck the garrison of the Philistines! Israel has become odious to the Philistines!” Then the army was called up to Saul in Gilgal.
text.DRB: And all Israel heard this manner of bruit: Saul hath strucken the garrison of the Philisthiims: & Israel took courage against the Philisthiims. The people therefore cried after Saul in Galgal.

text.NRSV: The Philistines mustered to fight with Israel, thirty thousand chariots, and six thousand horsemen, and troops like the sand on the seashore in multitude; they came up and encamped at Michmash, to the east of Beth-aven.
text.Rotherham: And, the Philistines, gathered themselves together to fight with Israel, thirty thousand chariots, and six thousand horsemen, a people also like the sand that is on the sea-shore for multitude,—and they came up, and encamped in Michmash, east of Beth-aven.
text.KJV: And the Philistines gathered themselves together to fight with Israel, thirty thousand chariots, and six thousand horsemen, and people as the sand which is on the sea shore in multitude: and they came up, and pitched in Michmash, eastward from Beth–aven.
text.NABRE: The Philistines also assembled for battle against Israel, with thirty thousand chariots, six thousand horsemen, and foot soldiers as numerous as the sand on the seashore. They came up and encamped in Michmash, east of Beth-aven.
text.DRB: And the Philisthiims were gathered together to fight against Israel, thirty thousand chariots, and six thousand horsemen, and the rest of the common people, as the sand which is in the sea shore very much. And going up they camped in Machmas at the East of Bethaven.

text.NRSV: When the Israelites saw that they were in distress (for the troops were hard pressed), the people hid themselves in caves and in holes and in rocks and in tombs and in cisterns.
text.Rotherham: So, the men of Israel, saw they were in a strait, for the people had been harassed,—and the people had hidden themselves in caves, and in thickets, and among cliffs, and in holes, and in pits.
text.KJV: When the men of Israel saw that they were in a strait, (for the people were distressed,) then the people did hide themselves in caves, and in thickets, and in rocks, and in high places, and in pits.
text.NABRE: When the soldiers saw they were in danger because the army was hardpressed, they hid themselves in caves, thickets, rocks, caverns, and cisterns.
text.DRB: Which when the men of Israel had seen themselves put in a strait (for the people was afflicted) they hid themselves in caves, and in secret places, in rocks also, and in dens, and in cisterns.

text.NRSV: Some Hebrews crossed the Jordan to the land of Gad and Gilead. Saul was still at Gilgal, and all the people followed him trembling.
text.Rotherham: And, Hebrews, had passed over the Jordan, to the land of Gad, and Gilead,—but, Saul, was yet in Gilgal, and all the people, trembled after him.
