text.KJV: And some of the Hebrews went over Jordan to the land of Gad and Gilead. As for Saul, he was yet in Gilgal, and all the people followed him trembling.
text.NABRE: Other Hebrews crossed the Jordan into the land of Gad and Gilead. Saul, however, held out in Gilgal, all his army trembling in fear behind him.
text.DRB: And the Hebrews passed Jordan into the Land of Gad and Galaad. And when Saul was yet in Galgal, all the people was sore afraid, which followed him.

text.NRSV: He waited seven days, the time appointed by Samuel; but Samuel did not come to Gilgal, and the people began to slip away from Saul.
text.Rotherham: And he waited seven days, by the set time that Samuel had named, but Samuel came not to Gilgal,—and the people were scattered from him.
text.KJV: And he tarried seven days, according to the set time that Samuel had appointed: but Samuel came not to Gilgal; and the people were scattered from him.
text.NABRE: He waited seven days, until the appointed time Samuel had set, but Samuel did not come, and the army deserted Saul.
text.DRB: And he expected seven days according to the appointment of Samuel, and Samuel came not into Galgal, and the people slipt away from him.

text.NRSV: So Saul said, “Bring the burnt offering here to me, and the offerings of well-being.” And he offered the burnt offering.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul, Bring near unto me, the ascending-sacrifice and the peace-offerings. And he offered up the ascending-sacrifice.
text.KJV: And Saul said, Bring hither a burnt offering to me, and peace offerings. And he offered the burnt offering.
text.NABRE: He then said, “Bring me the burnt offering and communion offerings!” Then he sacrificed the burnt offering.
text.DRB: Saul therefore said: Bring me the holocaust, and the pacifics. And he offered the holocaust.

text.NRSV: As soon as he had finished offering the burnt offering, Samuel arrived; and Saul went out to meet him and salute him.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as he had made an end of offering up the ascending-sacrifice, lo! Samuel, had come,—and Saul went out to meet him, that he might bless him.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, that as soon as he had made an end of offering the burnt offering, behold, Samuel came; and Saul went out to meet him, that he might salute him.
text.NABRE: King Saul Reproved. As he finished sacrificing the burnt offering, there came Samuel! So Saul went out toward him in order to greet him.
text.DRB: And when he had finished offering the holocaust, behold Samuel came: and Saul went forth to meet him & salute him.

text.NRSV: Samuel said, “What have you done?” Saul replied, “When I saw that the people were slipping away from me, and that you did not come within the days appointed, and that the Philistines were mustering at Michmash,
text.Rotherham: Then said Samuel—What hast thou done? And Saul said—Because I saw that the people had been scattered from me, and, thou, hadst not come within the appointed days, and, the Philistines, had gathered themselves together to Michmash,
text.KJV: And Samuel said, What hast thou done? And Saul said, Because I saw that the people were scattered from me, and that thou camest not within the days appointed, and that the Philistines gathered themselves together at Michmash;
text.NABRE: Samuel asked him, “What have you done?” Saul explained: “When I saw that the army was deserting me and you did not come on the appointed day, and that the Philistines were assembling at Michmash,
text.DRB: And Samuel spake to him. What hast thou done? Saul answered: Because I saw that the people slipt from me, and thou wast not come according to the days appointed, moreover the Philisthiims were gathered together into Machmas,

text.NRSV: I said, ‘Now the Philistines will come down upon me at Gilgal, and I have not entreated the favor of the Lord’; so I forced myself, and offered the burnt offering.”
text.Rotherham: therefore I said—Now, will the Philistines come down against me, at Gilgal, but, the face of Yahweh, have I not appeased: So I forced myself, and offered up the ascending-sacrifice.
text.KJV: Therefore said I, The Philistines will come down now upon me to Gilgal, and I have not made supplication unto the Lord: I forced myself therefore, and offered a burnt offering.
text.NABRE: I said to myself, ‘Now the Philistines will come down against me at Gilgal, and I have not yet sought the Lord ’s blessing.’ So I thought I should sacrifice the burnt offering.”
text.DRB: Isaid: Now will the Philisthiims come down to me into Galgal, and I have not pacified the face of our Lord. Compelled by necessity, I offered the holocaust.

text.NRSV: Samuel said to Saul, “You have done foolishly; you have not kept the commandment of the Lord your God, which he commanded you. The Lord would have established your kingdom over Israel forever,
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said unto Saul—Thou hast shewn thyself foolish,—thou hast not kept the commandment of Yahweh thy God, which he commanded thee, for, now, would Yahweh have established thy kingdom unto Israel until times age-abiding;
text.KJV: And Samuel said to Saul, Thou hast done foolishly: thou hast not kept the commandment of the Lord thy God, which he commanded thee: for now would the Lord have established thy kingdom upon Israel for ever.
text.NABRE: Samuel replied to Saul: “You have acted foolishly! Had you kept the command the Lord your God gave you, the Lord would now establish your kingship in Israel forever;
text.DRB: And Samuel said to Saul: Thou hast done foolishly, neither hast thou kept the commandments of our Lord thy God, which he commanded thee. Which if thou hadst not done, even now had our Lord prepared thy Kingdom over Israel for ever,

text.NRSV: but now your kingdom will not continue; the Lord has sought out a man after his own heart; and the Lord has appointed him to be ruler over his people, because you have not kept what the Lord commanded you.”
text.Rotherham: Whereas, now, shall thy kingdom not be established,—Yahweh hath sought out for him a man after his own heart, and Yahweh hath commanded him to be leader over his people, because thou hast not kept that which Yahweh commanded thee.
text.KJV: But now thy kingdom shall not continue: the Lord hath sought him a man after his own heart, and the Lord hath commanded him to be captain over his people, because thou hast not kept that which the Lord commanded thee.
text.NABRE: but now your kingship shall not endure. The Lord has sought out a man after his own heart to appoint as ruler over his people because you did not observe what the Lord commanded you.”
text.DRB: but thy Kingdom shall no farther arise. Our Lord hath sought him a man according to his heart: and him hath our Lord commanded to be Prince over his people, because thou hast not observed the things which our Lord commanded.

text.NRSV: And Samuel left and went on his way from Gilgal. The rest of the people followed Saul to join the army; they went up from Gilgal toward Gibeah of Benjamin. Saul counted the people who were present with him, about six hundred men.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel arose, and ascended from Gilgal unto Gibeah of Benjamin. And Saul numbered the people who were found with him, about six hundred men.
text.KJV: And Samuel arose, and gat him up from Gilgal unto Gibeah of Benjamin. And Saul numbered the people that were present with him, about six hundred men.
text.NABRE: Philistine Invasion. Then Samuel set out from Gilgal and went his own way; but what was left of the army went up after Saul to meet the soldiers, going from Gilgal to Gibeah of Benjamin. Saul then counted the soldiers he had with him, about six hundred.
text.DRB: And Samuel arose & went up from Galgal into Gabaa of Benjamin. And numbered the people, which were found with him, as it were six hundred men.

text.NRSV: Saul, his son Jonathan, and the people who were present with them stayed in Geba of Benjamin; but the Philistines encamped at Michmash.
text.Rotherham: Now, Saul, and Jonathan his son, and the people that were found with him, were abiding in Geba of Benjamin,—but, the Philistines, had encamped in Michmash.
text.KJV: And Saul, and Jonathan his son, and the people that were present with them, abode in Gibeah of Benjamin: but the Philistines encamped in Michmash.
text.NABRE: Saul, his son Jonathan, and the soldiers they had with them were now occupying Geba of Benjamin, and the Philistines were encamped at Michmash.
text.DRB: And Saul and Jonathas his son, and the people that were found with them, was in Gabaa of Benjamin: moreover the Philisthiims had pitched in Machmas.

text.NRSV: And raiders came out of the camp of the Philistines in three companies; one company turned toward Ophrah, to the land of Shual,
text.Rotherham: And the spoilers came forth out of the camp of the Philistines, in three companies, the first company, turned unto the way of Ophrah, unto the land of Shual;
text.KJV: And the spoilers came out of the camp of the Philistines in three companies: one company turned unto the way that leadeth to Ophrah, unto the land of Shual:
text.NABRE: Meanwhile, raiders left the camp of the Philistines in three bands. One band took the Ophrah road toward the district of Shual;
text.DRB: And there issued forth to prey from the camp of Philisthians three companies. One company went on against the way of Ephra to the Land of Saul.

text.NRSV: another company turned toward Beth-horon, and another company turned toward the mountain that looks down upon the valley of Zeboim toward the wilderness.
text.Rotherham: and, the second company, turned unto the way of Beth-horon,—and, the third company, turned unto the way of the boundary that overlooketh the valley of Zeboim, towards the wilderness.
text.KJV: And another company turned the way to Beth–horon: and another company turned to the way of the border that looketh to the valley of Zeboim toward the wilderness.
text.NABRE: another turned in the direction of Beth-horon; and the third took the road for Geba that overlooks the Valley of the Hyenas toward the desert. Disarmament of Israel.
text.DRB: Moreover another went by the way of Bethhoron, and the third had turned it-self to the way of the border, in the valley Seboim against the desert.

text.NRSV: Now there was no smith to be found throughout all the land of Israel; for the Philistines said, “The Hebrews must not make swords or spears for themselves”;
text.Rotherham: Now, a smith, could not be found, throughout all the land of Israel—for the Philistines had said, Lest the Hebrews make sword or spear.
text.KJV: Now there was no smith found throughout all the land of Israel: for the Philistines said, Lest the Hebrews make them swords or spears:
text.NABRE: Not a single smith was to be found anywhere in Israel, for the Philistines had said, “Otherwise the Hebrews will make swords or spears.”
text.DRB: Moreover there was not found an iron smith in all the Land of Israel, for the Philisthiims had so provided, lest perhaps the Hebrews should make sword or spear.

text.NRSV: so all the Israelites went down to the Philistines to sharpen their plowshares, mattocks, axes, or sickles;
text.Rotherham: So all Israel went down to the Philistines,—to sharpen every man his share, and his mattock, and his axe, and his sickle.
text.KJV: But all the Israelites went down to the Philistines, to sharpen every man his share, and his coulter, and his axe, and his mattock.
text.NABRE: All Israel, therefore, had to go down to the Philistines to sharpen their plowshares, mattocks, axes, and sickles.
text.DRB: All Israel therefore went down to the Philisthiims, that every man might whet his plough coulter, and spade, and axe, and rake.

text.NRSV: The charge was two-thirds of a shekel for the plowshares and for the mattocks, and one-third of a shekel for sharpening the axes and for setting the goads.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit they had a file for the sickles, and the mattocks, and the three-pronged forks, and the axes,—and for setting the goads.
text.KJV: Yet they had a file for the mattocks, and for the coulters, and for the forks, and for the axes, and to sharpen the goads.
text.NABRE: The price for the plowshares and mattocks was two thirds of a shekel, and a third of a shekel for sharpening the axes and for setting the ox-goads.
text.DRB: Therefore the edges of the shares, and spades, and forks with three teeth, and axes were blunt, even to the goadprick, which was to be mended.

text.NRSV: So on the day of the battle neither sword nor spear was to be found in the possession of any of the people with Saul and Jonathan; but Saul and his son Jonathan had them.
text.Rotherham: Thus would it come to pass, in the day of battle, that there was found—neither sword nor spear, in the hand of any of the people, that were with Saul and Jonathan, but such were found belonging to Saul and to Jonathan his son.
text.KJV: So it came to pass in the day of battle, that there was neither sword nor spear found in the hand of any of the people that were with Saul and Jonathan: but with Saul and with Jonathan his son was there found.
text.NABRE: And so on the day of battle neither sword nor spear could be found in the hand of any of the soldiers with Saul or Jonathan. Only Saul and his son Jonathan had them.
text.DRB: And when the day was come to fight, there was not found sword and spear in the hand of all the people, that was with Saul and Jonathas, except Saul and Jonathas his son.

text.NRSV: Now a garrison of the Philistines had gone out to the pass of Michmash.
text.Rotherham: Now the garrison of the Philistines had come out to the pass of Michmash.
text.KJV: And the garrison of the Philistines went out to the passage of Michmash.
text.NABRE: Jonathan’s Exploit. An outpost of the Philistines had pushed forward to the pass of Michmash.
text.DRB: And the station of the Philisthiims went forth, to pass up into Machmas.

text.NRSV: One day Jonathan son of Saul said to the young man who carried his armor, “Come, let us go over to the Philistine garrison on the other side.” But he did not tell his father.
text.Rotherham: And it came about, on a certain day, that Jonathan son of Saul said unto the young man bearing his armour: Come! and let us pass over unto the garrison of the Philistines, that is on the other side, yonder! but, to his father, he told it not.
text.KJV: Now it came to pass upon a day, that Jonathan the son of Saul said unto the young man that bare his armour, Come, and let us go over to the Philistines’ garrison, that is on the other side. But he told not his father.
text.NABRE: One day Jonathan, son of Saul, said to his armor-bearer, “Come, let us go over to the Philistine outpost on the other side.” But he did not inform his father—
text.DRB: And it chanced on a certain day, that Jonathas the son of Saul said to the young man that bare his armour: Come and Let us pass to the garrison of the Philisthiims, which is beyond yonder place. But to his father he told not this same thing.

text.NRSV: Saul was staying in the outskirts of Gibeah under the pomegranate tree that is at Migron; the troops that were with him were about six hundred men,
text.Rotherham: Now, Saul, was tarrying in the uttermost part of Gibeah, under the pomegranate tree that is in Migron,—and, the people that were with him, were about six hundred men;
text.KJV: And Saul tarried in the uttermost part of Gibeah under a pomegranate tree which is in Migron: and the people that were with him were about six hundred men;
text.NABRE: Saul was sitting under the pomegranate tree in Migron on the outskirts of Gibeah; with him were about six hundred men.
text.DRB: Moreover Saul abode in the utmost part of Gabaa under the pomegranate tree, which was in Magron: and the people with him was about six hundred men.

text.NRSV: along with Ahijah son of Ahitub, Ichabod’s brother, son of Phinehas son of Eli, the priest of the Lord in Shiloh, carrying an ephod. Now the people did not know that Jonathan had gone.
text.Rotherham: and, Ahijah, son of Ahitub, brother of Ichabod, son of Phinehas, son of Eli, was priest of Yahweh in Shiloh, wearing an ephod,—and, the people, knew not that Jonathan had departed.
text.KJV: And Ahiah, the son of Ahitub, I–chabod’s brother, the son of Phinehas, the son of Eli, the Lord’s priest in Shiloh, wearing an ephod. And the people knew not that Jonathan was gone.
text.NABRE: Ahijah, son of Ahitub, brother of Ichabod, the son of Phinehas, son of Eli, the priest of the Lord at Shiloh, was wearing the ephod—nor did the soldiers know that Jonathan had gone.
text.DRB: And Achias the son of Achitob the brother of Ichabod the son of Phinees, which was born of Heli the Priest of our Lord in Silo, bare the ephod. But the people also was ignorant whither Jonathas was gone.

text.NRSV: In the pass, by which Jonathan tried to go over to the Philistine garrison, there was a rocky crag on one side and a rocky crag on the other; the name of the one was Bozez, and the name of the other Seneh.
text.Rotherham: And, between the passes by which Jonathan sought to cross over up to the garrison of the Philistines, there was a crag of rock on the one side, and a crag of rock on the other side; and, the name of the one, was Bozez, and the name of the other, Seneh.
text.KJV: And between the passages, by which Jonathan sought to go over unto the Philistines’ garrison, there was a sharp rock on the one side, and a sharp rock on the other side: and the name of the one was Bozez, and the name of the other Seneh.
text.NABRE: Flanking the ravine through which Jonathan intended to cross to the Philistine outpost were rocky crags on each side, one named Bozez and the other Seneh.
text.DRB: And there were between the ascents, by the which Jonathas endeavoured to pass unto the garrison of the Philisthiims, rocks standing up on both sides, and as it were in manner of teeth steep broken rocks on either side, the name of one Boses, and the name of the other Sene:

text.NRSV: One crag rose on the north in front of Michmash, and the other on the south in front of Geba.
text.Rotherham: The one crag, was a pillar on the north, over against Michmash,—and, the other, on the south, over against Geba.
text.KJV: The forefront of the one was situate northward over against Michmash, and the other southward over against Gibeah.
text.NABRE: One crag was to the north, toward Michmash; the other to the south, toward Geba.
text.DRB: one rock standing out toward the North over-against Machmas, and the other to the South, against Gabaa.

text.NRSV: Jonathan said to the young man who carried his armor, “Come, let us go over to the garrison of these uncircumcised; it may be that the Lord will act for us; for nothing can hinder the Lord from saving by many or by few.”
text.Rotherham: So Jonathan said unto the young man bearing his armour—Come! and let us cross over unto the garrison of these uncircumcised, peradventure, Yahweh may work by us,—for there is no restraint with Yahweh, to save by many, or by few.
text.KJV: And Jonathan said to the young man that bare his armour, Come, and let us go over unto the garrison of these uncircumcised: it may be that the Lord will work for us: for there is no restraint to the Lord to save by many or by few.
text.NABRE: Jonathan said to his armor-bearer: “Come, let us go over to that outpost of the uncircumcised. Perhaps the Lord will help us, because it is no more difficult for the Lord to grant victory by means of a few than it is by means of many.”
text.DRB: And Jonathas said to the young man that bare his armour: Come, let us pass to the station of these uncircumcised, if happily our Lord will make for us: because it is not hard for our Lord to save either in many or in few.

text.NRSV: His armor-bearer said to him, “Do all that your mind inclines to. I am with you; as your mind is, so is mine.”
text.Rotherham: And his armour-bearer said to him, Do all that is in thy heart,—decide for thyself (lo! I am with thee!), according to thine own heart.
text.KJV: And his armourbearer said unto him, Do all that is in thine heart: turn thee; behold, I am with thee according to thy heart.
text.NABRE: His armor-bearer replied, “Do whatever you think best; I am with you in whatever you decide.”
text.DRB: And his esquire said to him: Do all things which please thy mind: go whither thou desirest, and I will be with thee wheresoever thou wilt.

text.NRSV: Then Jonathan said, “Now we will cross over to those men and will show ourselves to them.
text.Rotherham: Then said Jonathan, Lo! we, are crossing over unto the men,—and will discover ourselves unto them.
text.KJV: Then said Jonathan, Behold, we will pass over unto these men, and we will discover ourselves unto them.
text.NABRE: Jonathan continued: “When we cross over to those men, we will be visible to them.
text.DRB: And Jonathas said: Behold we pass to these men. And when we shall appear to them,

text.NRSV: If they say to us, ‘Wait until we come to you,’ then we will stand still in our place, and we will not go up to them.
text.Rotherham: If, thus, they say unto us, Keep quiet until we get to you, then will we stand still where we are, and will not go up unto them;
text.KJV: If they say thus unto us, Tarry until we come to you; then we will stand still in our place, and will not go up unto them.
text.NABRE: If they say to us, ‘Stay there until we can come to you,’ we will stop where we are; we will not go up to them.
text.DRB: if they shall speak to us in this manner: Tarry till we come to you: let us stand in our place, & not go up to them.

text.NRSV: But if they say, ‘Come up to us,’ then we will go up; for the Lord has given them into our hand. That will be the sign for us.”
text.Rotherham: but, if thus, they say, Come up unto us, then will we go up; for Yahweh hath delivered them into our hand,—This, then, is our sign.
text.KJV: But if they say thus, Come up unto us; then we will go up: for the Lord hath delivered them into our hand: and this shall be a sign unto us.
text.NABRE: But if they say, ‘Come up to us,’ we will go up, because the Lord has delivered them into our hand. That will be our sign.”
text.DRB: But if they shall say: Come up to us: let us go up, because our Lord hath delivered them in our hands, this shall be a sign unto us.

text.NRSV: So both of them showed themselves to the garrison of the Philistines; and the Philistines said, “Look, Hebrews are coming out of the holes where they have hidden themselves.”
text.Rotherham: So they two discovered themselves unto the garrison of the Philistines,—and the Philistines said—Lo! Hebrews, coming forth out of the holes, wherein they had hidden themselves.
text.KJV: And both of them discovered themselves unto the garrison of the Philistines: and the Philistines said, Behold, the Hebrews come forth out of the holes where they had hid themselves.
text.NABRE: When the two of them came into the view of the Philistine outpost, the Philistines remarked, “Look, some Hebrews are coming out of the holes where they have been hiding.”
text.DRB: Both of them therefore appeared to the station of the Philisthiims: and the Philisthiims said: Behold the Hebrews come out of the caves, wherein they were hid.

text.NRSV: The men of the garrison hailed Jonathan and his armor-bearer, saying, “Come up to us, and we will show you something.” Jonathan said to his armor-bearer, “Come up after me; for the Lord has given them into the hand of Israel.”
text.Rotherham: And the men of the garrison responded to Jonathan and his armour-bearer, and said—Come up unto us, and we will let you know a thing. Then said Jonathan unto his armour-bearer—Come up after me, for Yahweh hath delivered them into the hand of Israel.
text.KJV: And the men of the garrison answered Jonathan and his armourbearer, and said, Come up to us, and we will shew you a thing. And Jonathan said unto his armourbearer, Come up after me: for the Lord hath delivered them into the hand of Israel.
text.NABRE: The men of the outpost called to Jonathan and his armor-bearer. “Come up here,” they said, “and we will teach you a lesson.” So Jonathan said to his armor-bearer, “Climb up after me, for the Lord has delivered them into the hand of Israel.”
text.DRB: And the men of the garrison spake to Jonathas and to his esquire, and said: Come up to us, and we will shew you a thing. And Jonathas said to his esquire: Let us go up, follow me: for our Lord hath delivered them into the hands of Israel.

text.NRSV: Then Jonathan climbed up on his hands and feet, with his armor-bearer following after him. The Philistines fell before Jonathan, and his armor-bearer, coming after him, killed them.
text.Rotherham: So Jonathan went up on his hands and on his feet, and, his armour-bearer, followed him,—and they fell before Jonathan, and, his armour-bearer, was slaying after him.
text.KJV: And Jonathan climbed up upon his hands and upon his feet, and his armourbearer after him: and they fell before Jonathan; and his armourbearer slew after him.
text.NABRE: Jonathan clambered up with his armor-bearer behind him. As the Philistines fell before Jonathan, his armor-bearer, who followed him, would finish them off.
text.DRB: And Jonathas went up on his hands and feet creeping, and his esquire after him. Therefore some fell before Jonathas, other some his esquire following slew.

text.NRSV: In that first slaughter Jonathan and his armor-bearer killed about twenty men within an area about half a furrow long in an acre of land.
text.Rotherham: And the first smiting wherewith Jonathan and his armour-bearer smote, was about twenty men,—within, as it were, half a furrow’s length of a yoke of land.
text.KJV: And that first slaughter, which Jonathan and his armourbearer made, was about twenty men, within as it were an half acre of land, which a yoke of oxen might plow.
text.NABRE: In this first attack Jonathan and his armor-bearer killed about twenty men within half a furlong.
text.DRB: And the first slaughter, which Jonathas and his esquire made, was as it were of twenty men, in the half part of an acre, which a yoke of oxen is wont to plough in a day.

text.NRSV: There was a panic in the camp, in the field, and among all the people; the garrison and even the raiders trembled; the earth quaked; and it became a very great panic.
text.Rotherham: And there came to be a trembling in the camp, in the field, and among all the people, the outposts and the spoilers, they too, trembled,—and the earth quaked, so it became a preternatural trembling.
text.KJV: And there was trembling in the host, in the field, and among all the people: the garrison, and the spoilers, they also trembled, and the earth quaked: so it was a very great trembling.
text.NABRE: Then terror spread through the camp and the countryside; all the soldiers in the outpost and in the raiding parties shuddered in terror. The earth shook with an awesome shuddering.
text.DRB: And there was made a miracle in the camp, through the fields: yea and all the people of their garrison which had gone to take preys, was astonished, and the land was troubled: and it happened as a miracle from God.

text.NRSV: Saul’s sentinels in Gibeah of Benjamin were watching as the multitude was surging back and forth.
text.Rotherham: And the scouts of Saul in Gibeah of Benjamin looked, and lo! the camp, melted away, hither and thither.
text.KJV: And the watchmen of Saul in Gibeah of Benjamin looked; and, behold, the multitude melted away, and they went on beating down one another.
text.NABRE: Rout of the Philistines. Saul’s sentinels in Gibeah of Benjamin saw that the enemy camp had scattered and were running in all directions.
text.DRB: And the watchmen of Saul, which were in Gabaa of Benjamin, looked, and lo a multitude overthrown, and flying hither and thither.

text.NRSV: Then Saul said to the troops that were with him, “Call the roll and see who has gone from us.” When they had called the roll, Jonathan and his armor-bearer were not there.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul unto the people who were with him: Number, I pray you, and see who hath departed from us. So they numbered; and lo! Jonathan and his armour-bearer were missing.
text.KJV: Then said Saul unto the people that were with him, Number now, and see who is gone from us. And when they had numbered, behold, Jonathan and his armourbearer were not there.
text.NABRE: Saul said to those around him, “Count the troops and find out if any of us are missing.” When they had taken the count, they found Jonathan and his armor-bearer missing.
text.DRB: And Saul said to the people, which was with him: Inquire, and see who is gone from us. And when they had sought, it was found that Jonathas was not present and his esquire.

text.NRSV: Saul said to Ahijah, “Bring the ark of God here.” For at that time the ark of God went with the Israelites.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul unto Ahijah, Bring near the ark of God; for the ark of God was, on that day, in the midst of the sons of Israel.
text.KJV: And Saul said unto Ahiah, Bring hither the ark of God. For the ark of God was at that time with the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: Saul then said to Ahijah, “Bring the ephod here.” (Ahijah was wearing the ephod before the Israelites at that time.)
text.DRB: And Saul said to Achias: Bring the ark of our Lord. (for the ark of God was there that day with the children of Israel.)

text.NRSV: While Saul was talking to the priest, the tumult in the camp of the Philistines increased more and more; and Saul said to the priest, “Withdraw your hand.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, as soon as Saul had spoken unto the priest, that, the tumultuous noise that was in the camp of the Philistines, went on and on, increasing. Then said Saul unto the priest,—Withdraw thy hand.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, while Saul talked unto the priest, that the noise that was in the host of the Philistines went on and increased: and Saul said unto the priest, Withdraw thine hand.
text.NABRE: While Saul was speaking to the priest, the uproar in the Philistine camp kept increasing. So he said to the priest, “Withdraw your hand.”
text.DRB: And when Saul spake to the Priest, there arose a great tumult in the camp of the Philisthiims: and it grew by little and little, and sounded more clearly. And Saul said to the Priest: Draw together thy hand.

text.NRSV: Then Saul and all the people who were with him rallied and went into the battle; and every sword was against the other, so that there was very great confusion.
text.Rotherham: And Saul and all the people that were with him gathered themselves together, and came as far as the host,—and lo! the sword of every man was against his fellow, an exceeding great confusion.
text.KJV: And Saul and all the people that were with him assembled themselves, and they came to the battle: and, behold, every man’s sword was against his fellow, and there was a very great discomfiture.
text.NABRE: And Saul and all his men rallied and rushed into the fight, where the Philistines, wholly confused, were thrusting swords at one another.
text.DRB: Saul therefore and all the people that was with him shouted together, and they came to the place of the fight: and behold every man's sword had been turned to his neighbour, and a slaughter exceeding great.

text.NRSV: Now the Hebrews who previously had been with the Philistines and had gone up with them into the camp turned and joined the Israelites who were with Saul and Jonathan.
text.Rotherham: And, the Hebrews who had aforetime belonged to the Philistines, who had come up with them in the host, even they, turned round so as to be with Israel who were with Saul and Jonathan.
text.KJV: Moreover the Hebrews that were with the Philistines before that time, which went up with them into the camp from the country round about, even they also turned to be with the Israelites that were with Saul and Jonathan.
text.NABRE: The Hebrews who had previously sided with the Philistines and had gone up with them to their camp turned to join the Israelites under Saul and Jonathan.
text.DRB: But the Hebrews also which had been with the Philisthiims yesterday and the day before, and went up with them in the camp, returned to be with Israel, which were with Saul and Jonathas.

text.NRSV: Likewise, when all the Israelites who had gone into hiding in the hill country of Ephraim heard that the Philistines were fleeing, they too followed closely after them in the battle.
text.Rotherham: And, all the men of Israel who had hidden themselves throughout the hill country of Ephraim, heard that the Philistines had fled, and, they also, followed hard after them in the battle.
text.KJV: Likewise all the men of Israel which had hid themselves in mount Ephraim, when they heard that the Philistines fled, even they also followed hard after them in the battle.
text.NABRE: Likewise, all the Israelites who were hiding in the hill country of Ephraim, hearing that the Philistines were fleeing, kept after them in the battle.
text.DRB: All the Israelites also which had hid themselves in mount Ephraim, hearing that the Philisthiims were fled, joined themselves with their fellows in battle. And there were with Saul as it were ten thousand men.

text.NRSV: So the Lord gave Israel the victory that day. The battle passed beyond Beth-aven, and the troops with Saul numbered altogether about ten thousand men. The battle spread out over the hill country of Ephraim.
text.Rotherham: Thus did Yahweh, on that day, save Israel,—and, the battle, passed over by Beth-aven.
text.KJV: So the Lord saved Israel that day: and the battle passed over unto Beth–aven.
text.NABRE: Thus the Lord saved Israel that day. Saul’s Oath. The battle continued past Beth-aven.
text.DRB: And our Lord in that day saved Israel. And the fight reached as far as Bethaven.

text.NRSV: Now Saul committed a very rash act on that day. He had laid an oath on the troops, saying, “Cursed be anyone who eats food before it is evening and I have been avenged on my enemies.” So none of the troops tasted food.
text.Rotherham: Now, the men of Israel, were tired out on that day,—yet had Saul bound the people by an oath, saying—Cursed, be the man that eateth food until the evening, and I be avenged upon mine enemies. So none of the people had tasted food.
text.KJV: And the men of Israel were distressed that day: for Saul had adjured the people, saying, Cursed be the man that eateth any food until evening, that I may be avenged on mine enemies. So none of the people tasted any food.
text.NABRE: Even though the Israelites were exhausted that day, Saul laid an oath on them, saying, “Cursed be the one who takes food before evening, before I am able to avenge myself on my enemies.” So none of the people tasted food.
text.DRB: And the men of Israel were joined among themselves in that day: and Saul adjured the people, saying: Cursed be the man that shall eat bread until evening, till I be revenged of mine enemies. And the whole people did eat no bread:

text.NRSV: All the troops came upon a honeycomb; and there was honey on the ground.
text.Rotherham: And, all the land, had entered into the forest,—and there was honey upon the face of the ground.
text.KJV: And all they of the land came to a wood; and there was honey upon the ground.
text.NABRE: Now there was a honeycomb lying on the ground,
text.DRB: and all the common people of the land came into a forest, wherein was honey upon the face of the field.

text.NRSV: When the troops came upon the honeycomb, the honey was flowing; but they did not put their hands to their mouths, for they feared the oath.
text.Rotherham: So the people came into the forest, and lo! there were streams of honey,—but no man reached his hand to his mouth, because the people revered the oath.
text.KJV: And when the people were come into the wood, behold, the honey dropped; but no man put his hand to his mouth: for the people feared the oath.
text.NABRE: and when the soldiers came to the comb the honey was flowing; yet no one raised a hand from it to his mouth, because the people feared the oath.
text.DRB: The people therefore entered into the forest, and there appeared dropping honey, and no man put his hand to his mouth, for the people feared the oath.

text.NRSV: But Jonathan had not heard his father charge the troops with the oath; so he extended the staff that was in his hand, and dipped the tip of it in the honeycomb, and put his hand to his mouth; and his eyes brightened.
text.Rotherham: But, Jonathan, heard not his father putting the people on oath, so he reached forth the end of the staff that was in his hand, and dipped it in the honey-copse,—and brought back his hand to his mouth, and his eyes were brightened.
text.KJV: But Jonathan heard not when his father charged the people with the oath: wherefore he put forth the end of the rod that was in his hand, and dipped it in an honeycomb, and put his hand to his mouth; and his eyes were enlightened.
text.NABRE: Violation of the Oath. Jonathan, who had not heard that his father had put the people under oath, thrust out the end of the staff he was holding and dipped it into the honeycomb. Then he raised it to his mouth and his eyes brightened.
text.DRB: But Jonathas had not heard when his father adjured the people: & he put forth the tip of the rod which he had in his hand, and dipped it into a honey comb: and he turned his hand to his mouth, and his eyes were illuminated.

text.NRSV: Then one of the soldiers said, “Your father strictly charged the troops with an oath, saying, ‘Cursed be anyone who eats food this day.’ And so the troops are faint.”
text.Rotherham: Then responded one from among the people, and said—With an oath, did thy father charge the people, saying—Cursed, be the man that eateth food to-day! And so the people were faint.
text.KJV: Then answered one of the people, and said, Thy father straitly charged the people with an oath, saying, Cursed be the man that eateth any food this day. And the people were faint.
text.NABRE: At this, one of the soldiers spoke up: “Your father put the people under a strict oath, saying, ‘Cursed be the one who takes food today!’ As a result the people are weakened.”
text.DRB: And one of the people answering, said: Thy father hath bound the people with an oath, saying: Cursed be the man that shall eat bread this day. (And the people was faint).

text.NRSV: Then Jonathan said, “My father has troubled the land; see how my eyes have brightened because I tasted a little of this honey.
text.Rotherham: Then said Jonathan: My father hath afflicted the land,—See, I pray you, how my own eyes have been brightened, because I tasted a little of this honey.
text.KJV: Then said Jonathan, My father hath troubled the land: see, I pray you, how mine eyes have been enlightened, because I tasted a little of this honey.
text.NABRE: Jonathan replied: “My father brings trouble to the land. Look how bright my eyes are because I had this little taste of honey.
text.DRB: And Jonathas said: My father hath troubled the land: yourselves have seen that mine eyes are illuminated, because I have tasted a little of this honey:

text.NRSV: How much better if today the troops had been allowed to eat freely of the spoil taken from their enemies; for the slaughter among the Philistines has not been great.”
text.Rotherham: How much more if haply the people had, eaten freely, to-day of the spoil of their enemies, which they found? for, now, would not the smiting of the Philistines have been, mighty?
text.KJV: How much more, if haply the people had eaten freely to day of the spoil of their enemies which they found? for had there not been now a much greater slaughter among the Philistines?
text.NABRE: What is more, if the army had eaten freely of the enemy’s plunder when they came across it today, surely the slaughter of the Philistines would have been the greater by now!”
text.DRB: how much more if the people had eaten of the prey of their enemies, which they found? Had there not been made a greater plague in the Philisthiims?

text.NRSV: They struck down the Philistines that day from Michmash to Aijalon. And the troops were very faint;
text.Rotherham: Howbeit they smote the Philistines on that day from Michmash to Aijalon,—but the people became exceeding faint.
text.KJV: And they smote the Philistines that day from Michmash to Aijalon: and the people were very faint.
text.NABRE: Consuming the Blood. After the Philistines were routed that day from Michmash to Aijalon, the people were completely exhausted.
text.DRB: They struck therefore in that day the Philisthiims from Machmas unto Aialon. And the people was wearied exceedingly:

text.NRSV: so the troops flew upon the spoil, and took sheep and oxen and calves, and slaughtered them on the ground; and the troops ate them with the blood.
text.Rotherham: And the people darted greedily upon the spoil, and took sheep, and oxen, and calves, and felled them to the ground,—and the people did eat, with the blood.
text.KJV: And the people flew upon the spoil, and took sheep, and oxen, and calves, and slew them on the ground: and the people did eat them with the blood.
text.NABRE: So the army pounced upon the plunder and took sheep, oxen, and calves, slaughtering them on the ground and eating the meat with the blood in it.
text.DRB: and being turned to the prey took sheep, and oxen, and calves, and slew them on the ground: and the people did eat with blood.

text.NRSV: Then it was reported to Saul, “Look, the troops are sinning against the Lord by eating with the blood.” And he said, “You have dealt treacherously; roll a large stone before me here.”
text.Rotherham: And they told Saul, saying, Lo! the people, are sinning against Yahweh, by eating with the blood. And he said—Ye have dealt treacherously, roll unto me, here, a great stone.
text.KJV: Then they told Saul, saying, Behold, the people sin against the Lord, in that they eat with the blood. And he said, Ye have transgressed: roll a great stone unto me this day.
text.NABRE: Informed that the army was sinning against the Lord by eating the meat with blood in it, Saul said: “You have broken faith. Roll a large stone here for me.”
text.DRB: And they told Saul saying that the people had sinned to our Lord, eating with blood. Who said: You have transgressed: Roll to me even now a great stone.

text.NRSV: Saul said, “Disperse yourselves among the troops, and say to them, ‘Let each man bring his ox or his sheep, and slaughter them here, and eat; and do not sin against the Lord by eating with the blood.’” So every one of the troops brought his ox with him that night, and slaughtered it there.
text.Rotherham: And Saul said—Disperse yourselves among the people, and say unto them—Bring near unto me every man his ox, and every man his lamb, and slay them here, and then eat, so shall ye not sin against Yahweh, by eating with the blood. And all the people brought near—every man that which was in his hand, that night, and they slew them there.
text.KJV: And Saul said, Disperse yourselves among the people, and say unto them, Bring me hither every man his ox, and every man his sheep, and slay them here, and eat; and sin not against the Lord in eating with the blood. And all the people brought every man his ox with him that night, and slew them there.
text.NABRE: He continued: “Mingle with the people and tell each of them, ‘Bring an ox or sheep to me. Slaughter them here and then eat. But you must not sin against the Lord by eating meat with blood in it.’” So that night they all brought whatever oxen they had seized, and they slaughtered them there;
text.DRB: And Saul said: Disperse yourselves among the common people, and tell them that every man bring me his oxe and ram, and kill ye them upon this same, and eat, and you shall not sin to our Lord eating with blood. All the people therefore brought every man his oxe in his hand until night: and slew them there.

text.NRSV: And Saul built an altar to the Lord; it was the first altar that he built to the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And Saul built an altar unto Yahweh,—the same, was the first altar that he built unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Saul built an altar unto the Lord: the same was the first altar that he built unto the Lord.
text.NABRE: and Saul built an altar to the Lord —this was the first time he built an altar to the Lord.
text.DRB: And Saul built an altar to our Lord; and then first did he begin to build an altar to our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then Saul said, “Let us go down after the Philistines by night and despoil them until the morning light; let us not leave one of them.” They said, “Do whatever seems good to you.” But the priest said, “Let us draw near to God here.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul—Let us go down after the Philistines by night, and make of them a prey until the morning light, and let us not leave of them, a man. And they said, All that is good in thine eyes, do! Then said the priest, Let us draw near hither unto God.
text.KJV: And Saul said, Let us go down after the Philistines by night, and spoil them until the morning light, and let us not leave a man of them. And they said, Do whatsoever seemeth good unto thee. Then said the priest, Let us draw near hither unto God.
text.NABRE: Jonathan in Danger of Death. Then Saul said, “Let us go down in pursuit of the Philistines by night, to plunder them until daybreak and leave no one alive.” They replied, “Do what you think best.” But the priest said, “Let us consult God.”
text.DRB: And Saul said: Let us fall upon the Philisthiims by night, and let us spoil them till it wax light in the morning, neither let us leave a man of them. And the people said: Do all that seemeth good in thine eyes. And the Priest said: Let us approach hither to God.

text.NRSV: So Saul inquired of God, “Shall I go down after the Philistines? Will you give them into the hand of Israel?” But he did not answer him that day.
text.Rotherham: So Saul asked of God, Shall I go down after the Philistines? wilt thou deliver them into the hand of Israel? But he answered him not, that day.
text.KJV: And Saul asked counsel of God, Shall I go down after the Philistines? wilt thou deliver them into the hand of Israel? But he answered him not that day.
text.NABRE: So Saul inquired of God: “Shall I go down in pursuit of the Philistines? Will you deliver them into the hand of Israel?” But he received no answer on this occasion.
text.DRB: And Saul consulted our Lord: Shall I pursue the Philistiims? Wilt thou deliver them into the hands of Israel? And he answered him not in that day.

text.NRSV: Saul said, “Come here, all you leaders of the people; and let us find out how this sin has arisen today.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul, Come near hither, all ye chiefs of the people,—and get to know and see, wherein, hath been this sin, to-day.
text.KJV: And Saul said, Draw ye near hither, all the chief of the people: and know and see wherein this sin hath been this day.
text.NABRE: “All officers of the army,” Saul announced, “come forward. Find out how this sin was committed today.
text.DRB: And Saul said: Bring hither all the corners of the people: and know, and see by whom this sin hath chanced today.

text.NRSV: For as the Lord lives who saves Israel, even if it is in my son Jonathan, he shall surely die!” But there was no one among all the people who answered him.
text.Rotherham: For, by the life of Yahweh, who saveth Israel, though it be in Jonathan my son, yet shall he, die. But there was none ready to answer him, of all the people.
text.KJV: For, as the Lord liveth, which saveth Israel, though it be in Jonathan my son, he shall surely die. But there was not a man among all the people that answered him.
text.NABRE: As the Lord lives who has given victory to Israel, even if my son Jonathan has committed it, he shall surely die!” But none of the people answered him.
text.DRB: Our Lord the Savior of Israel liveth, that if it were done by Jonathas my son, he shall die without revoking. Whereunto none of the people gainsaid him.

text.NRSV: He said to all Israel, “You shall be on one side, and I and Jonathan my son will be on the other side.” The people said to Saul, “Do what seems good to you.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he unto all Israel—Ye, shall be on one side, and, I and Jonathan my son, will be on the other side. And the people said unto Saul, What is good in thine eyes, do!
text.KJV: Then said he unto all Israel, Be ye on one side, and I and Jonathan my son will be on the other side. And the people said unto Saul, Do what seemeth good unto thee.
text.NABRE: So he said to all Israel, “Stand on one side, and my son Jonathan and I will stand on the other.” The people responded, “Do what you think best.”
text.DRB: And he said to all Israel: Be you separated into one side, and I with Jonathas my son will be on the other side. And the people answered Saul: Do what seemeth good in thine eyes.

text.NRSV: Then Saul said, “O Lord God of Israel, why have you not answered your servant today? If this guilt is in me or in my son Jonathan, O Lord God of Israel, give Urim; but if this guilt is in your people Israel, give Thummim.” And Jonathan and Saul were selected, but the people were cleared.
text.Rotherham: And Saul said unto Yahweh—O God of Israel! now set forth the truth. Then were, Jonathan and Saul, taken, and, the people, escaped.
text.KJV: Therefore Saul said unto the Lord God of Israel, Give a perfect lot. And Saul and Jonathan were taken: but the people escaped.
text.NABRE: And Saul said to the Lord, the God of Israel: “Why did you not answer your servant this time? If the blame for this resides in me or my son Jonathan, Lord, God of Israel, respond with Urim; but if this guilt is in your people Israel, respond with Thummim.” Jonathan and Saul were designated, and the people went free.
text.DRB: And Saul said to our Lord: Lord God of Israel, give a sign: and Jonathas was caught and Saul, and the people went forth.

text.NRSV: Then Saul said, “Cast the lot between me and my son Jonathan.” And Jonathan was selected.
text.Rotherham: And Saul said, Cast lots between me, and Jonathan my son. Then was, Jonathan, taken.
text.KJV: And Saul said, Cast lots between me and Jonathan my son. And Jonathan was taken.
text.NABRE: Saul then said, “Cast lots between me and my son Jonathan.” And Jonathan was designated.
text.DRB: And Saul said: Cast ye lot between me, and Jonathas my son. And Jonathas was taken.

text.NRSV: Saul said to Jonathan, “Tell me what you have done.” Jonathan told him, “I tasted a little honey with the tip of the staff that was in my hand; here I am, I will die.”
text.Rotherham: And Saul said unto Jonathan, Come tell me, what thou hast done. So Jonathan told him, and said, I, just tasted, with the end of the staff that was in my hand, a little honey, here I am—I must die!
text.KJV: Then Saul said to Jonathan, Tell me what thou hast done. And Jonathan told him, and said, I did but taste a little honey with the end of the rod that was in mine hand, and, lo, I must die.
text.NABRE: Saul said to Jonathan, “Tell me what you have done.” Jonathan replied, “I only tasted a little honey from the end of the staff I was holding. Am I to die for this?”
text.DRB: And Saul said to Jonathas: Tell me what thou hast done. And Jonathas told him, and said: Tasting I tasted in the tip of the rod which was in mine hand a little honey, & behold I die.

text.NRSV: Saul said, “God do so to me and more also; you shall surely die, Jonathan!”
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul: So, may God do, and, so, may he add, surely thou must, die, Jonathan!
text.KJV: And Saul answered, God do so and more also: for thou shalt surely die, Jonathan.
text.NABRE: Saul declared, “May God do thus to me, and more, if you do not indeed die, Jonathan!”
text.DRB: And Saul said: These things do God to me, & these things add he, that dying thou shalt die Jonathas.

text.NRSV: Then the people said to Saul, “Shall Jonathan die, who has accomplished this great victory in Israel? Far from it! As the Lord lives, not one hair of his head shall fall to the ground; for he has worked with God today.” So the people ransomed Jonathan, and he did not die.
text.Rotherham: But the people said unto Saul—Shall, Jonathan, die, who hath wrought this great salvation in Israel? Far be it! By the life of Yahweh, there shall not fall a hair of his head to the ground, for, with God, hath he wrought this day. So the people delivered Jonathan, that he died not.
text.KJV: And the people said unto Saul, Shall Jonathan die, who hath wrought this great salvation in Israel? God forbid: as the Lord liveth, there shall not one hair of his head fall to the ground; for he hath wrought with God this day. So the people rescued Jonathan, that he died not.
text.NABRE: Rescue of Jonathan. But the soldiers protested to Saul: “Is Jonathan to die, the man who won this great victory for Israel? This must not be! As the Lord lives, not a single hair of his head shall fall to the ground, for God was with him in what he did today!” Thus the soldiers rescued Jonathan and he did not die.
text.DRB: And the people said to Saul: Shall Jonathas then die, which hath made this great salvation in Israel? This is unlawful: our Lord liveth, if there shall fall a hair from his head upon the ground, because with God hath he wrought today. The people therefore delivered Jonathas, that he should not die.

text.NRSV: Then Saul withdrew from pursuing the Philistines; and the Philistines went to their own place.
text.Rotherham: Then Saul went up from following the Philistines,—and, the Philistines, departed unto their own place.
text.KJV: Then Saul went up from following the Philistines: and the Philistines went to their own place.
text.NABRE: After that Saul gave up the pursuit of the Philistines, who returned to their own territory.
text.DRB: And Saul retired, neither did he pursue the Philistiims: moreover the Philistiims departed into their places.

text.NRSV: When Saul had taken the kingship over Israel, he fought against all his enemies on every side—against Moab, against the Ammonites, against Edom, against the kings of Zobah, and against the Philistines; wherever he turned he was victorious.
text.Rotherham: And, Saul, took possession of the kingdom over Israel,—and made war round about against all his enemies—against Moab, and against the sons of Ammon, and against Edom, and against the kings of Zobah, and against the Philistines, and, whomsoever he turned against, he was victorious.
text.KJV: So Saul took the kingdom over Israel, and fought against all his enemies on every side, against Moab, and against the children of Ammon, and against Edom, and against the kings of Zobah, and against the Philistines: and whithersoever he turned himself, he vexed them.
text.NABRE: Saul’s Victories. After taking possession of the kingship over Israel, Saul waged war on its enemies all around—Moab, the Ammonites, Edom, the kings of Zobah, and the Philistines. Wherever he turned, he was successful
text.DRB: And Saul, his kingdom being established over Israel, fought round about against all his enemies, against Moab, & the children of Ammon, and Edom, and the kings of Soba, and the Philisthians: and whither soever he turned himself, he overcame.

text.NRSV: He did valiantly, and struck down the Amalekites, and rescued Israel out of the hands of those who plundered them.
text.Rotherham: Then made he ready a force, and smote the Amalekites,—and delivered Israel out of the hand of such as plundered them.
text.KJV: And he gathered an host, and smote the Amalekites, and delivered Israel out of the hands of them that spoiled them.
text.NABRE: and fought bravely. He defeated Amalek and delivered Israel from the hand of those who were plundering them.
text.DRB: And gathering together an army, he struck Amalec, and delivered Israel from the hand of the spoilers thereof.

text.NRSV: The sons of Saul were Jonathan, Ishvi, and Malchishua; and the names of his two daughters were these: the name of the firstborn was Merab, and the name of the younger, Michal.
text.Rotherham: And the sons of Saul were Jonathan, and Ishvi, and Malchishua,—and, the names of his two daughters, were, the name of the firstborn, Merab, and, the name of the younger, Michal,—
text.KJV: Now the sons of Saul were Jonathan, and Ishui, and Melchi–shua: and the names of his two daughters were these; the name of the firstborn Merab, and the name of the younger Michal:
text.NABRE: Saul’s Family. The sons of Saul were Jonathan, Ishvi, and Malchishua; the name of his firstborn daughter was Merob; the name of the younger was Michal.
text.DRB: And the sons of Saul, were Jonathas and Jesui, and Melchisua: and the names of his two daughters, the name of the first born Merob, and the name of the younger Michol.

text.NRSV: The name of Saul’s wife was Ahinoam daughter of Ahimaaz. And the name of the commander of his army was Abner son of Ner, Saul’s uncle;
text.Rotherham: and, the name of Saul’s wife, was Ahinoam, daughter of Ahimaaz,—and the name of the prince of his host, Abner, son of Ner, Saul’s uncle.
text.KJV: And the name of Saul’s wife was Ahinoam, the daughter of Ahimaaz: and the name of the captain of his host was Abner, the son of Ner, Saul’s uncle.
text.NABRE: The name of Saul’s wife was Ahinoam, daughter of Ahimaaz. The name of his general was Abner, son of Ner, Saul’s uncle;
text.DRB: And the name of Saul's wife, Achinoam the daughter of Achimaas: and the name of the Prince of his host Abner, the son of Ner, the cousin german of Saul by the father.

text.NRSV: Kish was the father of Saul, and Ner father of Abner was the son of Abiel.
text.Rotherham: And, Kish, was Saul’s father, and, Ner, Abner’s father, was son of Abiel.
text.KJV: And Kish was the father of Saul; and Ner the father of Abner was the son of Abiel.
text.NABRE: Kish, Saul’s father, and Ner, Abner’s father, were sons of Abiel.
text.DRB: Moreover Cis was the father of Saul, and Ner the father of Abner, the son of Abiel.

text.NRSV: There was hard fighting against the Philistines all the days of Saul; when Saul saw any strong or valiant warrior, he took him into his service.
text.Rotherham: And the war was severe against the Philistines all the days of Saul,—and, whensoever Saul saw any mighty man, or any son of valour, he drew him unto himself.
text.KJV: And there was sore war against the Philistines all the days of Saul: and when Saul saw any strong man, or any valiant man, he took him unto him.
text.NABRE: There was heavy fighting with the Philistines during Saul’s lifetime. Whenever Saul saw any strong or brave man, he took him into his service.
text.DRB: And there was mighty battle against the Philisthians all the days of Saul. For whomsoever Saul had seen a valiant man, and fit for battle, he joined him to himself.

text.NRSV: Samuel said to Saul, “The Lord sent me to anoint you king over his people Israel; now therefore listen to the words of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said unto Saul, It was, me, Yahweh sent to anoint thee to be king over his people, over Israel,—now, therefore, hearken thou to the voice of Yahweh’s words.
text.KJV: Samuel also said unto Saul, The Lord sent me to anoint thee to be king over his people, over Israel: now therefore hearken thou unto the voice of the words of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Samuel said to Saul: “It was I the Lord sent to anoint you king over his people Israel. Now, therefore, listen to the message of the Lord.
text.DRB: And Samuel said to Saul: Our Lord sent me to anoint thee king over his people Israel; now therefore hear the voice of our Lord:

text.NRSV: Thus says the Lord of hosts, ‘I will punish the Amalekites for what they did in opposing the Israelites when they came up out of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Thus, saith Yahweh of hosts, I have well considered what Amalek did unto Israel—how he lay in wait for him in the way, when he came up out of Egypt.
text.KJV: Thus saith the Lord of hosts, I remember that which Amalek did to Israel, how he laid wait for him in the way, when he came up from Egypt.
text.NABRE: Thus says the Lord of hosts: I will punish what Amalek did to the Israelites when he barred their way as they came up from Egypt.
text.DRB: Thus saith the Lord of hosts: I have recounted whatsoever Amalec hath done to Israel: how he resisted them in the way when they came up out of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Now go and attack Amalek, and utterly destroy all that they have; do not spare them, but kill both man and woman, child and infant, ox and sheep, camel and donkey.’”
text.Rotherham: Now, go and smite Amalek, and devote ye to destruction all that he hath, and spare him not,—but thou shalt slay both man and woman, both child and suckling, both ox and sheep, both camel and ass.
text.KJV: Now go and smite Amalek, and utterly destroy all that they have, and spare them not; but slay both man and woman, infant and suckling, ox and sheep, camel and ass.
text.NABRE: Go, now, attack Amalek, and put under the ban everything he has. Do not spare him; kill men and women, children and infants, oxen and sheep, camels and donkeys.”
text.DRB: Now therefore go, and strike Amalec, and destroy all that he hath: spare him not, and covet not ought of his things: but kill from man unto woman, both child & suckling, oxe and sheep, camel and ass.

text.NRSV: So Saul summoned the people, and numbered them in Telaim, two hundred thousand foot soldiers, and ten thousand soldiers of Judah.
text.Rotherham: So then Saul called together the people, and numbered them in Telaim, two hundred thousand footmen,—and ten thousand men of Judah.
text.KJV: And Saul gathered the people together, and numbered them in Telaim, two hundred thousand footmen, and ten thousand men of Judah.
text.NABRE: Saul alerted the army, and at Telaim reviewed two hundred thousand foot soldiers and ten thousand men of Judah.
text.DRB: Saul therefore commanded the people, and numbered them as it were lambs: two hundred thousand footmen, & ten thousand of the men of Juda.

text.NRSV: Saul came to the city of the Amalekites and lay in wait in the valley.
text.Rotherham: And Saul came as far as the city of Amalek,—and stirred up strife in the ravine.
text.KJV: And Saul came to a city of Amalek, and laid wait in the valley.
text.NABRE: Saul went to the city of Amalek and set up an ambush in the wadi.
text.DRB: And when Saul was come unto the city of Amalec, he laid ambushments in the torrent.

text.NRSV: Saul said to the Kenites, “Go! Leave! Withdraw from among the Amalekites, or I will destroy you with them; for you showed kindness to all the people of Israel when they came up from Egypt.” So the Kenites withdrew from the Amalekites.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul unto the Kenites—Go, depart, get you down out of the midst of the Amalekites, lest I destroy you with them, yet, ye, dealt in lovingkindness, with all the sons of Israel, when they came up out of Egypt. So the Kenites departed out of the midst of the Amalekites.
text.KJV: And Saul said unto the Kenites, Go, depart, get you down from among the Amalekites, lest I destroy you with them: for ye shewed kindness to all the children of Israel, when they came up out of Egypt. So the Kenites departed from among the Amalekites.
text.NABRE: He warned the Kenites: “Leave Amalek, turn aside and come down so I will not have to destroy you with them, for you were loyal to the Israelites when they came up from Egypt.” After the Kenites left,
text.DRB: And Saul said to the Cineite: Go ye, retire & depart from Amalec: lest perhaps I wrap thee in with him, for thou hast done mercy with all the children of Israel, when they descended out of Ægypt. And the Cineite departed out of the midst of Amalec.

text.NRSV: Saul defeated the Amalekites, from Havilah as far as Shur, which is east of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: And Saul smote Amalek,—from Havilah, till thou enterest Shur, which is over against Egypt.
text.KJV: And Saul smote the Amalekites from Havilah until thou comest to Shur, that is over against Egypt.
text.NABRE: Saul routed Amalek from Havilah to the approaches of Shur, on the frontier of Egypt.
text.DRB: And Saul struck Amalec from Hevila, until thou come to Sur, which is over-against Ægypt.

text.NRSV: He took King Agag of the Amalekites alive, but utterly destroyed all the people with the edge of the sword.
text.Rotherham: And he took Agag, king of Amalek, alive,—but, all the people, devoted he to destruction at the edge of the sword.
text.KJV: And he took Agag the king of the Amalekites alive, and utterly destroyed all the people with the edge of the sword.
text.NABRE: He took Agag, king of Amalek, alive, but the rest of the people he destroyed by the sword, putting them under the ban.
text.DRB: And he apprehended Agag the king of Amalec alive: but all the common people he slew in the edge of the sword.

text.NRSV: Saul and the people spared Agag, and the best of the sheep and of the cattle and of the fatlings, and the lambs, and all that was valuable, and would not utterly destroy them; all that was despised and worthless they utterly destroyed.
text.Rotherham: But Saul and the people spared Agag, and the best of the sheep and the oxen and the lambs, also the fatlings, and all that was good, and would not devote them to destruction,—but, all the cattle that was contemptible and diseased, that, devoted they to destruction.
text.KJV: But Saul and the people spared Agag, and the best of the sheep, and of the oxen, and of the fatlings, and the lambs, and all that was good, and would not utterly destroy them: but every thing that was vile and refuse, that they destroyed utterly.
text.NABRE: He and his troops spared Agag and the best of the fat sheep and oxen, and the lambs. They refused to put under the ban anything that was worthwhile, destroying only what was worthless and of no account.
text.DRB: And Saul and the people spared Agag, & the best flocks of sheep & herds, & the garments & rams, & all things, that were fair, neither would they destroy them: but whatsoever was vile and refuse, that they destroyed.

text.NRSV: The word of the Lord came to Samuel:
text.Rotherham: Then came the word of Yahweh unto Samuel, saying—
text.KJV: Then came the word of the Lord unto Samuel, saying,
text.NABRE: Samuel Rebukes Saul. Then the word of the Lord came to Samuel:
text.DRB: And the word of our Lord was made to Samuel, saying:

text.NRSV: “I regret that I made Saul king, for he has turned back from following me, and has not carried out my commands.” Samuel was angry; and he cried out to the Lord all night.
text.Rotherham: I am grieved that I made Saul to be king, for he hath turned back from following me, and, my words, hath he not established. And it was vexing to Samuel, so that he made outcry unto Yahweh, all the night.
text.KJV: It repenteth me that I have set up Saul to be king: for he is turned back from following me, and hath not performed my commandments. And it grieved Samuel; and he cried unto the Lord all night.
text.NABRE: I regret having made Saul king, for he has turned from me and has not kept my command. At this Samuel grew angry and cried out to the Lord all night.
text.DRB: It repenteth me that I have made Saul king: because he hath forsaken me, & hath not fulfilled my words in work. And Samuel was strucken sad, and cried to our Lord all the night.

text.NRSV: Samuel rose early in the morning to meet Saul, and Samuel was told, “Saul went to Carmel, where he set up a monument for himself, and on returning he passed on down to Gilgal.”
text.Rotherham: And Samuel rose early to meet Saul, in the morning, and it was told Samuel, saying—Saul having come to Carmel, and lo! having set him up a sign, hath gone round, and passed over, and descended to Gilgal.
text.KJV: And when Samuel rose early to meet Saul in the morning, it was told Samuel, saying, Saul came to Carmel, and, behold, he set him up a place, and is gone about, and passed on, and gone down to Gilgal.
text.NABRE: Early in the morning he went to meet Saul, but was informed that Saul had gone to Carmel, where he set up a monument in his own honor, and that on his return he had gone down to Gilgal.
text.DRB: And when Samuel had risen in the night, to go to Saul in the morning, it was told Samuel, that Saul was come into Carmelus, and had erected to himself a triumphant arch, and returning was passed, & gone into Galgal. Samuel therefore came to Saul, and Saul offered an holocaust to our Lord of the first of the preys, which he had brought from Amalec.

text.NRSV: When Samuel came to Saul, Saul said to him, “May you be blessed by the Lord; I have carried out the command of the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: And Samuel came unto Saul,—and Saul said unto him—Blessed, be thou by Yahweh: I have established the word of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Samuel came to Saul: and Saul said unto him, Blessed be thou of the Lord: I have performed the commandment of the Lord.
text.NABRE: When Samuel came to him, Saul greeted him: “The Lord bless you! I have kept the command of the Lord.”
text.DRB: And when Samuel was come to Saul, Saul said to him: Blessed be thou to our Lord, I have fulfilled the word of our Lord.

text.NRSV: But Samuel said, “What then is this bleating of sheep in my ears, and the lowing of cattle that I hear?”
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said, What then is this bleating of sheep, in mine ears,—and the lowing of oxen, which I can hear?
text.KJV: And Samuel said, What meaneth then this bleating of the sheep in mine ears, and the lowing of the oxen which I hear?
text.NABRE: But Samuel asked, “What, then, is this bleating of sheep that comes to my ears, the lowing of oxen that I hear?”
text.DRB: And Samuel said: And what is this voice of flocks, which soundeth in mine ears, and of herds, which I hear?

text.NRSV: Saul said, “They have brought them from the Amalekites; for the people spared the best of the sheep and the cattle, to sacrifice to the Lord your God; but the rest we have utterly destroyed.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul—From the Amalekites, have they brought them in; in that the people spared the best of the sheep, and of the oxen, to sacrifice unto Yahweh thy God,—but, the rest, have we devoted to destruction.
text.KJV: And Saul said, They have brought them from the Amalekites: for the people spared the best of the sheep and of the oxen, to sacrifice unto the Lord thy God; and the rest we have utterly destroyed.
text.NABRE: Saul replied: “They were brought from Amalek. The people spared the best sheep and oxen to sacrifice to the Lord, your God; but the rest we destroyed, putting them under the ban.”
text.DRB: And Saul said: They have brought them from Amalec: for the people hath spared the better sheep and herds that they might be immolated to our Lord thy God, but the rest we have slain.

text.NRSV: Then Samuel said to Saul, “Stop! I will tell you what the Lord said to me last night.” He replied, “Speak.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Samuel unto Saul, Stay, and let me tell thee, that which Yahweh hath spoken unto me, this night. And he said to him—Speak.
text.KJV: Then Samuel said unto Saul, Stay, and I will tell thee what the Lord hath said to me this night. And he said unto him, Say on.
text.NABRE: Samuel said to Saul: “Stop! Let me tell you what the Lord said to me last night.” “Speak!” he replied.
text.DRB: And Samuel said to Saul: Suffer me, and I will shew thee what our Lord hath spoken to me this night. And he said to him: Speak.

text.NRSV: Samuel said, “Though you are little in your own eyes, are you not the head of the tribes of Israel? The Lord anointed you king over Israel.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said, Was it not, when, little, thou wast in thine own eyes, that thou wast made, head of the tribes of Israel,—and that Yahweh anointed thee to be king over Israel?
text.KJV: And Samuel said, When thou wast little in thine own sight, wast thou not made the head of the tribes of Israel, and the Lord anointed thee king over Israel?
text.NABRE: Samuel then said: “Though little in your own eyes, are you not chief of the tribes of Israel? The Lord anointed you king of Israel
text.DRB: And Samuel said: When thou wast a little one in thine own eyes, was thou not made chief in the tribes of Israel? And our Lord anointed thee to be king over Israel,

text.NRSV: And the Lord sent you on a mission, and said, ‘Go, utterly destroy the sinners, the Amalekites, and fight against them until they are consumed.’
text.Rotherham: So then Yahweh sent thee on a journey,—and said—Go, and devastate to destruction, the sinners—the Amalekites, and make war against them, until they have consumed them.
text.KJV: And the Lord sent thee on a journey, and said, Go and utterly destroy the sinners the Amalekites, and fight against them until they be consumed.
text.NABRE: and sent you on a mission, saying: Go and put the sinful Amalekites under a ban of destruction. Fight against them until you have exterminated them.
text.DRB: and our Lord sent thee on the way, and said: Go, and kill the sinners of Amalec, and thou shalt fight against them until the utter destruction of them.

text.NRSV: Why then did you not obey the voice of the Lord? Why did you swoop down on the spoil, and do what was evil in the sight of the Lord?”
text.Rotherham: Wherefore, then, didst thou not hearken unto the voice of Yahweh,—but didst rush upon the spoil, and do that which was wrong in the sight of Yahweh?
text.KJV: Wherefore then didst thou not obey the voice of the Lord, but didst fly upon the spoil, and didst evil in the sight of the Lord?
text.NABRE: Why then have you disobeyed the Lord? You have pounced on the spoil, thus doing what was evil in the Lord ’s sight.”
text.DRB: Why therefore hast thou not heard the voice of our Lord: but art turned to the prey, & hast done evil in the eyes of our Lord?

text.NRSV: Saul said to Samuel, “I have obeyed the voice of the Lord, I have gone on the mission on which the Lord sent me, I have brought Agag the king of Amalek, and I have utterly destroyed the Amalekites.
text.Rotherham: And Saul said unto Samuel—As indeed I have hearkened unto the voice of Yahweh, and have been on the journey on which Yahweh sent me,—and have brought in Agag, king of Amalek, and, the Amalekites, have I devoted to destruction.
text.KJV: And Saul said unto Samuel, Yea, I have obeyed the voice of the Lord, and have gone the way which the Lord sent me, and have brought Agag the king of Amalek, and have utterly destroyed the Amalekites.
text.NABRE: Saul explained to Samuel: “I did indeed obey the Lord and fulfill the mission on which the Lord sent me. I have brought back Agag, the king of Amalek, and, carrying out the ban, I have destroyed the Amalekites.
text.DRB: And Saul said to Samuel: Yea I have heard the voice of our Lord, & have walked in the way by which our Lord sent me, & have brought Agag the king of Amalec, & Amalec I have slain.

text.NRSV: But from the spoil the people took sheep and cattle, the best of the things devoted to destruction, to sacrifice to the Lord your God in Gilgal.”
text.Rotherham: And the people took, of the spoil, sheep and oxen, the chief of the things so devoted,—to sacrifice unto Yahweh thy God, in Gilgal.
text.KJV: But the people took of the spoil, sheep and oxen, the chief of the things which should have been utterly destroyed, to sacrifice unto the Lord thy God in Gilgal.
text.NABRE: But from the spoil the army took sheep and oxen, the best of what had been banned, to sacrifice to the Lord your God in Gilgal.”
text.DRB: But the people took of the prey sheep and oxen, the principal of those things which were slain, to immolate to our Lord their God in Galgal.

text.NRSV: And Samuel said, “Has the Lord as great delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices, as in obeying the voice of the Lord? Surely, to obey is better than sacrifice, and to heed than the fat of rams.
text.Rotherham: Then said Samuel—Doth that which is pleasing unto Yahweh consist in ascending-offerings and sacrifices, So much as in hearkening unto the voice of Yahweh? Lo! to hearken, is, better, than, sacrifice. And, to give heed, than, the fat of rams;
text.KJV: And Samuel said, Hath the Lord as great delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices, as in obeying the voice of the Lord? Behold, to obey is better than sacrifice, and to hearken than the fat of rams.
text.NABRE: But Samuel said: “Does the Lord delight in burnt offerings and sacrifices as much as in obedience to the Lord ’s command? Obedience is better than sacrifice, to listen, better than the fat of rams.
text.DRB: And Samuel said: why? Will our Lord have holocausts and victims, and not rather that the voice of our Lord be obeyed? For Better is Obedience Than Victims: and to harken rather than to offer the fat of rams.

text.NRSV: For rebellion is no less a sin than divination, and stubbornness is like iniquity and idolatry. Because you have rejected the word of the Lord, he has also rejected you from being king.”
text.Rotherham: For, as the sin of divination, is, rebelliousness,—and, as transgression with household gods, is, stubbornness,—Because thou hast rejected the word of Yahweh, therefore hath he rejected thee from being king.
text.KJV: For rebellion is as the sin of witchcraft, and stubbornness is as iniquity and idolatry. Because thou hast rejected the word of the Lord, he hath also rejected thee from being king.
text.NABRE: For a sin of divination is rebellion, and arrogance, the crime of idolatry. Because you have rejected the word of the Lord, the Lord in turn has rejected you as king.”
text.DRB: Because it is as it were the sin of enchantment, to resist, & as it were the wickedness of idolatry, to refuse to obey. For as much therefore as thou hast rejected the word of our Lord, our Lord hath rejected thee, that thou shalt not be king.

text.NRSV: Saul said to Samuel, “I have sinned; for I have transgressed the commandment of the Lord and your words, because I feared the people and obeyed their voice.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul unto Samuel—I have sinned; for I have transgressed the bidding of Yahweh, and thy words,—for I feared the people, and hearkened unto their voice.
text.KJV: And Saul said unto Samuel, I have sinned: for I have transgressed the commandment of the Lord, and thy words: because I feared the people, and obeyed their voice.
text.NABRE: Rejection of Saul. Saul admitted to Samuel: “I have sinned, for I have transgressed the command of the Lord and your instructions. I feared the people and obeyed them.
text.DRB: And Saul said to Samuel: I have sinned, because I have transgressed the saying of our Lord, and thy words, fearing the people, and obeying their voice.

text.NRSV: Now therefore, I pray, pardon my sin, and return with me, so that I may worship the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, I pray thee, pardon my sin,—and turn again with me, that I may bow down unto Yahweh.
text.KJV: Now therefore, I pray thee, pardon my sin, and turn again with me, that I may worship the Lord.
text.NABRE: Now forgive my sin, and return with me, that I may worship the Lord.”
text.DRB: But now bear I beseech thee my sin, and return with me, that I may adore our Lord.

text.NRSV: Samuel said to Saul, “I will not return with you; for you have rejected the word of the Lord, and the Lord has rejected you from being king over Israel.”
text.Rotherham: And Samuel said unto Saul, I will not turn again with thee,—Because thou hast rejected the word of Yahweh, therefore hath Yahweh rejected thee from being king over Israel.
text.KJV: And Samuel said unto Saul, I will not return with thee: for thou hast rejected the word of the Lord, and the Lord hath rejected thee from being king over Israel.
text.NABRE: But Samuel said to Saul, “I will not return with you, because you rejected the word of the Lord and the Lord has rejected you as king of Israel.”
