text.NABRE: The priest replied: “The sword of Goliath the Philistine, whom you killed in the Valley of Elah, is here wrapped in a garment behind an ephod. If you wish to take it, do so; there is no sword here except that one.” “There is none like it,” David cried, “give it to me!”
text.DRB: David therefore arose, and fled that day from the face of Saul: and came to Achis the king of Geth,

text.NRSV: The servants of Achish said to him, “Is this not David the king of the land? Did they not sing to one another of him in dances, ‘Saul has killed his thousands, and David his ten thousands’?”
text.Rotherham: And the servants of Achish said unto him, Is not, this, David, king of the land? Was it not, of this man, that they kept responding in the dances, saying, Saul, hath smitten his, thousands, but, David, his, tens of thousands?
text.KJV: And the servants of Achish said unto him, Is not this David the king of the land? did they not sing one to another of him in dances, saying, Saul hath slain his thousands, and David his ten thousands?
text.NABRE: David a Fugitive. That same day David fled from Saul, going to Achish, king of Gath.
text.DRB: & the servants of Achis said to him, when they had seen David: Is not this David the king of the land? Did they not sing in dances to this man saying: Saul struck a thousand, and David ten thousand?

text.NRSV: David took these words to heart and was very much afraid of King Achish of Gath.
text.Rotherham: And David laid up these words in his heart,—and feared greatly, because of Achish king of Gath.
text.KJV: And David laid up these words in his heart, and was sore afraid of Achish the king of Gath.
text.NABRE: But the servants of Achish said to him, “Is this not David, the king of the land? Is it not for him that during their dances they sing out, ‘Saul has slain his thousands, David his tens of thousands’?”
text.DRB: But David put these words in his heart, and feared exceedingly at the face of Achis the king of Geth.

text.NRSV: So he changed his behavior before them; he pretended to be mad when in their hands. He scratched on the doors of the gate, and let his spittle run down his beard.
text.Rotherham: So he feigned himself mad, in their sight, and raged in their hand,—and struck against the doors of the gate, and let his spittle run down upon his beard.
text.KJV: And he changed his behaviour before them, and feigned himself mad in their hands, and scrabbled on the doors of the gate, and let his spittle fall down upon his beard.
text.NABRE: David took note of these remarks and became very much afraid of Achish, king of Gath.
text.DRB: And he changed his countenance before them, and slipt down between their hands: and he stumbled at the doors of the gate, and his spittle ran down upon his beard.

text.NRSV: Achish said to his servants, “Look, you see the man is mad; why then have you brought him to me?
text.Rotherham: Then said Achish unto his servants,—Lo! ye can see, a madman playing his pranks, wherefore should ye bring him in, unto me?
text.KJV: Then said Achish unto his servants, Lo, ye see the man is mad: wherefore then have ye brought him to me?
text.NABRE: So, he feigned insanity in front of them and acted like a madman in their custody, drumming on the doors of the gate and drooling onto his beard.
text.DRB: And Achis said to his servants: You have seen the man mad: why have you brought him to me?

text.NRSV: Do I lack madmen, that you have brought this fellow to behave as a madman in my presence? Shall this fellow come into my house?”
text.Rotherham: Lacking of madmen, am I that ye should bring in this one to play his mad pranks, unto me? Shall, this, one enter my household?
text.KJV: Have I need of mad men, that ye have brought this fellow to play the mad man in my presence? shall this fellow come into my house?
text.NABRE: Finally Achish said to his servants: “You see the man is mad. Why did you bring him to me?
text.DRB: Do we lack mad men, that you have brought in this fellow, to play the mad man in my presence? Shall this man enter into my house?

text.NRSV: David left Gath and escaped to the cave of Adullam; when his brothers and all his father’s house heard it, they went down there to him.
text.Rotherham: David therefore departed thence, and escaped, into the cave of Adullam,—and, when his brethren and all the household of his father heard it, they went down unto him, thither.
text.KJV: David therefore departed thence, and escaped to the cave Adullam: and when his brethren and all his father’s house heard it, they went down thither to him.
text.NABRE: When his brothers and the rest of his family heard about it, they came down to him there.
text.DRB: David therefore went from thence, and fled to the cave of Odollam. Which when his brethren had heard, and all his father's house, they went down to him thither.

text.NRSV: Everyone who was in distress, and everyone who was in debt, and everyone who was discontented gathered to him; and he became captain over them. Those who were with him numbered about four hundred.
text.Rotherham: And there gathered themselves unto him—every one that was in distress, and every one that had a creditor, and every one embittered in soul, and he came to be over them as a prince,—and there were with him, about four hundred men.
text.KJV: And every one that was in distress, and every one that was in debt, and every one that was discontented, gathered themselves unto him; and he became a captain over them: and there were with him about four hundred men.
text.NABRE: He was joined by all those in difficulties or in debt, or embittered, and became their leader. About four hundred men were with him.
text.DRB: And there were gathered unto him all that were in distress, and oppressed with debt, and of a pensive heart, and he was made their Prince, and there were with him about four hundred men.

text.NRSV: David went from there to Mizpeh of Moab. He said to the king of Moab, “Please let my father and mother come and stay with you, until I know what God will do for me.”
text.Rotherham: And David departed thence, to Mizpah of Moab,—and said unto the king of Moab, I pray thee, let my father and my mother dwell with you, until that I know what God will do for me.
text.KJV: And David went thence to Mizpeh of Moab: and he said unto the king of Moab, Let my father and my mother, I pray thee, come forth, and be with you, till I know what God will do for me.
text.NABRE: From there David went to Mizpeh of Moab and said to the king of Moab, “Let my father and mother stay with you, until I learn what God will do for me.”
text.DRB: And David departed from thence into Maspha which is Moab: and he said to the king of Moab: Let my father and my mother tarry with you, I beseech thee, till I know what God will do to me.

text.NRSV: He left them with the king of Moab, and they stayed with him all the time that David was in the stronghold.
text.Rotherham: So he set them before the king of Moab,—and they dwelt with him, all the days that David was in the fortress.
text.KJV: And he brought them before the king of Moab: and they dwelt with him all the while that David was in the hold.
text.NABRE: He left them with the king of Moab; they stayed with him as long as David remained in the stronghold.
text.DRB: And he left them before the face of the king of Moab, and they abode with him all the days that David was in garrison.

text.NRSV: Then the prophet Gad said to David, “Do not remain in the stronghold; leave, and go into the land of Judah.” So David left, and went to the forest of Hereth.
text.Rotherham: Then said Gad the prophet unto David—Thou must not abide in the fortress, go and get thee into the land of Judah. So David departed, and entered the forest of Hereth.
text.KJV: And the prophet Gad said unto David, Abide not in the hold; depart, and get thee into the land of Judah. Then David departed, and came into the forest of Hareth.
text.NABRE: But Gad the prophet said to David: “Do not remain in the stronghold! Leave! Go to the land of Judah.” And so David left and went to the forest of Hereth.
text.DRB: And Gad the Prophet said to David. Tarry not in garrison, depart, and go into the Land of Juda. And David departed, and came into the forest of Haret.

text.NRSV: Saul heard that David and those who were with him had been located. Saul was sitting at Gibeah, under the tamarisk tree on the height, with his spear in his hand, and all his servants were standing around him.
text.Rotherham: And, when Saul heard that David was discovered, and the men that were with him—Saul, being seated in Gibeah under the tamarisk-tree in Ramah, with his spear in his hand, and all his servants stationed by him,—
text.KJV: When Saul heard that David was discovered, and the men that were with him, (now Saul abode in Gibeah under a tree in Ramah, having his spear in his hand, and all his servants were standing about him;)
text.NABRE: Doeg Betrays Ahimelech. Now Saul heard that David and his men had been located. At the time he was sitting in Gibeah under a tamarisk tree on the high place, holding his spear, while all his servants stood by him.
text.DRB: And Saul heard that David had appeared, and the men that were with him. And Saul when he abode in Gabaa, and was in the wood which is in Rama, holding a spear in his hand, and all his servants that stood about him,

text.NRSV: Saul said to his servants who stood around him, “Hear now, you Benjaminites; will the son of Jesse give every one of you fields and vineyards, will he make every one of you commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds?
text.Rotherham: then said Saul unto his servants who were stationed by him—Hear, I pray you, ye Benjamites! What! even to all of you, will the son of Jesse give fields and vineyards? All of you, will he appoint to be princes of thousands, and princes of hundreds?
text.KJV: Then Saul said unto his servants that stood about him, Hear now, ye Benjamites; will the son of Jesse give every one of you fields and vineyards, and make you all captains of thousands, and captains of hundreds;
text.NABRE: So he said to them: “Listen, men of Benjamin! Will the son of Jesse give all of you fields and vineyards? Will he appoint any of you an officer over a thousand or a hundred men?
text.DRB: he said to his servants that stood about him: Hear me now ye children of Jemini: will the son of Isai give to all you fields, and vineyards, and make all you tribunes, and centurions:

text.NRSV: Is that why all of you have conspired against me? No one discloses to me when my son makes a league with the son of Jesse, none of you is sorry for me or discloses to me that my son has stirred up my servant against me, to lie in wait, as has happened today.”
text.Rotherham: That ye have conspired, all of you, against me, and there is none to uncover mine ear as to the covenanting of my son with the son of Jesse? And none from among you that taketh pity upon me, and that uncovereth mine ear [to this],—that, mine own son, hath stirred up, my servants, against me, to lie in wait, as at this day?
text.KJV: That all of you have conspired against me, and there is none that sheweth me that my son hath made a league with the son of Jesse, and there is none of you that is sorry for me, or sheweth unto me that my son hath stirred up my servant against me, to lie in wait, as at this day?
text.NABRE: Is that why you have all conspired against me? Why no one told me that my son had made a pact with the son of Jesse? None of you has shown compassion for me by revealing to me that my son has incited my servant to ambush me, as is the case today.”
text.DRB: because you have all conspired against me, and there is none that telleth me, especially where my son also hath entered league with the son of Isai? There is none of you that pitieth my case, neither is there that telleth me: for that my son hath raised up my servant against me, lying in wait for me until this day.

text.NRSV: Then Doeg the Edomite, who was in charge of Saul’s servants, answered, “I saw the son of Jesse coming to Nob, to Ahimelech son of Ahitub;
text.Rotherham: Then answered Doeg the Edomite—he, being stationed near the servants of Saul—and said,—I saw the son of Jesse coming into Nob, unto Ahimelech son of Ahitub;
text.KJV: Then answered Doeg the Edomite, which was set over the servants of Saul, and said, I saw the son of Jesse coming to Nob, to Ahimelech the son of Ahitub.
text.NABRE: Then Doeg the Edomite, who was standing with Saul’s officers, spoke up: “I saw the son of Jesse come to Ahimelech, son of Ahitub, in Nob.
text.DRB: And Doeg the Idumeite which stood by, and was the chief among the servants of Saul, answering, I saw, quoth he, the son of Isai in Nobe with Achimelech the son of Achitob the Priest.

text.NRSV: he inquired of the Lord for him, gave him provisions, and gave him the sword of Goliath the Philistine.”
text.Rotherham: Then enquired he for him of Yahweh, Provisions also, gave he unto him,—and, the sword of Goliath the Philistine, gave he unto him.
text.KJV: And he enquired of the Lord for him, and gave him victuals, and gave him the sword of Goliath the Philistine.
text.NABRE: He consulted the Lord for him, furnished him with provisions, and gave him the sword of Goliath the Philistine.”
text.DRB: Who consulted our Lord for him, and gave him victuals, yea and the sword of Goliath the Philistian he gave to him.

text.NRSV: The king sent someone to summon the priest Ahimelech son of Ahitub and all his father’s house, the priests who were at Nob; all of them came to the king.
text.Rotherham: Then the king sent to call Ahimelech, son of Ahitub, the priest, and all the house of his father—the priests, who were in Nob,—and they came, all of them, unto the king.
text.KJV: Then the king sent to call Ahimelech the priest, the son of Ahitub, and all his father’s house, the priests that were in Nob: and they came all of them to the king.
text.NABRE: Slaughter of the Priests. So the king summoned Ahimelech the priest, son of Ahitub, and all his family, the priests in Nob. They all came to the king.
text.DRB: The king therefore sent to call for Achimelech the Priest the son of Achitob & all his father's house, the Priests that were in Nobe, who came all to the king.

text.NRSV: Saul said, “Here now, son of Ahitub.” He answered, “Here I am, my lord.”
text.Rotherham: And Saul said, Hear, I pray thee, thou son of Ahitub! And he said—Behold me! my lord.
text.KJV: And Saul said, Hear now, thou son of Ahitub. And he answered, Here I am, my lord.
text.NABRE: “Listen, son of Ahitub!” Saul declared. “Yes, my lord,” he replied.
text.DRB: And Saul said to Achimelech: Hear thou son of Achitob. Who answered: I am ready, my Lord.

text.NRSV: Saul said to him, “Why have you and the son of Jesse conspired against me? Why have you given him bread and a sword, and have inquired of God for him, so that he has risen against me, lying in wait, as has happened today?”
text.Rotherham: And Saul said unto him, Wherefore have ye conspired against me, thou, and the son of Jesse,—in that thou gavest him bread and a sword, and didst enquire for him of God, that he might rise up against me, that he might lie in wait, as at this day?
text.KJV: And Saul said unto him, Why have ye conspired against me, thou and the son of Jesse, in that thou hast given him bread, and a sword, and hast enquired of God for him, that he should rise against me, to lie in wait, as at this day?
text.NABRE: Saul questioned him, “Why have you conspired against me with the son of Jesse by giving him food and a sword and by consulting God for him, that he might rise up against me in ambush, as is the case today?”
text.DRB: And Saul said to him: Why have you conspired against me, thou, and the son of Isai, and hast given him bread and a sword, & hast consulted our Lord for him, that he might rise up against me, continuing a traitor until this day?

text.NRSV: Then Ahimelech answered the king, “Who among all your servants is so faithful as David? He is the king’s son-in-law, captain of your bodyguard and respected in your house.
text.Rotherham: Then Ahimelech answered the king, and said,—But who, among all thy servants, like David, is, faithful, being son-in-law to the king, and, cometh near to have audience with thee, and is, honoured in thy household?
text.KJV: Then Ahimelech answered the king, and said, And who is so faithful among all thy servants as David, which is the king’s son in law, and goeth at thy bidding, and is honourable in thine house?
text.NABRE: Ahimelech answered the king: “Who among all your servants is as loyal as David, the king’s son-in-law, captain of your bodyguard, and honored in your own house?
text.DRB: And Achimelech answering the king, said: And who amongst all thy servants faithful as David, and the king's son in law, and going forth at thy commandment, & glorious in thy house?

text.NRSV: Is today the first time that I have inquired of God for him? By no means! Do not let the king impute anything to his servant or to any member of my father’s house; for your servant knew nothing of all this, much or little.”
text.Rotherham: Did I, that day, begin to enquire for him of God? Far from me! Let not the king impute to his servant such a thing, nor to any of the household of my father, for thy servant knoweth nothing of all this, less or more.
text.KJV: Did I then begin to enquire of God for him? be it far from me: let not the king impute any thing unto his servant, nor to all the house of my father: for thy servant knew nothing of all this, less or more.
text.NABRE: Is this the first time I have consulted God for him? No indeed! Let not the king accuse his servant or anyone in my family of such a thing. Your servant knows nothing at all, great or small, about the whole matter.”
text.DRB: Did I begin this day to consult our Lord for him? Far be this from me: let not the king suspect such a thing against his servant, in all the house of my father: for thy servant knew not any thing concerning this business, either little or great.

text.NRSV: The king said, “You shall surely die, Ahimelech, you and all your father’s house.”
text.Rotherham: And the king said: Thou shalt, die, Ahimelech,—thou and all the household of thy father.
text.KJV: And the king said, Thou shalt surely die, Ahimelech, thou, and all thy father’s house.
text.NABRE: But the king said, “You shall certainly die, Ahimelech, with all your family.”
text.DRB: And the king said: Dying thou shalt die Achimelech, thou, and all thy father's house.

text.NRSV: The king said to the guard who stood around him, “Turn and kill the priests of the Lord, because their loyalty is with David, and because they knew that he fled, and did not disclose it to me.” But the servants of the king would not raise a hand to attack the priests of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: And the king said unto the runners that were stationed by him—Turn ye round and put to death the priests of Yahweh, because, their hand also, is with David, and because they knew that he was, in flight, and unveiled not mine ear. But the servants of the king were not willing to thrust forth their hand, to fall upon the priests of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And the king said unto the footmen that stood about him, Turn, and slay the priests of the Lord; because their hand also is with David, and because they knew when he fled, and did not shew it to me. But the servants of the king would not put forth their hand to fall upon the priests of the Lord.
text.NABRE: The king then commanded his guards standing by him: “Turn and kill the priests of the Lord, for they gave David a hand. They knew he was a fugitive and yet failed to inform me.” But the king’s servants refused to raise a hand to strike the priests of the Lord.
text.DRB: And the king said to the couriers, that stood about him. Turn yourselves, and kill the Priests of our Lord, for their hand is with David, knowing that he was fled, and they told me not. And the king's servants would not extend their hands upon the Priests of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Then the king said to Doeg, “Turn and attack the priests.” Doeg the Edomite turned and attacked the priests; on that day he killed eighty-five who wore the linen ephod.
text.Rotherham: Then said the king to Doeg, Turn, thou, and fall upon the priests. So Doeg the Edomite turned, and, himself, fell upon the priests, and put to death, that day, four score and five men bearing an ephod of linen;
text.KJV: And the king said to Doeg, Turn thou, and fall upon the priests. And Doeg the Edomite turned, and he fell upon the priests, and slew on that day fourscore and five persons that did wear a linen ephod.
text.NABRE: The king therefore commanded Doeg, “You, turn and kill the priests!” So Doeg the Edomite himself turned and killed the priests that day—eighty-five who wore the linen ephod.
text.DRB: And the king said to Doeg: Turn thou, and run upon the Priests. And Doeg the Idumeite being turned, ran upon the Priests, and murdered in that day eighty five men revested with an ephod of linen.

text.NRSV: Nob, the city of the priests, he put to the sword; men and women, children and infants, oxen, donkeys, and sheep, he put to the sword.
text.Rotherham: Nob also, the city of the priests, smote he with the edge of the sword, both men and women, children and sucklings,—and oxen and asses and sheep, with the edge of the sword.
text.KJV: And Nob, the city of the priests, smote he with the edge of the sword, both men and women, children and sucklings, and oxen, and asses, and sheep, with the edge of the sword.
text.NABRE: Saul also put the priestly city of Nob to the sword, including men and women, children and infants, and oxen, donkeys and sheep.
text.DRB: And Nobe the city of the Priests he struck in the edge of the sword, men and women, and children, and sucklings, and oxe and ass and sheep in the edge of the sword.

text.NRSV: But one of the sons of Ahimelech son of Ahitub, named Abiathar, escaped and fled after David.
text.Rotherham: But there escaped one son of Ahimelech, son of Ahitub, whose, name, was Abiathar,—and he fled after David.
text.KJV: And one of the sons of Ahimelech the son of Ahitub, named Abiathar, escaped, and fled after David.
text.NABRE: Abiathar Escapes. One son of Ahimelech, son of Ahitub, named Abiathar, escaped and fled to David.
text.DRB: But one son of Achimelech the son of Achitob, escaping, whose name was Abiathar, fled to David,

text.NRSV: Abiathar told David that Saul had killed the priests of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So Abiathar told David,—that Saul had slain the priests of Yahweh.
text.KJV: And Abiathar shewed David that Saul had slain the Lord’s priests.
text.NABRE: When Abiathar told David that Saul had slain the priests of the Lord,
text.DRB: and told him that Saul had slain the Priests of our Lord.

text.NRSV: David said to Abiathar, “I knew on that day, when Doeg the Edomite was there, that he would be sure to tell Saul. I am responsible for the lives of all your father’s house.
text.Rotherham: Then said David to Abiathar—I knew, that day, when Doeg the Edomite was, there, that he would, surely tell, Saul. I, am chargeable with all the lives of the house of thy father.
text.KJV: And David said unto Abiathar, I knew it that day, when Doeg the Edomite was there, that he would surely tell Saul: I have occasioned the death of all the persons of thy father’s house.
text.NABRE: David said to him: “I knew that day, when Doeg the Edomite was there, that he would certainly tell Saul. I am responsible for the slaughter of all your family.
text.DRB: And David said to Abiathar: I knew in that day when Doeg the Idumeite was there, without doubt he would tell Saul, I am guilty of all the souls of thy father.

text.NRSV: Stay with me, and do not be afraid; for the one who seeks my life seeks your life; you will be safe with me.”
text.Rotherham: Abide with me! do not fear, for, whoso seeketh my life, seeketh thy life,—for, in safeguard, shall thou be, with me.
text.KJV: Abide thou with me, fear not: for he that seeketh my life seeketh thy life: but with me thou shalt be in safeguard.
text.NABRE: Stay with me. Do not be afraid; whoever seeks your life must seek my life also. You are under my protection.”
text.DRB: Abide with me, fear not: if any man shall seek my life, he shall seek thy life also, and with me thou shall be preserved.

text.NRSV: Now they told David, “Look, the Philistines are fighting against Keilah, and are robbing the threshing floors.”
text.Rotherham: Then told they David, saying,—Lo! the Philistines are fighting against Keilah, and they are plundering the threshing-floors.
text.KJV: Then they told David, saying, Behold, the Philistines fight against Keilah, and they rob the threshingfloors.
text.NABRE: David was informed that the Philistines were attacking Keilah and plundering the threshing floors.
text.DRB: And they told David, saying: Behold the Philistiims oppugn Ceila, and spoil the barns.

text.NRSV: David inquired of the Lord, “Shall I go and attack these Philistines?” The Lord answered David, “Go and attack the Philistines and save Keilah.”
text.Rotherham: So David enquired of Yahweh, saying, Shall I go and smite these Philistines? And Yahweh said unto David—Go, and smite the Philistines, and save Keilah.
text.KJV: Therefore David enquired of the Lord, saying, Shall I go and smite these Philistines? And the Lord said unto David, Go, and smite the Philistines, and save Keilah.
text.NABRE: So he consulted the Lord, asking, “Shall I go and attack these Philistines?” The Lord answered, Go, attack them, and free Keilah.
text.DRB: David therefore consulted our Lord, saying: Shall I go, and strike these Philistians? And our Lord said to David: Go, and thou shalt strike the Philistians, and shalt save Ceila.

text.NRSV: But David’s men said to him, “Look, we are already afraid here in Judah; how much more then if we go to Keilah against the armies of the Philistines?”
text.Rotherham: But the men of David said unto him, Lo! we, here, in Judah, are afraid,—how much more, then, if we go to Keilah, against the ranks of the Philistines?
text.KJV: And David’s men said unto him, Behold, we be afraid here in Judah: how much more then if we come to Keilah against the armies of the Philistines?
text.NABRE: But David’s men said to him: “Even in Judah we have reason to fear. How much more so if we go to Keilah against the forces of the Philistines!”
text.DRB: And the men that were with David, said to him: Behold we resting here in Jury are afraid, how much more if we shall go into Ceila against the bands of the Philistians?

text.NRSV: Then David inquired of the Lord again. The Lord answered him, “Yes, go down to Keilah; for I will give the Philistines into your hand.”
text.Rotherham: So David yet again enquired if Yahweh, and Yahweh answered him,—and said—Arise, go down to Keilah, for I am about to deliver the Philistines into thy hand.
text.KJV: Then David enquired of the Lord yet again. And the Lord answered him and said, Arise, go down to Keilah; for I will deliver the Philistines into thine hand.
text.NABRE: Again David consulted the Lord, who answered: Go down to Keilah, for I will deliver the Philistines into your power.
text.DRB: Again therefore David consulted our Lord. Who answering said to him: Arise, and go into Ceila: for I will deliver the Philistians in thy hand.

text.NRSV: So David and his men went to Keilah, fought with the Philistines, brought away their livestock, and dealt them a heavy blow. So David rescued the inhabitants of Keilah.
text.Rotherham: David therefore went, with his men, to Keilah, and fought with the Philistines, and drave forth their cattle, and smote them, with a great smiting, so David saved the inhabitants of Keilah.
text.KJV: So David and his men went to Keilah, and fought with the Philistines, and brought away their cattle, and smote them with a great slaughter. So David saved the inhabitants of Keilah.
text.NABRE: So David went with his men to Keilah and fought against the Philistines. He drove off their cattle and inflicted a severe defeat on them, and freed the inhabitants of Keilah.
text.DRB: David therefore and his men went into Ceila, and fought against the Philistians, and drove away their beasts, and struck them with a great slaughter, and David saved the inhabitants of Ceila.

text.NRSV: When Abiathar son of Ahimelech fled to David at Keilah, he came down with an ephod in his hand.
text.Rotherham: Now it came to pass, when Abiathar, son of Ahimelech, fled unto David to Keilah, that he came down with, an ephod, in his hand.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when Abiathar the son of Ahimelech fled to David to Keilah, that he came down with an ephod in his hand.
text.NABRE: Abiathar, son of Ahimelech, who had fled to David, went down with David to Keilah, taking the ephod with him.
text.DRB: Moreover at that time, when Abiathar the son of Achimelech fled to David into Ceila, he went down having with him an ephod.

text.NRSV: Now it was told Saul that David had come to Keilah. And Saul said, “God has given him into my hand; for he has shut himself in by entering a town that has gates and bars.”
text.Rotherham: And, when it was told Saul that David had entered Keilah, Saul said—God hath given him over into my hand, for he hath shut himself in by entering into a city with folding doors and bar.
text.KJV: And it was told Saul that David was come to Keilah. And Saul said, God hath delivered him into mine hand; for he is shut in, by entering into a town that hath gates and bars.
text.NABRE: Flight from Keilah. When Saul was told that David had entered Keilah, he thought: “God has put him in my hand, for he has boxed himself in by entering a city with gates and bars.”
text.DRB: And it was told Saul that David was come into Ceila: and Saul said: Our Lord hath delivered him into my hands, & he is shut up being entered the city, where in are gates and locks.

text.NRSV: Saul summoned all the people for war, to go down to Keilah, to besiege David and his men.
text.Rotherham: So Saul summoned all the people to war,—to go down to Keilah, to besiege David, and his men.
text.KJV: And Saul called all the people together to war, to go down to Keilah, to besiege David and his men.
text.NABRE: Saul then called all the army to war, in order to go down to Keilah and besiege David and his men.
text.DRB: And Saul commanded all the people, that they should go down into Ceila to fight, and besiege David and his men.

text.NRSV: When David learned that Saul was plotting evil against him, he said to the priest Abiathar, “Bring the ephod here.”
text.Rotherham: And David ascertained that, against him, Saul was contriving mischief,—so he said unto Abiathar the priest, Bring hither the ephod.
text.KJV: And David knew that Saul secretly practised mischief against him; and he said to Abiathar the priest, Bring hither the ephod.
text.NABRE: When David found out that Saul was planning to harm him, he said to the priest Abiathar, “Bring the ephod here.”
text.DRB: Which when David understood, that Saul secretly prepared evil against him, he said to Abiathar the Priest: Apply the Ephod.

text.NRSV: David said, “O Lord, the God of Israel, your servant has heard that Saul seeks to come to Keilah, to destroy the city on my account.
text.Rotherham: Then said David: O Yahweh, God of Israel, thy servant, hath heard, that Saul is seeking to come unto Keilah,—to destroy the city, for my sake:
text.KJV: Then said David, O Lord God of Israel, thy servant hath certainly heard that Saul seeketh to come to Keilah, to destroy the city for my sake.
text.NABRE: “ Lord God of Israel,” David prayed, “your servant has heard that Saul plans to come to Keilah, to destroy the city on my account.
text.DRB: And David said: Lord God of Israel, thy servant hath heard a bruit, that Saul determineth to come into Ceila, to destroy the city for me:

text.NRSV: And now the citizens of Keilah will surrender me into his hand; will Saul come down, as your servant has heard? O Lord, the God of Israel, I pray you, tell your servant.” The Lord said, “He will come down.”
text.Rotherham: Will the owners of Keilah surrender me into his hand? Will Saul come down, as thy servant hath heard? O Yahweh, God of Israel, I pray thee, tell thy servant. And Yahweh said—He will come down.
text.KJV: Will the men of Keilah deliver me up into his hand? will Saul come down, as thy servant hath heard? O Lord God of Israel, I beseech thee, tell thy servant. And the Lord said, He will come down.
text.NABRE: Will they hand me over? Will Saul come down as your servant has heard? Lord God of Israel, tell your servant.” The Lord answered: He will come down.
text.DRB: Will the men of Ceila deliver me into his hands? And will Saul come down, as thy servant hath heard? Lord God of Israel tell thy servant. And our Lord said: He will come down.

text.NRSV: Then David said, “Will the citizens of Keilah surrender me and my men into the hand of Saul?” The Lord said, “They will surrender you.”
text.Rotherham: Then said David, Will the owners of Keilah surrender me and my men, into the hand of Saul? And Yahweh said—They will surrender.
text.KJV: Then said David, Will the men of Keilah deliver me and my men into the hand of Saul? And the Lord said, They will deliver thee up.
text.NABRE: David then asked, “Will the citizens of Keilah deliver me and my men into the hand of Saul?” The Lord answered: They will deliver you.
text.DRB: And David said: Will the men of Ceila deliver me, and the men that are with me, into the hands of Saul? And our Lord said: They will deliver thee.

text.NRSV: Then David and his men, who were about six hundred, set out and left Keilah; they moved about among the strongholds.
text.Rotherham: Then arose David and his men, about six hundred, and went forth out of Keilah, and went to and fro, whithersoever they could,—and, unto Saul, it was told that David had escaped out of Keilah, so he forbare to go forth.
text.KJV: Then David and his men, which were about six hundred, arose and departed out of Keilah, and went whithersoever they could go. And it was told Saul that David was escaped from Keilah; and he forbare to go forth.
text.NABRE: So David and his men, about six hundred in number, left Keilah and wandered from place to place. When Saul was informed that David had fled from Keilah, he did not go forth.
text.DRB: David therefore arose and his men, about six hundred, and going out of Ceila, wandered hither and thither uncertain: and it was told Saul that David was fled from Ceila, and was saved: for which cause he dissembled to go forth.

text.NRSV: David remained in the strongholds of the wilderness in the hill country of the Wilderness of Ziph. Saul sought him every day, but the Lord did not give him into his hand.
text.Rotherham: Then David abode in the wilderness, in the fortresses, and he abode in the hill country, in the wilderness of Ziph. And Saul sought him continually, but God delivered him not into his hand.
text.KJV: And David abode in the wilderness in strong holds, and remained in a mountain in the wilderness of Ziph. And Saul sought him every day, but God delivered him not into his hand.
text.NABRE: David and Jonathan in Horesh. David now lived in the strongholds in the wilderness, or in the barren hill country near Ziph. Though Saul sought him continually, the Lord did not deliver David into his hand.
text.DRB: But David abode in the desert in most strong places, & he tarried in the mount of the desert Ziph, in a shadowed hill. Saul notwithstanding sought him always: and our Lord delivered him not into his hands.

text.NRSV: David was in the Wilderness of Ziph at Horesh when he learned that Saul had come out to seek his life.
text.Rotherham: Then David saw that Saul had come out to seek his life, David, being in the wilderness of Ziph, in the thicket.
text.KJV: And David saw that Saul was come out to seek his life: and David was in the wilderness of Ziph in a wood.
text.NABRE: While David was in the wilderness of Ziph at Horesh he was afraid that Saul had come out to seek his life.
text.DRB: And David saw that Saul was gone forth to seek his life. Moreover David was in the desert Ziph, in a wood.

text.NRSV: Saul’s son Jonathan set out and came to David at Horesh; there he strengthened his hand in God.
text.Rotherham: So Jonathan, Saul’s son, arose and went unto David in the thicket,—and strengthened his hand in God;
text.KJV: And Jonathan Saul’s son arose, and went to David into the wood, and strengthened his hand in God.
text.NABRE: Then Saul’s son, Jonathan, came down to David at Horesh and encouraged him in the Lord.
text.DRB: And Jonathas the son of Saul arose, and went to David into the wood, and strengthened his hands in God, and said to him:

text.NRSV: He said to him, “Do not be afraid; for my father Saul shall not find you; you shall be king over Israel, and I shall be second to you; my father Saul also knows that.”
text.Rotherham: and said unto him,—Do not fear, for the hand of Saul my father shall not find thee, but, thou, shalt become king over Israel, and, I, shall be, next, unto thee, yea and, Saul my father, knoweth this.
text.KJV: And he said unto him, Fear not: for the hand of Saul my father shall not find thee; and thou shalt be king over Israel, and I shall be next unto thee; and that also Saul my father knoweth.
text.NABRE: He said to him: “Have no fear, my father Saul shall not lay a hand to you. You shall be king of Israel and I shall be second to you. Even my father Saul knows this.”
text.DRB: Fear not; for neither shall the hand of Saul my father find thee, and thou shalt reign over Israel, & I shall be second to thee, yea and my father knoweth this.

text.NRSV: So the two of them made a covenant before the Lord; and David remained at Horesh, and Jonathan went home.
text.Rotherham: And they two solemnised a covenant before Yahweh,—and David remained in the thicket, but, Jonathan, departed to his own house.
text.KJV: And they two made a covenant before the Lord: and David abode in the wood, and Jonathan went to his house.
text.NABRE: The two of them made a covenant before the Lord in Horesh, where David remained, while Jonathan returned to his home.
text.DRB: Both therefore made a league before our Lord: and David abode in the wood: but Jonathas returned into his house.

text.NRSV: Then the Ziphites went up to Saul at Gibeah, saying, “David is hiding among us in the strongholds of Horesh on the hill of Hachilah, which is south of Jeshimon.
text.Rotherham: Then came up the Ziphites unto Saul in Gibeah, saying,—Is not David hiding himself with us, in the strongholds in the thicket, in the hill of Hachilah, which is on the right of Jeshimon?
text.KJV: Then came up the Ziphites to Saul to Gibeah, saying, Doth not David hide himself with us in strong holds in the wood, in the hill of Hachilah, which is on the south of Jeshimon?
text.NABRE: Treachery of the Ziphites. Some of the Ziphites went up to Saul in Gibeah and said, “David is hiding among us in the strongholds at Horesh on the hill of Hachilah, south of Jeshimon.
text.DRB: And the Zipheites went up unto Saul in Gabaa, saying: Lo, doth not David lie hid with us in the most safe places of the wood, in the hill Hachila, which is on the right hand of the desert?

text.NRSV: Now, O king, whenever you wish to come down, do so; and our part will be to surrender him into the king’s hand.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, according to all the desire of thy soul O king, to come down, come! and, ours, [will it be] to surrender him into the hand of the king.
text.KJV: Now therefore, O king, come down according to all the desire of thy soul to come down; and our part shall be to deliver him into the king’s hand.
text.NABRE: Therefore, whenever the king wishes to come down, let him do so. It will be our task to deliver him into the king’s hand.”
text.DRB: Now therefore, as thy soul hath desired, come down, & it shall be our charge to deliver him into the king's hands.

text.NRSV: Saul said, “May you be blessed by the Lord; for you have had compassion on me.
text.Rotherham: Then said Saul, Blessed, be ye, of Yahweh,—because ye have taken pity upon me.
text.KJV: And Saul said, Blessed be ye of the Lord; for ye have compassion on me.
text.NABRE: Saul replied: “The Lord bless you for your compassion toward me.
text.DRB: And Saul said: Blessed be ye of our Lord, because you have pitied my case.

text.NRSV: Go and make sure once more; look and see the place where he usually stations himself, and who has seen him there; for I have been told that he is very cunning.
text.Rotherham: Go, I pray you, make ready yet further, and get to know and see his place, where may be his track, who hath seen him there,—for it hath been said unto me, Cunning indeed, is, he!
text.KJV: Go, I pray you, prepare yet, and know and see his place where his haunt is, and who hath seen him there: for it is told me that he dealeth very subtilly.
text.NABRE: Go now and make sure once more! Take note of the place where he sets foot for I am told that he is very cunning.
text.DRB: Go therefore I pray you, and prepare diligently, and deal curiously, and consider the place, where his foot is, and who hath seen him there, for he thinketh of me, that I craftily lie in wait for him.

text.NRSV: Look around and see all the hiding places where he hides, and come back to me with sure information. Then I will go with you; and if he is in the land, I will track him down among all the thousands of Judah.”
text.Rotherham: See, then, and get to know—of all the hiding places, where he hideth himself, and return unto me, for certainty, then will I go with you,—and it shall be, if he is in the land, that I will search him out, through all the thousands of Judah.
text.KJV: See therefore, and take knowledge of all the lurking places where he hideth himself, and come ye again to me with the certainty, and I will go with you: and it shall come to pass, if he be in the land, that I will search him out throughout all the thousands of Judah.
text.NABRE: Look around and learn in which of all the various hiding places he is holding out. Then come back to me with reliable information, and I will go with you. If he is in the region, I will track him down out of all the families of Judah.”
text.DRB: Consider and see all his lurking holes, wherein he is hid, and return to me with the certainty of the thing, that I may go with you. Yea and if he shall stop up himself into the earth, I will search him out among all the thousands of Juda.

text.NRSV: So they set out and went to Ziph ahead of Saul. Now David and his men were in the wilderness of Maon, in the Arabah to the south of Jeshimon.
text.Rotherham: So they arose and departed to Ziph, before Saul,—but, David and his men, were in the wilderness of Maon, in the Arabah, to the right of Jeshimon.
text.KJV: And they arose, and went to Ziph before Saul: but David and his men were in the wilderness of Maon, in the plain on the south of Jeshimon.
text.NABRE: So they went off to Ziph ahead of Saul. At this time David and his men were in the wilderness below Maon, in the Arabah south of the wasteland.
text.DRB: But they rising went into Ziph before Saul: and David and his men were in the desert Maon, in the champion country at the right hand of Jesimon.

text.NRSV: Saul and his men went to search for him. When David was told, he went down to the rock and stayed in the wilderness of Maon. When Saul heard that, he pursued David into the wilderness of Maon.
text.Rotherham: Then Saul and his men departed, to seek [him]; but it had been told David, and he had gone down the cliff, and taken up his abode in the wilderness of Maon,—and when Saul had, heard it, he pursued David in the wilderness of Maon.
text.KJV: Saul also and his men went to seek him. And they told David: wherefore he came down into a rock, and abode in the wilderness of Maon. And when Saul heard that, he pursued after David in the wilderness of Maon.
text.NABRE: Escape from Saul. When Saul and his men came looking for him, David got word of it and went down to the gorge in the wilderness below Maon. Saul heard of this and pursued David into the wilderness below Maon.
text.DRB: Saul therefore and his company went to seek him: and it was told David, and forthwith he went down to the rock, and abode in the desert Maon, which when Saul had heard, he pursued David in the desert Maon.

text.NRSV: Saul went on one side of the mountain, and David and his men on the other side of the mountain. David was hurrying to get away from Saul, while Saul and his men were closing in on David and his men, to capture them.
text.Rotherham: And Saul went on this side of the mountain, and, David and his men, on that side of the mountain,—wherefore David became hurried, to get away from the presence of Saul, but, Saul and his men, were surrounding David and his men, to capture then:
text.KJV: And Saul went on this side of the mountain, and David and his men on that side of the mountain: and David made haste to get away for fear of Saul; for Saul and his men compassed David and his men round about to take them.
text.NABRE: As Saul moved along one side of the gorge, David and his men took to the other. David was anxious to escape Saul, while Saul and his men were trying to outflank David and his men in order to capture them.
text.DRB: And Saul went at the side of the mountain on the one part: and David and his men were in the side of the mountain on the other part: moreover David despaired that he could escape from the face of Saul: Saul therefore & his men in manner of a ring compassed David & his men, to take them.

text.NRSV: Then a messenger came to Saul, saying, “Hurry and come; for the Philistines have made a raid on the land.”
text.Rotherham: when, a messenger, came unto Saul, saying,—Haste thee and come! for the Philistines have spread over the land.
text.KJV: But there came a messenger unto Saul, saying, Haste thee, and come; for the Philistines have invaded the land.
text.NABRE: Then a messenger came to Saul, saying, “Come quickly, because the Philistines have invaded the land.”
text.DRB: And a messenger came to Saul, saying: Make haste, and come, because the Philistiims have poured in themselves upon the land.

text.NRSV: So Saul returned from pursuing David and went to meet the Philistines; therefore that place was called Sela-hammahlekoth.
text.Rotherham: Then returned Saul from pursuing David, and departed to meet the Philistines,—for which cause, they called that place Sela-hammahlekoth ["the Cliff of Separation"].
text.KJV: Wherefore Saul returned from pursuing after David, and went against the Philistines: therefore they called that place Sela–hammahlekoth.
text.NABRE: Saul interrupted his pursuit of David and went to meet the Philistines. This is how that place came to be called the Rock of Divisions.
text.DRB: Saul therefore returned leaving off to pursue David, and he went to meet the Philistians. For this cause they called that place, The Rock dividing.

text.NRSV: David then went up from there and lived in the strongholds of En-gedi.
text.Rotherham: And David went up from thence,—and abode in the strongholds of En-gedi.
text.KJV: And David went up from thence, and dwelt in strong holds at En–gedi.
text.NABRE: David then went up from there and stayed in the strongholds of Engedi.

text.NRSV: When Saul returned from following the Philistines, he was told, “David is in the wilderness of En-gedi.”
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when Saul had returned from following the Philistines, that it was told him, saying: Lo! David is in the wilderness of En-gedi.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when Saul was returned from following the Philistines, that it was told him, saying, Behold, David is in the wilderness of En–gedi.
text.NABRE: David then went up from there and stayed in the strongholds of Engedi.
text.DRB: David therefore went up from thence, and dwelt in the safest places of Engaddi.

text.NRSV: Then Saul took three thousand chosen men out of all Israel, and went to look for David and his men in the direction of the Rocks of the Wild Goats.
text.Rotherham: Then Saul took three thousand chosen men, out of all Israel,—and went to seek David and his men, over the face of the rocks of the mountain-goats.
text.KJV: Then Saul took three thousand chosen men out of all Israel, and went to seek David and his men upon the rocks of the wild goats.
text.NABRE: When Saul returned from the pursuit of the Philistines, he was told that David was in the desert near Engedi.
text.DRB: And when Saul was returned, after he pursued the Philistians, they told him, saying: Behold, David is in the desert Engaddi.

text.NRSV: He came to the sheepfolds beside the road, where there was a cave; and Saul went in to relieve himself. Now David and his men were sitting in the innermost parts of the cave.
text.Rotherham: And he came into the sheep-folds by the way, there, being a cave, which Saul entered, to cover his feet,—David and his men, in the hinder part of the cave, having taken up their abode.
text.KJV: And he came to the sheepcotes by the way, where was a cave; and Saul went in to cover his feet: and David and his men remained in the sides of the cave.
text.NABRE: So Saul took three thousand of the best men from all Israel and went in search of David and his men in the direction of the wild goat crags.
text.DRB: Saul therefore taking unto him three thousand chosen men of all Israel, went forth to search out David & his men, yea over the steep broken rocks, which are accessible only to wild goats.

text.NRSV: The men of David said to him, “Here is the day of which the Lord said to you, ‘I will give your enemy into your hand, and you shall do to him as it seems good to you.’” Then David went and stealthily cut off a corner of Saul’s cloak.
text.Rotherham: So David’s men said unto him—Lo! the day of which Yahweh said unto thee—Lo! I am about to deliver up thine enemy, into thy hand: therefore shall thou do unto him, as shall be good in thine eyes. And David arose, and cut off the corner of the robe which belonged to Saul, by stealth.
text.KJV: And the men of David said unto him, Behold the day of which the Lord said unto thee, Behold, I will deliver thine enemy into thine hand, that thou mayest do to him as it shall seem good unto thee. Then David arose, and cut off the skirt of Saul’s robe privily.
text.NABRE: When he came to the sheepfolds along the way, he found a cave, which he entered to relieve himself. David and his men were occupying the inmost recesses of the cave.
text.DRB: And he came to the sheepcotes, which fell in his way as he went, and there was a cave, which Saul entered into, to do his easement: moreover David and his men lay hid in the inner part of the cave.

text.NRSV: Afterward David was stricken to the heart because he had cut off a corner of Saul’s cloak.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass afterwards that David’s heart smote him,—because he had cut off the corner [of the robe] which belonged to Saul.
text.KJV: And it came to pass afterward, that David’s heart smote him, because he had cut off Saul’s skirt.
text.NABRE: David’s servants said to him, “This is the day about which the Lord said to you: I will deliver your enemy into your hand; do with him as you see fit.” So David moved up and stealthily cut off an end of Saul’s robe.
text.DRB: And the servants of David said to him: Behold the day, whereof our Lord said to thee: I will deliver thee thine enemy, that thou mayest do to him as it shall seem good in thine eyes. David therefore arose, and cut off the hem of Saul's cloak softly.

text.NRSV: He said to his men, “The Lord forbid that I should do this thing to my lord, the Lord’s anointed, to raise my hand against him, for he is the Lord’s anointed.”
text.Rotherham: And he said unto his men—Far be it from me, of Yahweh, that I should do this thing unto my lord, unto the Anointed of Yahweh, to thrust forth my hand against him,—for, the Anointed of Yahweh, is he!
text.KJV: And he said unto his men, The Lord forbid that I should do this thing unto my master, the Lord’s anointed, to stretch forth mine hand against him, seeing he is the anointed of the Lord.
text.NABRE: Afterward, however, David regretted that he had cut off an end of Saul’s robe.
text.DRB: After this David's heart struck him, for that he had cut off the hem of Saul's cloak.

text.NRSV: So David rebuked his men severely and did not permit them to attack Saul. Then Saul got up and left the cave, and went on his way.
text.Rotherham: So David did chide his men with the words, and did not suffer them to rise up against Saul. And, Saul, rose up out of the cave, and went on his way.
text.KJV: So David stayed his servants with these words, and suffered them not to rise against Saul. But Saul rose up out of the cave, and went on his way.
text.NABRE: He said to his men, “The Lord forbid that I should do such a thing to my master, the Lord ’s anointed, to lay a hand on him, for he is the Lord ’s anointed.”
text.DRB: And he said to his men: Our Lord be merciful unto me, that I do not this thing to my master the anointed of our Lord, that I should lay my hand upon him, because he is the anointed of our Lord.

text.NRSV: Afterward David also rose up and went out of the cave and called after Saul, “My lord the king!” When Saul looked behind him, David bowed with his face to the ground, and did obeisance.
text.Rotherham: Then David rose up, afterwards, and went forth out of the cave, and cried out after Saul, saying—My lord, O king! And, when Saul looked about behind him, David inclined his face to the earth, and bowed himself down.
text.KJV: David also arose afterward, and went out of the cave, and cried after Saul, saying, My lord the king. And when Saul looked behind him, David stooped with his face to the earth, and bowed himself.
text.NABRE: With these words David restrained his men and would not permit them to attack Saul. Saul then left the cave and went on his way.
text.DRB: And David persuaded his men with words, and permitted them not to rise against Saul: moreover Saul rising out of the cave, went on his journey begun.

text.NRSV: David said to Saul, “Why do you listen to the words of those who say, ‘David seeks to do you harm’?
text.Rotherham: Then said David to Saul, Wherefore shouldst thou hearken unto the words of the sons of earth, saying,—Lo! David is seeking thy hurt?
text.KJV: And David said to Saul, Wherefore hearest thou men’s words, saying, Behold, David seeketh thy hurt?
text.NABRE: David also stepped out of the cave, calling to Saul, “My lord the king!” When Saul looked back, David bowed, his face to the ground in homage,
text.DRB: And David rose up after him: and going out of the cave, cried behind Saul's back, saying: My lord king. And Saul looked back behind him: and David bowing himself flat toward the ground adored,

text.NRSV: This very day your eyes have seen how the Lord gave you into my hand in the cave; and some urged me to kill you, but I spared you. I said, ‘I will not raise my hand against my lord; for he is the Lord’s anointed.’
text.Rotherham: Lo! this day, have thine own eyes seen, how Yahweh had delivered thee up, to-day, into my hand in the cave, and, when one bade me slay thee, I looked with compassion upon thee,—and I said—I will not thrust forth my hand against my lord, for, the Anointed of Yahweh, is he!
text.KJV: Behold, this day thine eyes have seen how that the Lord had delivered thee to day into mine hand in the cave: and some bade me kill thee: but mine eye spared thee; and I said, I will not put forth mine hand against my lord; for he is the Lord’s anointed.
text.NABRE: and asked Saul: “Why do you listen to those who say, ‘David is trying to harm you’?
text.DRB: and said to Saul: Why dost thou hear the words of men that say: David seeketh evil against thee?

text.NRSV: See, my father, see the corner of your cloak in my hand; for by the fact that I cut off the corner of your cloak, and did not kill you, you may know for certain that there is no wrong or treason in my hands. I have not sinned against you, though you are hunting me to take my life.
text.Rotherham: But, my father, see, yea, see, the corner of thy robe in my hand,—for, in that I cut off the corner of thy robe, and yet did not slay thee, know thou, and see, that there is not in my hand either wrong or transgression, neither have I sinned against thee, yet art thou hunting my life, to take it.
text.KJV: Moreover, my father, see, yea, see the skirt of thy robe in my hand: for in that I cut off the skirt of thy robe, and killed thee not, know thou and see that there is neither evil nor transgression in mine hand, and I have not sinned against thee; yet thou huntest my soul to take it.
text.NABRE: You see for yourself today that the Lord just now delivered you into my hand in the cave. I was told to kill you, but I took pity on you instead. I decided, ‘I will not raise a hand against my master, for he is the Lord ’s anointed.’
text.DRB: Lo this day thine eyes have seen, that our Lord delivered thee in my hand, in the cave, and I had a cogitation to kill thee, but mine eye hath spared thee. For I said: I will not extend mine hand upon my Lord, because he is the anointed of our Lord.

text.NRSV: May the Lord judge between me and you! May the Lord avenge me on you; but my hand shall not be against you.
text.Rotherham: Let Yahweh do justice betwixt me and thee, and let Yahweh avenge me of thee,—but, mine own hand, shall not be upon thee.
text.KJV: The Lord judge between me and thee, and the Lord avenge me of thee: but mine hand shall not be upon thee.
text.NABRE: Look here, my father. See the end of your robe which I hold. I cut off an end of your robe and did not kill you. Now see and be convinced that I plan no harm and no rebellion. I have done you no wrong, though you are hunting me down to take my life.
text.DRB: But rather see and know, o my father, the hem of thy cloak in my hand, that when I did cut off the hem of thy cloak, I would not extend my hand upon thee. Mark, and see, that there is no evil in my hand, nor iniquity, neither have I sinned against thee: but thou liest in wait for my life, to take it away.

text.NRSV: As the ancient proverb says, ‘Out of the wicked comes forth wickedness’; but my hand shall not be against you.
text.Rotherham: As saith the proverb of the ancients, From the lawless, proceedeth lawlessness,—mine own hand, therefore shall not be upon thee.
text.KJV: As saith the proverb of the ancients, Wickedness proceedeth from the wicked: but mine hand shall not be upon thee.
text.NABRE: May the Lord judge between me and you. May the Lord exact justice from you in my case. I shall not lay a hand on you.
text.DRB: Our Lord judge between me and thee, and our Lord revenge me of thee, but be not my hand upon thee.

text.NRSV: Against whom has the king of Israel come out? Whom do you pursue? A dead dog? A single flea?
text.Rotherham: After whom, hath the king of Israel come forth? After whom, art thou in pursuit? After a dead dog! after a single flea!
text.KJV: After whom is the king of Israel come out? after whom dost thou pursue? after a dead dog, after a flea.
text.NABRE: As the old proverb says, ‘From the wicked comes wickedness.’ Thus I will not lay a hand on you.
text.DRB: As also it is said in the old proverb: From The Impious shall impiety proceed: be not therefore my hand upon thee: Whom dost thou persecute, o king of Israel?

text.NRSV: May the Lord therefore be judge, and give sentence between me and you. May he see and plead my cause, and vindicate me against you.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall, Yahweh, become, judge, and do justice betwixt me and thee,—that he may see, and plead my cause, and justly deliver me out of thy hand.
text.KJV: The Lord therefore be judge, and judge between me and thee, and see, and plead my cause, and deliver me out of thine hand.
text.NABRE: What is the king of Israel attacking? What are you pursuing? A dead dog! A single flea!
text.DRB: Whom dost thou persecute? Thou persecutest a dead dog, and a flea.

text.NRSV: When David had finished speaking these words to Saul, Saul said, “Is this your voice, my son David?” Saul lifted up his voice and wept.
text.Rotherham: And it came to pass, when David had made an end of speaking these words unto Saul, that Saul said, Thy voice, is this, my son David? And Saul lifted up his voice, and wept.
text.KJV: And it came to pass, when David had made an end of speaking these words unto Saul, that Saul said, Is this thy voice, my son David? And Saul lifted up his voice, and wept.
text.NABRE: The Lord will be the judge to decide between us. May the Lord see this, defend my cause, and give me justice against you!”
text.DRB: Our Lord be judge, and judge between me and thee, and he see, and judge my cause, and deliver me out of thy hand.

text.NRSV: He said to David, “You are more righteous than I; for you have repaid me good, whereas I have repaid you evil.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto David, More righteous, art thou than I, for, thou, hast requited me, good, but, I, have requited thee, evil.
text.KJV: And he said to David, Thou art more righteous than I: for thou hast rewarded me good, whereas I have rewarded thee evil.
text.NABRE: Saul’s Remorse. When David finished saying these things to Saul, Saul answered, “Is that your voice, my son David?” And he wept freely.
text.DRB: And when David had fully ended speaking such words to Saul, Saul said: Is this thy voice my son David? And Saul lifted up his voice, and wept:

text.NRSV: Today you have explained how you have dealt well with me, in that you did not kill me when the Lord put me into your hand.
text.Rotherham: Thou, then, hast told to-day, how thou hast dealt with me, for good,—how, when Yahweh had surrendered me into thy hand, thou didst not slay me.
text.KJV: And thou hast shewed this day how that thou hast dealt well with me: forasmuch as when the Lord had delivered me into thine hand, thou killedst me not.
text.NABRE: Saul then admitted to David: “You are more in the right than I am. You have treated me graciously, while I have treated you badly.
text.DRB: and said to David: Thou art juster than I: for thou hast done me good turns, and I have rendered thee evil.

text.NRSV: For who has ever found an enemy, and sent the enemy safely away? So may the Lord reward you with good for what you have done to me this day.
text.Rotherham: Yet, when a man findeth his enemy, will he let him get easily away? Yahweh, then, give thee, good, reward for what, this day, thou hast done unto me.
text.KJV: For if a man find his enemy, will he let him go well away? wherefore the Lord reward thee good for that thou hast done unto me this day.
text.NABRE: You have declared this day how you treated me graciously: the Lord delivered me into your hand and you did not kill me.
text.DRB: And thou hast shewed this day what good things thou hast done to me: how our Lord delivered me into thy hand, and thou hast not killed me.

text.NRSV: Now I know that you shall surely be king, and that the kingdom of Israel shall be established in your hand.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, lo! I know that thou, shalt indeed become king,—and that the kingdom of Israel, shall be established in thy hand.
text.KJV: And now, behold, I know well that thou shalt surely be king, and that the kingdom of Israel shall be established in thine hand.
text.NABRE: For if someone comes upon an enemy, do they send them graciously on their way? So may the Lord reward you graciously for what you have done this day.
text.DRB: For who when he hath found his enemy, will let him go in a good way? But our Lord render thee this good turn, for that which thou hast wrought toward me this day.

text.NRSV: Swear to me therefore by the Lord that you will not cut off my descendants after me, and that you will not wipe out my name from my father’s house.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, swear unto me by Yahweh, that thou wilt not eat off my seed, after me,—and wilt not destroy my name out of the house my father.
text.KJV: Swear now therefore unto me by the Lord, that thou wilt not cut off my seed after me, and that thou wilt not destroy my name out of my father’s house.
text.NABRE: And now, since I know that you will certainly become king and that the kingship over Israel shall come into your possession,
text.DRB: And now because I know that thou most certainly shalt reign, and have the Kingdom of Israel in thy hand:

text.NRSV: So David swore this oath to Saul. Then Saul went home; but David and his men went up to the stronghold.
text.Rotherham: So David sware unto Saul,—and Saul departed unto his own house, but, David and his men, went up on the stronghold.
text.KJV: And David sware unto Saul. And Saul went home; but David and his men gat them up unto the hold.
text.NABRE: swear to me by the Lord that you will not cut off my descendants and that you will not blot out my name from my father’s house.”
text.DRB: swear to me in our Lord, not to destroy my seed after me, nor to take away my name from the house of my father.

text.NRSV: Now Samuel died; and all Israel assembled and mourned for him. They buried him at his home in Ramah. Then David got up and went down to the wilderness of Paran.
text.Rotherham: And Samuel died, and all Israel were gathered together, and made lamentation for him, and buried him within his own house, in Ramah,—and David arose and went down into the wilderness of Maon.
text.KJV: And Samuel died; and all the Israelites were gathered together, and lamented him, and buried him in his house at Ramah. And David arose, and went down to the wilderness of Paran.
text.NABRE: Samuel died, and all Israel gathered to mourn him; they buried him at his home in Ramah. Then David went down to the wilderness of Paran.
text.DRB: And Samuel died, and all Israel was gathered together, and they mourned for him, and buried him in his house in Ramatha. And David rising went down into the desert of Pharan.

text.NRSV: There was a man in Maon, whose property was in Carmel. The man was very rich; he had three thousand sheep and a thousand goats. He was shearing his sheep in Carmel.
text.Rotherham: Now there was, a man, in Maon, whose cattle were in Carmel, and, the man, was exceeding great, and, he, had three thousand sheep, and a thousand goats,—and so it was, that he was shearing his sheep in Carmel.
text.KJV: And there was a man in Maon, whose possessions were in Carmel; and the man was very great, and he had three thousand sheep, and a thousand goats: and he was shearing his sheep in Carmel.
text.NABRE: Nabal and Abigail. There was a man of Maon who had property in Carmel; he was very wealthy, owning three thousand sheep and a thousand goats. At the time, he was present for the shearing of his flock in Carmel.
text.DRB: And there was a certain man in the wilderness of Maon, & his possession in Carmel, & that man was exceeding great: and he had three thousand sheep, & a thousand goats: and it chanced that his flock was shorn in Carmel.

text.NRSV: Now the name of the man was Nabal, and the name of his wife Abigail. The woman was clever and beautiful, but the man was surly and mean; he was a Calebite.
text.Rotherham: Now, the name of the man, was Nabal, and, the name of his wife, Abigail,—and, the woman, was of good understanding, and of beautiful figure,—but, the man, was unfeeling and of evil practices, and he was a Calebite.
text.KJV: Now the name of the man was Nabal; and the name of his wife Abigail: and she was a woman of good understanding, and of a beautiful countenance: but the man was churlish and evil in his doings; and he was of the house of Caleb.
text.NABRE: The man’s name was Nabal and his wife was Abigail. The woman was intelligent and attractive, but Nabal, a Calebite, was harsh and bad-mannered.
text.DRB: And the name of the man was Nabal: & the name of his wife Abigail. And that woman was very wise and beautiful: moreover her husband hard, and very ill, and malicious: and he was of the kindred of Caleb.

text.NRSV: David heard in the wilderness that Nabal was shearing his sheep.
text.Rotherham: So then David heard in the wilderness,—that Nabal was shearing his sheep.
text.KJV: And David heard in the wilderness that Nabal did shear his sheep.
text.NABRE: While in the wilderness, David heard that Nabal was shearing his flock,
text.DRB: When David therefore heard in the desert, that Nabal sheared his flock,

text.NRSV: So David sent ten young men; and David said to the young men, “Go up to Carmel, and go to Nabal, and greet him in my name.
text.Rotherham: David, therefore, sent ten young men,—and David said unto the young men,—Go up to Carmel, and enter in unto Nabal, and ye shall ask him, in my name, of his welfare;
text.KJV: And David sent out ten young men, and David said unto the young men, Get you up to Carmel, and go to Nabal, and greet him in my name:
text.NABRE: so he sent ten young men, instructing them: “Go up to Carmel. Pay Nabal a visit and greet him in my name.
text.DRB: he sent ten young men, and said to them: Go up into Carmel, & you shall come to Nabal, and shall salute him in my name peaceably.

text.NRSV: Thus you shall salute him, ‘Peace be to you, and peace be to your house, and peace be to all that you have.
text.Rotherham: and say thus—Long life to thee! Mayest, thou, prosper, And, thy household, prosper, And, all that thou hast, prosper!
text.KJV: And thus shall ye say to him that liveth in prosperity, Peace be both to thee, and peace be to thine house, and peace be unto all that thou hast.
text.NABRE: Say to him, ‘Peace be with you, my brother, and with your family, and with all who belong to you.
text.DRB: And you shall say: Peace be to my brethren, and to thee, and peace to thy house, and to all whatsoever thou hast be peace.

text.NRSV: I hear that you have shearers; now your shepherds have been with us, and we did them no harm, and they missed nothing, all the time they were in Carmel.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, have I heard that thou hast shearers,—Well, the shepherds that thou hast, have been with us, we reproached them not, neither missed they, anything, all the days they were in Carmel.
text.KJV: And now I have heard that thou hast shearers: now thy shepherds which were with us, we hurt them not, neither was there ought missing unto them, all the while they were in Carmel.
text.NABRE: I have just heard that shearers are with you. Now, when your shepherds were with us, we did them no injury, neither did they miss anything while they were in Carmel.
text.DRB: I have heard, that thy shepherds which were with us in the desert did shear: we have never molested them, neither hath ought been wanting to them at any time of the flock, all the time that they were with us in Carmel.

text.NRSV: Ask your young men, and they will tell you. Therefore let the young men find favor in your sight; for we come on a feast day. Please give whatever you have at hand to your servants and to your son David.’”
text.Rotherham: Ask thy young men, that they may tell thee, that so my young men may find favour in thine eyes, for, on a good day, have we come! Give, I pray thee, what thy hand findeth, to thy servants, and to thy son, to David!
text.KJV: Ask thy young men, and they will shew thee. Wherefore let the young men find favour in thine eyes: for we come in a good day: give, I pray thee, whatsoever cometh to thine hand unto thy servants, and to thy son David.
text.NABRE: Ask your servants and they will tell you. Look kindly on these young men, since we come at a festival time. Please give your servants and your son David whatever you can.’”
text.DRB: Ask thy servants, and they will tell thee. Now therefore let thy servants find grace in thine eyes: for we are come in a good day, whatsoever thy hand shall find, give thy servants, and thy son David.

text.NRSV: When David’s young men came, they said all this to Nabal in the name of David; and then they waited.
text.Rotherham: So David’s young men came, and spake unto Nabal, according to all these words, in the name of David,—and waited.
text.KJV: And when David’s young men came, they spake to Nabal according to all those words in the name of David, and ceased.
text.NABRE: When David’s young men arrived, they delivered the entire message to Nabal in David’s name, and then waited.
text.DRB: And when David's servants were come, they spake to Nabal all these words in David's name: and so held their peace.

text.NRSV: But Nabal answered David’s servants, “Who is David? Who is the son of Jesse? There are many servants today who are breaking away from their masters.
text.Rotherham: Then Nabal answered the servants of David, and said, Who is David? And who is the son of Jesse? Nowadays, many are the servants that have broken away, every one from the presence of his lord:
text.KJV: And Nabal answered David’s servants, and said, Who is David? and who is the son of Jesse? there be many servants now a days that break away every man from his master.
text.NABRE: But Nabal answered the servants of David: “Who is David? Who is the son of Jesse? Nowadays there are many servants who run away from their masters.
text.DRB: But Nabal answering the servants of David, said: Who is David? And what is the son of Isai? There are servants multiplied now a days which fly from their masters.

text.NRSV: Shall I take my bread and my water and the meat that I have butchered for my shearers, and give it to men who come from I do not know where?”
text.Rotherham: Shall I, then, take my bread, and my wine, and my slain beasts, that I have slaughtered for my shearers,—and give unto men of whom I know not whence they are?
text.KJV: Shall I then take my bread, and my water, and my flesh that I have killed for my shearers, and give it unto men, whom I know not whence they be?
text.NABRE: Must I take my bread, my wine, my meat that I have slaughtered for my own shearers, and give them to men who come from who knows where?”
text.DRB: Shall I then take my breads, and my waters, and the flesh of my cattle, which I have killed for my shearers, and give to men whom I know not whence they are?

text.NRSV: So David’s young men turned away, and came back, and told him all this.
text.Rotherham: So the young men of David turned away for their journey,—and came back, and drew near, and told him, according to all these words.
text.KJV: So David’s young men turned their way, and went again, and came and told him all those sayings.
text.NABRE: So David’s young men retraced their steps and on their return reported to him all that had been said.
text.DRB: Therefore the servants of David returned by their way, and returning came & told him all the words that he had said.

text.NRSV: David said to his men, “Every man strap on his sword!” And every one of them strapped on his sword; David also strapped on his sword; and about four hundred men went up after David, while two hundred remained with the baggage.
text.Rotherham: Then said David unto his men—Gird on you every man his sword. And they girded on them, every man his sword, and, David also, girded on his sword,—and there went up after David—about four hundred men, and, two hundred, abode by the stores.
text.KJV: And David said unto his men, Gird ye on every man his sword. And they girded on every man his sword; and David also girded on his sword: and there went up after David about four hundred men; and two hundred abode by the stuff.
text.NABRE: Thereupon David said to his men, “Let everyone strap on his sword.” And everyone did so, and David put on his own sword. About four hundred men went up after David, while two hundred remained with the baggage.
text.DRB: Then said David to his servants: Every man gird him with his sword. And they were every one girded with their swords. And David also was girded with his sword: and there followed David about four hundred men: moreover two hundred remained at the baggage.

text.NRSV: But one of the young men told Abigail, Nabal’s wife, “David sent messengers out of the wilderness to salute our master; and he shouted insults at them.
text.Rotherham: But a certain one of her young men told, Abigail, Nabal’s wife, saying,—Lo! David sent messengers out of the wilderness to bless our lord, and he treated them with contempt.
text.KJV: But one of the young men told Abigail, Nabal’s wife, saying, Behold, David sent messengers out of the wilderness to salute our master; and he railed on them.
text.NABRE: Abigail, Nabal’s wife, was informed of this by one of the servants, who said: “From the wilderness David sent messengers to greet our master, but he screamed at them.
text.DRB: But to Abigail the wife of Nabal one of their servants told, saying: Behold David hath sent messengers from the desert, to bless our master, and he disdained them:

text.NRSV: Yet the men were very good to us, and we suffered no harm, and we never missed anything when we were in the fields as long as we were with them;
text.Rotherham: But, the men, were exceeding good to us,—and we were not harmed, neither missed we, anything, all the days we went to and fro with them, while we were in the fields:
text.KJV: But the men were very good unto us, and we were not hurt, neither missed we any thing, as long as we were conversant with them, when we were in the fields:
text.NABRE: Yet these men were very good to us. We were not harmed, neither did we miss anything all the while we were living among them during our stay in the open country.
text.DRB: these men were good enough to us, & not troublesome: neither did ever any thing perish all the time, that we have converst with them in the desert:

text.NRSV: they were a wall to us both by night and by day, all the while we were with them keeping the sheep.
text.Rotherham: A wall, became they unto us, both by night and by day, all the days we were with them, tending the flock.
text.KJV: They were a wall unto us both by night and day, all the while we were with them keeping the sheep.
text.NABRE: Day and night they were a wall of protection for us, the whole time we were pasturing the sheep near them.
text.DRB: they were in stead of a wall to us both in the day and in the night, all the days that we fed the flocks with them.

text.NRSV: Now therefore know this and consider what you should do; for evil has been decided against our master and against all his house; he is so ill-natured that no one can speak to him.”
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, know thou, and see, what thou canst do, for mischief is determined against our lord, and against all his household,—but, he, is such an abandoned man, that one cannot speak unto him.
text.KJV: Now therefore know and consider what thou wilt do; for evil is determined against our master, and against all his household: for he is such a son of Belial, that a man cannot speak to him.
text.NABRE: Now, see what you can do, for you must realize that otherwise disaster is in store for our master and for his whole house. He is such a scoundrel that no one can talk to him.”
text.DRB: Wherefore consider, and think what thou hast to do, for malice is accomplished against thy husband, & against thy house, and he is the son of Belial, so that no man can speak to him.

text.NRSV: Then Abigail hurried and took two hundred loaves, two skins of wine, five sheep ready dressed, five measures of parched grain, one hundred clusters of raisins, and two hundred cakes of figs. She loaded them on donkeys
text.Rotherham: Then Abigail hastened—and took two hundred loaves, and two skins of wine, and five sheep made ready, and five measures of parched corn, and a hundred cakes of raisins, and two hundred cakes of figs,—and put them on the asses.
